6180: Data block at 6180
List of every 3D object: 19 (jp opcode byte, blueprint address, class byte) triples. The jp opcode byte ($C3) is a leftover of an older calling convention and is never executed, only the address and class byte are read. The class byte groups objects into five tiers ($00-$04: stations/missiles, small drops, common ships, top-tier ships, escorts), used elsewhere to decide which ships are eligible to spawn as random traffic or pirates at a given point in the game, separate from anything in the object's own header.
AdrDataShips 6180 DEFB $C3 jp opcode (unused)

61CC: Data block at 61CC
Every 3D object (ships, station, missiles, cargo canisters, escape pods, asteroids) begins with this same 23-byte header. Compared to the BBC Micro original's 20-byte header, the concepts largely line up (vertex/edge/face counts, bounty, a visibility-distance-for-dot-rendering field, launchable-craft bits), but this header is 3 bytes longer - it carries an explicit, separate combat-rating-score field (see rating points below) in addition to the monetary bounty, where the BBC version has only the one bounty value doing both jobs. Each table offset is also stored as a full 16-bit byte offset (from the object's own base address) rather than the BBC's packed single-byte-plus-shared-high-byte scheme - there's no equivalent here of the BBC trick where one ship's blueprint references another ship's geometry tables directly; every object has its own independent vertex/edge/normal tables immediately following its header (the reused-geometry blueprints in this table, e.g. d_Asteroid1 and d_Cobra1, instead duplicate the header and just point their offsets at another object's tables).
d_ASP 61CC DEFB $00 cargo hold

61DC DEFW $00F9 offset to normal data
61DE DEFB $8C flags
61DF DEFB $01
61E0 DEFB $08
61E1 DEFW $0340 rating points
Vertex format, 6 bytes each: +0 |X| magnitude, +1 |Y| magnitude, +2 |Z| magnitude, +3 bits 7-5 sign of X/Y/Z and bits 4-0 a distance-cull threshold ($1F = always compute), +4 low nibble face 1 index/high nibble face 2 index, +5 low nibble face 3 index/high nibble face 4 index - so each vertex can reference up to four different faces; E338 tests the low nibble first, falling back to the high nibble only if the low nibble's face turns out to be invisible.

6253 DEFB $04,$04
Edge format, 4 bytes each: +0 distance-cull threshold (same $1F bypass convention as vertices), +1 start vertex index, +2 end vertex index, +3 low nibble face 1 index/high nibble face 2 index.

Normal (face) format, 4 bytes each: +0 |X| magnitude, +1 |Y| magnitude, +2 |Z| magnitude, +3 bits 7-5 sign of X/Y/Z and bits 4-0 a distance-cull threshold - same magnitude/sign/cull-threshold shape as a vertex, used for backface culling in E923/E338.

68BE DEFB $0F,$16,$09,$FF,$FF,$FF,$0F,$26 vertices (4 * 6 bytes: X,Y,Z coords)
68C6 DEFB $09,$BF,$FF,$FF,$13,$20,$0B,$14
68CE DEFB $FF,$FF,$0A,$2E,$06,$54,$FF,$FF
The Alloys canister is the only object with zero normals (field_5=0, and there is no normals table after the edges) - a flat, four-vertex/four-edge shape with backface culling switched off entirely: E923 treats a zero normal count as "every face always visible" and skips the culling test for it, consistent with it being a small tumbling piece of debris rather than a solid hull, where culling wouldn't meaningfully change its appearance.

WhatOnScreen 7041 DEFB $00 What is shown on screen =0..3 in flight, view-screen number (front, rear, left, right) =$04 buy-goods menu =$05 sell-goods menu =$06 buy-equipment menu =$07 global map =$08 local map =$09 planet info not yet shown =$0A planet prices =$0B pilot info =$0C Inventory =$20 planet info
HighAngleLR 7042 DEFB $00 high byte of the rotation angle 7,n sign (left/right) $00..$0F
HighAngleDU 7043 DEFB $00 high byte of the rotation angle 7,n sign (up/down) $00..$0F
HighDeltaSpeed 7044 DEFB $0A,$00 high byte of speed $00..$1F
ShieldFront 7046 DEFB $00,$00 front shield +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
ShieldRear 7048 DEFB $00,$00 rear shield +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
Fuel 704A DEFB $A0,$00 fuel +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
Temperature 704C DEFB $00,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
TempLaser 704E DEFB $00,$00 laser temperature +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen >$90 we don't fire
Altitude 7050 DEFB $A0,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
Speed 7052 DEFB $00,$FF +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
ShieldUnit1 7054 DEFB $00,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
ShieldUnit2 7056 DEFB $00,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
ShieldUnit3 7058 DEFB $A0,$00 energy level +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
ShieldUnit4 705A DEFB $A0,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
RotateLR 705C DEFB $00,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
RotateDU 705E DEFB $00,$00 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
Missile 7060 DEFB $00
InFreeSpace 7061 DEFB $00 <>0 between systems (F interception)
IndDanger 7062 DEFB $00 <>0 in flight (also the danger indicator color) =0 docked =$04=%00 000 100 leaving the station, in flight (green) =$63=%01 100 011 4th energy unit not full (flashing red)) =$02=%00 000 010 one of the front/rear shields empty (red) =$06=%00 000 110 something nearby (yellow)
RndWord 7063 DEFW $4A37 random number
SunCrd 7065 DEFB $00 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
SunCrdX_med 7066 DEFB $00
SunCrdX_hi 7067 DEFB $00 crd high X sun
SunCrdY_low 7068 DEFB $00
SunCrdY_med 7069 DEFB $80
SunCrdY_hi 706A DEFB $01 crd high Y sun
SunCrdZ_low 706B DEFB $00
SunCrdZ_med 706C DEFB $10
SunCrdZ_hi 706D DEFB $09 crd high Z sun
PlanetCrd 706E DEFB $00 Planet coordinates in space
PlanetCrdX_med 706F DEFB $00
PlanetCrdX_hi 7070 DEFB $00 crd high x planet
PlanetCrdY_low 7071 DEFB $00
PlanetCrdY_med 7072 DEFB $00
PlanetCrdY_hi 7073 DEFB $00 crd high y planet
PlanetCrdZ_low 7074 DEFB $00
PlanetCrdZ_med 7075 DEFB $80 crd med z planet
PlanetCrdZ_hi 7076 DEFB $14 crd high z planet
CoriolisNear 7077 DEFB $00 <>0 near the station, docking is possible
J_Drive_Coriolis 7078 DEFB $00 =0 J-drive unavailable
FuelScoopsOn 7079 DEFB $00 <>0 near the sun, fuel scoops working
PlayerGameOver 707A DEFB $00 <>0 energy units empty, player destroyed
TypeOfPrint 707B DEFB $00 adjust the print routine depending on distance to the sun =$00 or c <>$00 xor c
BlockControl 707C DEFB $00 =0 after leaving the station =1 when docking
nBeepFX 707D DEFB $00 which sound to play =$00 no sound (silent) 0,=1 energy bomb exploded 1,=1 missile launched 2,=1 colliding with an object/object destroyed 3,=1 laser fired 4,=1 laser fired 5,=1 ECM working 6,=1 hit by laser on the player/docking damage 7,=1
AutoDocking 707E DEFB $00 <>0(=$01) docking enabled

7398: CopyBuf2Screen
Used by the routines at 716B, 7287, 79A3 and 7AF8.
Copy buffer to screen
Copies only the two screen thirds that make up the 3D viewport, deliberately leaving the third third (where the cockpit panel lives) untouched between frames.
7398 LD DE,$4000
739B LD HL,$C000
739E CALL $73BD fast interlaced transfer of the screen buffer to 1/3 of the screen
73A1 LD DE,$4800
73A4 LD HL,$C800
73A7 CALL $73BD fast interlaced transfer of the screen buffer to 1/3 of the screen
73AA LD DE,$4100
73AD LD HL,$C100
73B0 CALL $73BD fast interlaced transfer of the screen buffer to 1/3 of the screen
73B3 LD DE,$4900
73B6 LD HL,$C900
73B9 CALL $73BD fast interlaced transfer of the screen buffer to 1/3 of the screen
73BC RET

745F: ClearScrBuffer
Used by the routines at 716B, 7287, 79A3 and 7AF8.
Fast clear of the screen buffer
This is not a plain clear: the repeating byte pattern it writes already contains the 3D viewport border (a full horizontal line at the top and bottom, and a single vertical pixel down each edge column for every row in between), so the border is regenerated as a side effect of "clearing" every single frame, with no separate border-preserving or redrawing code needed.
745F DI

7464 LD SP,$D000
7467 LD HL,$0000
746A LD DE,$00FF

CircleCrdZhigh 74BC DEFB $00
SunRadiusNova 74BD DEFB $00 sun radius during the supernova explosion (mission 1)
74BE: PrnSunPlanet
Used by the routines at 7287 and 7AF8.
Print the sun and planet
74BE XOR A
74BF LD ($707B),A set TypeOfPrint
74C2 LD HL,$7065 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
74C5 LD DE,$74B4 distance to the sun/planet along the X axis, high byte 7,=1 negative value
74C8 LD BC,$0009
74CB LDIR
74CD CALL $751F calculate on-screen coordinates of the sun/planet projection
74D0 PUSH AF
74D1 CALL $74FA Correct data if mission 1 has been given
74D4 POP AF
74D5 AND A
74D6 JR Z,$74E7
74D8 CALL $7CF0 print a circle on screen/in the buffer
74DB LD A,($7C3D) get CircleRadius
74DE CP $28 '('
74E0 JR C,$74E7
74E2 LD A,$01
74E4 LD ($707B),A set TypeOfPrint
74E7 LD HL,$706E planet coordinates in space
74EA LD DE,$74B4 distance to the sun/planet along the X axis, high byte 7,=1 negative value
74ED LD BC,$0009
74F0 LDIR
74F2 CALL $751F calculate on-screen coordinates of the sun/planet projection
74F5 AND A
74F6 RET Z
74F7 JP PrnCircle_NoFill

76D6: CalcPrnCrdSunAxis
Calculate one on-screen coordinate of the planet/sun projection
In: c' =3 for X/Z coordinates
=2 for Y/Z coordinates
hl.c - X/Y coordinate (for front/rear)
de.b - Z coordinate (for front/rear)
Out: 2,b' =1 coordinate crdX =< crdZ
3,b' =1 coordinate crdY =< crdZ
d - on-screen coordinate of the planet/sun projection
76D6 LD H,L Out: d - delta
76D7 LD L,C
76D8 LD D,E
76D9 LD E,B
76DA JR $7700

778F: CalcCrdSunPlanet
Used by the routines at 7287 and 7AF8.
Update sun/planet coordinates as the player moves
778F LD IX,$7065 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
7793 CALL $DED1 update object coordinates from player movement
7796 LD IX,$706E planet coordinates in space
779A JP $DED1 update object coordinates from player movement
779D: CalcCrdCoriolis
Used by the routine at 7287.
Handle Coriolis station movement
779D LD A,($7078) get J_Drive_Coriolis
77A0 AND A
77A1 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
77A5 JR NZ,$77B0 update Coriolis coordinates from player movement
77A7 LD A,(IX+$22)
77AA AND A
77AB RET NZ
77AC DEC A
77AD LD ($7078),A =$FF J-drive usable
77B0 CALL $DCE0 update Coriolis coordinates from player movement
77B3 LD A,(IX+$02)
77B6 AND $7F
77B8 RET NZ
77B9 LD A,(IX+$05)
77BC AND $7F
77BE RET NZ
77BF LD A,(IX+$08)
77C2 AND $7F
77C4 RET NZ
77C5 BIT 7,(IX+$26)
77C9 RET NZ
77CA XOR A station is close, disable the J-drive
77CB LD ($7078),A set J_Drive_Coriolis
77CE LD A,$F0
77D0 LD (IX+$22),A
77D3 RET
77D4: Data block at 77D4
77D4 DEFB $01
77D5: Use_Y_Cloacking
Used by the routine at 7287.
Use of the Cloaking Device
77D5 LD HL,$77D4
77D8 DEC (HL)
77D9 LD HL,$FC0E 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device 6,=1 - 0 -- ??? 5,=1 - I -- Global Map 4,=1 - O -- Local Map 3,=1 - P -- Planet Info 2,=1 - K -- Planet Price 1,=1 - L -- Commander Info 0,=1 - Enter -- Inventory
77DC RES 7,(HL)
77DE RET NZ
77DF LD A,$02
77E1 LD ($77D4),A
77E4 HALT
77E5 BIT 7,(HL)
77E7 RET Z if the key is still pressed, continue
77E8 LD HL,$A872 +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed
77EB BIT 6,(HL)
77ED RET Z
77EE LD HL,$A80C =1 Cloaking Device on =1 energy drain while in use
77F1 DEC (HL)
77F2 JR Z,$780A paper 0, ink 7
77F4 LD A,($7058) check energy
77F7 CP $28 '('
77F9 RET C
77FA LD A,$01 energy is low
77FC LD (HL),A
77FD LD HL,$6069 2,=1 Coriolis is hostile
7800 RES 2,(HL)
7802 XOR A
7803 LD ($DA14),A set PlayerPirate
7806 LD L,$0F paper 1, ink 7
7808 JR $780C
780A LD L,$07 paper 0, ink 7
780C JP $9287 print view-screen attributes
780F LD A,($A843) get PilotMission1
7812 AND A
This entry point is used by the routine at 781F.
7813 LD L,$02 paper 0, ink 2 of the view screen
7815 JR NZ,$780C
7817 RET
7818 LD A,($A80C) get CloakingDeviceOn
781B AND A
781C JR NZ,$7806 paper 1, ink 7
781E RET
781F: SetViewScrAtr
Used by the routine at 7AF8.
Set view-screen attributes accounting for mission 1 being given and turning on
Cloaking Device
781F LD A,($A843) get PilotMission1
7822 AND A
7823 RET Z
7824 LD A,$01
7826 LD ($A87C),A set PilotMission1_1
7829 JR $7813 paper 0, ink 2 of the view screen
782B: GoMission3
Used by the routine at 7AF8.
Give/complete mission 3, if available
782B LD A,($A871) get PilotMission3
782E DEC A
782F JR Z,$7832
7831 DEC A
7832 JP Z,$D219 offer of mission 3
7835 SUB $03
7837 JP Z,$D243 successful completion of mission 3
783A INC A
783B LD A,$D4
783D JR Z,$7848
783F RET
7840: GoPirates
Used by the routine at 7AF8.
Ship capture by pirates, if needed
7840 LD HL,$6065
7843 BIT 7,(HL)
7845 RET NZ
7846 LD A,$0D
This entry point is used by the routine at 782B.
7848 CALL $D1EC Print the message "ship captured by pirates"
784B XOR A
784C LD ($7041),A set WhatOnScreen
784F CALL $741C Print the frame and panel if needed
7852 LD SP,($74B2)
7856 JP $718F NewGame

7ADD: Data block at 7ADD
NumOfCircle 7ADD DEFB $08 number of concentric circles
CounterCircle 7ADE DEFB $00

7AE8 DEFB $32,$35,$37,$3A,$3C,$3F,$41,$44
7AF0 DEFB $10,$10,$10,$18,$20,$28,$30,$38

Docking/Takeoff

7AFB AND A
7AFC RET NZ
7AFD LD HL,$7AF0
7B00 LD ($7A07),HL
7B03 LD A,$08
7B05 LD ($7ADD),A set NumOfCircle
7B08 LD A,$30 '0'
7B0A LD ($7A33),A
7B0D LD A,$40 '@'
7B0F LD ($7ADF),A
7B12 LD HL,$0101
7B15 LD ($A80A),HL set JumpDocking
7B18 LD A,($7062) get IndDanger
7B1B AND A
7B1C JR Z,$7B68 leaving the planet
7B1E LD A,$38 '8'
7B20 LD ($7A38),A jr c,7A4E for jump jr c,7A71 for docking jr c,7A6b for takeoff
7B23 LD A,$0C
7B25 LD ($7044),A set HighDeltaSpeed

7B39 RES 7,(HL)
7B3B CALL $745F Fast clear of the screen buffer
7B3E CALL $7999 visualize docking/takeoff (2x1 / 2x0.75 rectangle)

7B88 CALL $781F set view-screen attributes accounting for mission 1 being given and the Cloaking Device being on

7B8E LD B,$07

7BFF: InitSpeedAngle
Used by the routines at 716B, 79A3, 7AF8 and D1EC.
Initialize ship movement parameters
In: ----
Out: b=$00
a=$00
hl - ????
7BFF LD B,$05
7C01 LD HL,$FF08 zero out the rotation angles
7C04 CALL $7C0E Zero out a memory area
7C07 LD (HL),$0C set speed
7C09: ClearKeyBuffer
Used by the routines at 741C and 8F02.
Clear the keyboard buffer
7C09 LD B,$05
7C0B LD HL,$FC0E 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device
7C0E: ClearMemory
Used by the routines at 7AF8 and 7BFF.
Zero out a memory area
hl - address
b - length
7C0E XOR A
7C0F LD (HL),A
7C10 INC HL
7C11 DJNZ $7C0F
7C13 RET
7C14: ClearAroundObj
Used by the routines at 7287, 79A3, 7AF8 and 9966.
Zero out the list of nearby objects
In: ---
Out: b =$00
a =$00
de =$LenSlotObj
ix - undefined
7C14 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
7C18 RES 2,(IX+$21) station is no longer hostile
7C1C XOR A
7C1D LD ($DA14),A set PlayerPirate
7C20 LD DE,$0027 '''
7C23 LD B,$07
7C25 ADD IX,DE
7C27 LD (IX+$22),A
7C2A DJNZ $7C25
7C2C RET
7C2D: RestorePilotFromMemory
Used by the routines at A976 and D3FF.
Restore pilot data from memory
In: ---
Out: bc =$0000
hl,de - undefined
7C2D LD HL,$A910 for keeping the last loaded game in memory
7C30 LD DE,$A816 1..11 pilot name
7C33 LD BC,$0066 'f' ; 102 bytes
7C36 LDIR
7C38 RET
7C39: Data block at 7C39
7C39 DEFB $00 =$01 G or H pressed (jump)
CircleCrdY 7C3A DEFB $00 distance from the circle center to the screen center along Y
CircleCrdX 7C3B DEFB $00 distance from the circle center to the screen center along X
Circle drawing flags:
bit 0: 0 = X offset negative, 1 = X offset positive
bit 1: 0 = Y offset negative, 1 = Y offset positive
bit 2: whether the X extent needs clipping against the viewport edge
bit 3: whether the Y extent needs clipping against the viewport edge
CircleFlg 7C3C DEFB $0F circle flags
CircleRadius 7C3D DEFB $64 circle radius
CircleCrdY_temp 7C3E DEFB $00 temporary: distance to the screen center along Y
CircleCrdX_temp 7C3F DEFB $00 temporary: distance to the screen center along X
CircleCrdY_prn 7C40 DEFB $00 print Y coordinate of the circle center
CircleCrdX_prn 7C41 DEFB $00 print X coordinate of the circle center
CircleDeltaY 7C42 DEFB $00 point where the circle intersects the left/right screen edge, relative to the coordinate center
SaveSPcircle 7C43 DEFW $0000
SaveHL3 7C45 DEFW $0000
SaveA7 7C47 DEFB $00 byte to print
DirPrnCircle 7C48 DEFB $00 =0 print upward =1 print downward
FillCircle 7C49 DEFB $00 =1 draw an unfilled circle =0 filled
7C4A DEFB $20 number of bytes to modify in the circle-printing routine
7C4B: half_rClip_Down
Used by the routines at 7E14, 7E60, 7EAE, 7ED8 and 85F4.
Print a half-circle by OR (circle extends past the right X boundary).
The midpoint/Bresenham error term and step deltas are kept in the alternate register set (hl', bc', e', reached via exx), freeing the primary registers for screen addressing.
In: b - left byte to print
c - right byte to print
hl - address to print the right byte
hl - address to print the left byte
e' =0
c' =0 minus radius
Out: hl,de,bc,hl',de',bc' - ????
7C4B PUSH AF
7C4C EXX
7C4D LD HL,$0000
7C50 LD D,H
7C51 LD B,$FF
7C53 EXX
7C54 LD A,$01
7C56 LD ($7C48),A set DirPrnCircle
7C59 LD A,($7C49) get FillCircle
7C5C AND A
7C5D JP NZ,$869C print an unfilled half-circle by OR (circle extends past the right X boundary)
7C60 JP $8331 print a filled half-circle by OR (circle extends past the right X boundary)
7C63: half_rClip_Up
Used by the routines at 7E14, 7E60, 7EAE, 7ED8 and 85F4.

7C74 JP NZ,$869C print an unfilled half-circle by OR (circle extends past the right X boundary)
7C77 JP $8331 print a filled half-circle by OR (circle extends past the right X boundary)

c - right byte to print

hl - address to print the left byte
e' =0
c' =0 minus radius

7CAF LD B,$FF
7CB1 EXX

7CB4 LD ($7C48),A set DirPrnCircle
7CB7 LD A,($7C49) get FillCircle

7CBB JP NZ,$8740 print an unfilled half-circle by OR (circle stays within X boundaries)
7CBE JP $8117 print a filled half-circle by OR (circle stays within X boundaries)
7CC1: half_NoClip_Up

7CD8: Fill_Up
Used by the routines at 7E14 and 7E60.
Fill/dry-run the view screen upward from a given Y coordinate
In: b - window height in pixels
a - Y coordinate in pixels
Out: hl - address of the window's top border
7CD8 LD C,A
7CD9 LD A,($7C49) get FillCircle
7CDC AND A
7CDD LD A,C
7CDE JP NZ,$8640 dry-run fill of the view screen upward from a given Y coordinate
7CE1 JP $8057 fill the view screen upward from a given Y coordinate with byte $FF
7CE4: Fill_Down
Used by the routines at 7E14 and 7E60.
Fill/dry-run the view screen downward from a given Y coordinate
In: b - window height in pixels
a - Y coordinate in pixels
Out: hl - address of the window's top border
7CE4 LD C,A
7CE5 LD A,($7C49) get FillCircle
7CE8 AND A
7CE9 LD A,C
7CEA JP NZ,$866E dry-run fill of the view screen downward from a given Y coordinate
7CED JP $80A2 fill the view screen downward from a given Y coordinate with byte $FF
7CF0: PrnCircle_Fill
Used by the routines at 74BE and 8545.
Print a circle on screen/in the buffer
In: (CircleFlg) - flags
(CircleRadius) - radius
(CircleCrdY) - center coordinates
(FillCircle) =0 draw a filled circle
=1 draw an unfilled circle
Out: circle printed
Circles smaller than radius 9 are rejected outright (presumably drawn elsewhere as a fixed dot sprite).
Every circle is plotted with a plain OR into screen memory; there is no XOR/blend-mode switch.
7CF0 LD A,($7C3D) get CircleRadius
7CF3 CP $09
7CF5 RET C
7CF6 DI
7CF7 XOR A
7CF8 LD ($7C49),A set FillCircle
7CFB JP $7D09
This entry point is used by the routines at 74BE and 8461.
PrnCircle_NoFill 7CFE LD A,($7C3D) get CircleRadius
7D01 CP $09
7D03 RET C
This entry point is used by the routine at 79A3.
PrnCircle_NoFill_NoRadius 7D04 LD A,$01
7D06 LD ($7C49),A set FillCircle
7D09 CALL $7D0E print a circle on screen/in the buffer
7D0C EI
7D0D RET
7D0E: PrnCircle_Clip
Used by the routine at 7CF0.
Print a circle on screen/in the buffer
In: (CircleFlg) - flags
(CircleRadius) - radius
(CircleCrdY) - center coordinates
(FillCircle) =0 draw a filled circle
=1 draw an unfilled circle
Out: circle printed
7D0E LD BC,($7C3A) get CircleCrdY

Determine left byte data and print address

hl - print address
e - low byte of the left-byte print address

7FF7 CALL $22B0 hl - address

8001 LD A,B
8002 JP NZ,$861F continuation of CalcLeftAddrCircle

8008 SRL B
800A DEC A
800B JR NZ,$8008
800D LD E,L
800E RET

8035 RET

8117: half_NoClip_Fill
Used by the routines at 7CA9 and 7CC1.
Print a filled half-circle by OR (circle stays within X boundaries)
In: b - left byte to print
c - right byte to print
hl - address to print the right byte
hl - address to print the left byte
hl' =0
de' =0
b' =FF
c' =0 minus radius
(DirectionPrnCircle) =0 print upward / =1 print downward
Out: hl,de,bc,hl',de',bc',a - ????
8117 LD A,E
8118 SUB L
8119 JR NC,$812D
811B LD A,($7C48) get DirPrnCircle
811E AND A
811F JR NZ,$8128
8121 LD A,$BF
8123 CP H
8124 JR C,$812F
8126 JR $812D
8128 LD A,$CF
812A CP H
812B JR NC,$812F
812D POP AF
812E RET
812F LD ($7C43),SP
8133 LD A,(HL) fill the line with $FF
8134 OR C
8135 LD (HL),A
8136 LD D,L
8137 LD L,E
8138 LD A,(HL)
8139 OR B
813A LD (HL),A
813B LD A,D
813C SUB E
813D DEC A
813E SRL A
8140 JR NC,$8145
8142 INC L
8143 LD (HL),$FF
8145 LD L,D
8146 SUB $11
8148 CPL
8149 LD ($8152),A
814C LD SP,$85F2 fill texture
814F POP AF
8150 LD SP,HL
Self-modifying PUSH AF cascade: the byte count for this scanline's span is turned into an offset and patched into the operand of a JR instruction just above, so it jumps into the middle of a run of 16 back-to-back PUSH AF instructions; SP is temporarily repointed at the destination screen address so each PUSH AF writes 2 bytes straight into screen memory instead of a real stack.
8151 JR $8151
8153 PUSH AF
8154 PUSH AF
8155 PUSH AF
8156 PUSH AF
8157 PUSH AF
8158 PUSH AF
8159 PUSH AF
815A PUSH AF
815B PUSH AF
815C PUSH AF
815D PUSH AF
815E PUSH AF
815F PUSH AF
8160 PUSH AF
8161 PUSH AF
8162 PUSH AF
8163 LD A,($7C48) get DirPrnCircle
8166 AND A
8167 LD SP,($7C43)
816B JR NZ,$8176
816D LD A,H

8461: PrintCircleMap
Used by the routines at A9CA, AB4E and B27B.
Draw on the galaxy map a circle of the flight radius reachable with available fuel
In: (PrnCrdXCursor) - circle center coordinates
(RadiusCircleMap) - circle radius
Out: a =$00
hl,de,bc - undefined
After drawing the map marker, it immediately re-runs the same 32-byte patch loop using Data4CircleEngine instead of Data4CircleMap, repointing the shared circle engine at the 3D flight-view viewport constants before returning, so the very next PrnCircle_Fill call (for the sun/planet disc) draws correctly sized/positioned for that screen.
8461 LD B,$0C
8463 LD A,($A813) get PrnCrdXCursor
8466 LD C,A
8467 SUB $80
8469 JR C,$846F
846B SET 0,B
846D JR $8472
846F LD A,$80
8471 SUB C
8472 LD ($7C3B),A set CircleCrdX
8475 LD A,($A814) get PrnCrdYCursor
8478 LD C,A
8479 SUB $60 '`'
847B JR NC,$8482
847D SET 1,B
847F LD A,$60 '`'
8481 SUB C
8482 LD ($7C3A),A set CircleCrdY
8485 LD A,B
8486 LD ($7C3C),A set CircleFlg
8489 LD A,($A815)
848C LD ($7C3D),A set CircleRadius
848F DI
8490 LD ($7C43),SP
8494 LD A,($7C4A) number of bytes to modify in the circle-printing routine
8497 LD HL,$84C5 modify the routine
849A LD SP,$8505 addresses in the circle-printing routine to modify
849D POP DE
849E LDI
84A0 DEC A
84A1 JR NZ,$849D
84A3 LD SP,($7C43)
84A7 EI
84A8 CALL PrnCircle_NoFill
84AB DI
84AC LD ($7C43),SP
84B0 LD A,($7C4A) number of bytes to modify in the circle-printing routine
84B3 LD HL,$84E5 revert the routine modification
84B6 LD SP,$8505 addresses in the circle-printing routine to modify
84B9 POP DE
84BA LDI
84BC DEC A
84BD JR NZ,$84B9
84BF LD SP,($7C43)
84C3 EI
84C4 RET
84C5: Data block at 84C5
Viewport clipping-boundary/dimension constants for the Short Range Chart map circle; patched into 32 immediate operands scattered through the shared circle-drawing engine by PrintCircleMap.
Data4CircleMap 84C5 DEFB $60,$60,$80,$80,$80,$80,$80,$7F
84CD DEFB $1F,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$80,$00
84D5 DEFB $FF,$00,$00,$28,$FF,$00,$00,$58
84DD DEFB $57,$3F,$3F,$57,$57,$3F,$58,$3F
Viewport clipping-boundary/dimension constants for the 3D flight-view engine screen; patched into the same 32 operand locations as Data4CircleMap, but for the sun/planet circle instead of the map marker.
Data4CircleEngine 84E5 DEFB $40,$40,$78,$78,$78,$88,$78,$77
84ED DEFB $1E,$01,$CB,$FC,$CB,$FC,$88,$01
84F5 DEFB $F7,$CB,$FC,$C0,$F7,$CB,$FC,$D0
84FD DEFB $CF,$BF,$BF,$CF,$CF,$BF,$D0,$BF
AdrProcCircle 8505 DEFW $7D1B addresses in the circle-printing routine to modify
8507 DEFW $7D34

850D DEFW $7D64
850F DEFW $7E1F

8640: NoFillBuffUp
Used by the routine at 7CD8.
Dry-run fill of the view screen upward from a given Y coordinate
In: b - window height in pixels
a - Y coordinate in pixels
Out: hl - address of the window's top border
8640 PUSH AF

8645 CALL $22B0
8648 SET 7,H
864A POP BC

88E1: PrintCompass
Used by the routine at 7287.
Calculate print parameters and draw the compass with pointer on screen
In: ---
Out: b=$00
hl,de,c,a - undefined
88E1 XOR A
88E2 LD ($88DC),A =1 J-drive unavailable (sun nearby)
88E5 LD IX,$7065 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
88E9 LD A,(IX+$02)
88EC AND $7F
88EE JR NZ,$892F
88F0 LD A,(IX+$05)
88F3 AND $7F
88F5 JR NZ,$892F
88F7 LD A,(IX+$08)
88FA AND $7F
88FC JR NZ,$892F
88FE CALL $8A5A calculate distance to an object from its coordinates (vector length)
8901 LD A,H
8902 OR L
8903 JR Z,$892F
8905 LD A,$01
8907 LD ($88DC),A =1 J-drive unavailable (sun nearby)
890A EX DE,HL
890B LD HL,$0000
890E SBC HL,DE
8910 EX DE,HL
8911 LD HL,$4000
8914 AND A
8915 SBC HL,DE
8917 JP C,$8A09
891A LD HL,$2000
891D XOR A
891E SBC HL,DE
8920 JR NC,$8924
8922 LD A,$01
8924 LD ($7079),A set FuelScoopsOn
8927 EX DE,HL
8928 CALL $8A0F calculate temperature/altitude from proximity to the sun/planet and set the danger-indicator color
892B LD A,H
892C LD ($704C),A set Temperature
892F XOR A
8930 LD ($88DD),A =1 J-drive unavailable (planet nearby)
8933 LD IX,$706E planet coordinates in space
8937 LD A,(IX+$02)
893A AND $7F
893C CP $02
893E JR NC,$89B2
8940 LD B,A
8941 LD A,(IX+$05)
8944 AND $7F
8946 CP $02
8948 JR NC,$89B2
894A OR B
894B LD B,A
894C LD A,(IX+$08)
894F AND $7F
8951 CP $02
8953 JR NC,$89B2
8955 OR B
8956 LD A,$01
8958 LD ($88DD),A =1 J-drive unavailable (planet nearby)
895B JR NZ,$897E
895D CALL $8A5A calculate distance to an object from its coordinates (vector length)
8960 LD A,H
8961 OR L
8962 JR Z,$897E
8964 EX DE,HL
8965 LD HL,$0000
8968 SBC HL,DE
896A EX DE,HL
896B LD HL,$4000
896E AND A
896F SBC HL,DE
8971 JP C,$8A09
8974 EX DE,HL
8975 CALL $8A0F calculate temperature/altitude from proximity to the sun/planet and set the danger-indicator color
8978 LD A,$A0
897A SUB H
897B LD ($7050),A set Altitude
897E LD A,$01
8980 LD ($7077),A set CoriolisNear
8983 LD A,($7078) get J_Drive_Coriolis
8986 INC A
8987 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
898B JR Z,$89B6
898D CALL $8A5A calculate distance to an object from its coordinates (vector length)
8990 LD A,H
8991 OR L
8992 JR NZ,$8995
8994 DEC HL
8995 EX DE,HL
8996 PUSH DE
8997 CALL $89A9 calculate the X coordinate for drawing the station icon on the compass
899A LD ($88DE),A 88DE 7,=1 left 7,=0 right 0-6 =X coordinate increment
899D INC IX
899F INC IX
89A1 INC IX
89A3 POP DE
89A4 CALL $89A9 calculate the X coordinate for drawing the station icon on the compass
89A7 JR $89D4
89A9: CompasCrdNnear
Used by the routine at 88E1.
Calculate the X coordinate for drawing the station icon on the compass
In: de - distance to station (vector length)
Out: 7,a - sign
0-6,a - X coordinate increment
hl,de,bc - undefined
89A9 XOR A
89AA LD L,(IX+$00)
89AD LD H,(IX+$01)
89B0 JR $89F3
89B2: PrintCompass2
Used by the routine at 88E1.
89B2 XOR A
89B3 LD ($7077),A set CoriolisNear
This entry point is used by the routine at 88E1.
89B6 INC IX
89B8 CALL $8A4D calculate distance (vector length) to an object from its coordinates, for far distances

89BF DEC HL

89C3 PUSH DE

89CC INC IX
89CE INC IX
89D0 POP DE
89D1 CALL $89E8 calculate the X coordinate for drawing the planet icon on the compass
This entry point is used by the routine at 88E1.
89D4 LD ($88DF),A 88DF 7,=1 DownScrAdr 7,=0 UpScrAdr 0-6 =Y coordinate increment

89DC LD A,$00
89DE JR Z,$89E2

89E8: CompasCrdNfar
Used by the routine at 89B2.
Calculate the X coordinate for drawing the planet icon on the compass
In: de - distance to station (vector length)
Out: 7,a - sign
0-6,a - X coordinate increment
hl,de,bc - undefined

89EE LD H,(IX+$02)

This entry point is used by the routine at 89A9.

89F6 RL B

89FB RLA
89FC RL B
89FE RLA
89FF LD H,(IX+$02)
8A02 BIT 7,H

8A06 OR $80
8A08 RET
8A09: GameOver
Used by the routine at 88E1.
8A09 LD A,$01
8A0B LD ($707A),A set PlayerGameOver
8A0E RET
8A0F: CalcTempAltitude
Used by the routine at 88E1.
Calculate temperature/altitude from proximity to the sun/planet and set the color
of the danger indicator
In: hl = 0 - distance (vector length)
Out: hl = hl*2.5
de,b,a - undefined
8A0F PUSH HL
8A10 ADD HL,HL
8A11 POP DE
8A12 SRL D
8A14 RR E
8A16 ADD HL,DE hl=hl*2.5
8A17 LD A,($7062) get IndDanger
8A1A LD B,A
8A1B LD A,H
8A1C CP $50 'P'
8A1E JR C,$8A30
8A20 CP $78 'x'
8A22 JR NC,$8A2C
8A24 LD A,B
8A25 CP $63 'c'
8A27 RET Z
8A28 LD A,$02
8A2A JR $8A36
8A2C LD A,$63 'c'
8A2E JR $8A36
8A30 LD A,B
8A31 CP $04
8A33 RET NZ
8A34 LD A,$06
8A36 LD ($7062),A set IndDanger
8A39 RET
8A3A: GetCrdObject
Used by the routines at 8A4D and 8A5A.
Read object coordinates
In: ix - start address of the object slot
Out: de - crd X (med/low)
bc - crd Y (med/low)
hl - crd Z (med/low)
8A3A LD E,(IX+$00)
8A3D LD D,(IX+$01)
8A40 LD C,(IX+$03)
8A43 LD B,(IX+$04)
8A46 LD L,(IX+$06)
8A49 LD H,(IX+$07)
8A4C RET
8A4D: CalcLenVectorCrdH
Used by the routine at 89B2.
Calculate distance (vector length) to an object from its coordinates, for far
distances
In: ix - start address of the object slot +1
Out: hl - distance to the object
=$0000 means more than $FFFF
de,bc,a - undefined
8A4D CALL $8A3A read object coordinates
8A50 RES 7,H
8A52 RES 7,D
8A54 RES 7,B
8A56 LD A,$01
8A58 JR $8A5F
8A5A: CalcLenVectorCrd

Calculate distance to an object from its coordinates (vector length)
In: ix - start address of the object slot
Out: hl - distance to the object
=$0000 means more than $FFFF
de,bc,a - undefined
8A5A CALL $8A3A read object coordinates
8A5D LD A,$01

8A83 CALL $7DDF Squaring
8A86 EX DE,HL
8A87 LD A,B
8A88 LD B,$00
8A8A CALL $7DDF Squaring
8A8D ADD HL,DE
8A8E JR NC,$8A91

8A91 LD A,C
8A92 CALL $7DDF Squaring

8AAF CALL $DC53 extract the square root h=sqr(hl)
8AB2 POP AF

8AB5 JR Z,$8AC6
8AB7 SRL H
8AB9 RR L
8ABB JR $8AB3
8ABD CALL $DC53 extract the square root h=sqr(hl)
8AC0 ADD HL,HL
8AC1 JR NC,$8AC6
8AC3 LD HL,$0000
8AC6 RET
8AC7: RenewCompass
Used by the routine at 89B2.
Draw the compass with pointer on screen
In: ---
Out: b=$00
hl,de,c,a - undefined
8AC7 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
8ACA CP $04
8ACC RET NC exit if not in flight
8ACD LD HL,$8BC0 the compass pointer, filled
8AD0 LD A,($88E0) =0 print the compass filled
8AD3 AND A
8AD4 JR Z,$8AD9
8AD6 LD HL,$8BB8 the compass pointer, empty
8AD9 LD DE,$8BB0 buffer for preparing to print the pointer
8ADC LD BC,$0008
8ADF LDIR
8AE1 LD HL,$5156
8AE4 LD A,($88DF) 88DF 7,=1 DownScrAdr 7,=0 UpScrAdr 0-6 =Y coordinate increment
8AE7 LD B,A
8AE8 AND $7F %01111111
8AEA JR Z,$8B07
8AEC LD A,B
8AED AND $80
8AEF JR NZ,$8AFC
8AF1 LD A,H
8AF2 DEC H
8AF3 AND $07
8AF5 CALL Z,$8B86 continue calculating the display-file address one pixel above
8AF8 DJNZ $8AF1
8AFA JR $8B07
8AFC RES 7,B
8AFE INC H
8AFF LD A,H
8B00 AND $07
8B02 CALL Z,$8B7C continue calculating the display-file address one pixel below
8B05 DJNZ $8AFE
8B07 LD ($8BAE),HL set TempAdrPrnScr
8B0A CALL $8B90 draw an empty compass on screen
8B0D LD A,($88DE) 88DE 7,=1 left 7,=0 right 0-6 =X coordinate increment
8B10 LD B,A
8B11 AND $7F
8B13 LD C,A
8B14 JR Z,$8B2C
8B16 LD A,B
8B17 AND $80
8B19 LD B,C
8B1A JR NZ,$8B4C

8F68: ScanKeyInPause
Used by the routine at 7287.
Check for pause keypress and poll the keyboard, changing game modes
In: ----
Out: cy=1 - end of game
8F68 LD A,($FC10) get ControlKey2
8F6B AND $80
8F6D RET Z
8F6E LD HL,$FC14 0-1,=00 SXNMA controls (LLFC0F)<>0 reset CS,F,c =01 Kempston controls =10 cursor controls 76850 =11 Sinclair right controls 2,=1 realistic control approximation (reaction) 3,=1 turn inertia on 4,=1 controls inverted 5,=1 up/down inverted 6,=1 Thargon interception on 7,=1 sound on
8F71 HALT
8F72 LD A,($FC13) get PressKeyPrnSymbol
8F75 INC A
8F76 JR NZ,$8F71
8F78 HALT
8F79 LD A,($FC13) get PressKeyPrnSymbol
8F7C CP $FF
8F7E JR Z,$8F78
8F80 CP $42 'B'
8F82 CALL Z,$8FC9 Invert controls
8F85 CP $44 'D'
8F87 CALL Z,$8FCF Turn inertia (toggle)
8F8A CP $46 'F'
8F8C CALL Z,$8FD5 Intercept the Thargons
8F8F CP $4B 'K'
8F91 CALL Z,$8FF6 switch controls
8F94 CP $51 'Q'
8F96 CALL Z,$8FDB Turn off sound
8F99 CP $52 'R'
8F9B CALL Z,$8FE1 Control-realism approximation system (Reaction)
8F9E CP $53 'S'
8FA0 CALL Z,$8FE7 Turn on sound
8FA3 CP $59 'Y'
8FA5 CALL Z,$8FF0 Invert up/down
8FA8 CP $31 '1'
8FAA JR Z,$8FC4 end the game
8FAC CP $20 ' '
8FAE JR Z,$8FB6 reset the pressed-keys table
8FB0 AND A
8FB1 CALL Z,$9009 sound beep
8FB4 JR $8F71
8FB6: PauseOff
Used by the routines at 8F68 and 8FC4.
Reset the pressed-keys table
8FB6 LD HL,$FC0E 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device 6,=1 - 0 -- ??? 5,=1 - I -- Global Map 4,=1 - O -- Local Map 3,=1 - P -- Planet Info 2,=1 - K -- Planet Price 1,=1 - L -- Commander Info 0,=1 - Enter -- Inventory
8FB9 XOR A
8FBA LD (HL),A
8FBB INC HL
8FBC LD (HL),A
8FBD INC HL
8FBE LD (HL),A
8FBF INC HL
8FC0 LD (HL),A
8FC1 INC HL
8FC2 LD (HL),A
8FC3 RET
8FC4: NewGame
Used by the routine at 8F68.
End the game
8FC4 CALL $8FB6 Reset the pressed-keys table
8FC7 SCF
8FC8 RET
8FC9: InvertControl
Used by the routine at 8F68.
Invert controls
8FC9 LD A,(HL)
8FCA XOR $10
8FCC LD (HL),A
8FCD XOR A
8FCE RET

Used by the routine at 8F68.

8FD0 XOR $08
8FD2 LD (HL),A
8FD3 XOR A
8FD4 RET

Used by the routine at 8F68.

8FD6 XOR $40
8FD8 LD (HL),A
8FD9 XOR A
8FDA RET
8FDB: SFX_OFF

StarsFastScroll 9965 DEFS $01
9966: Use_J_Drive
Used by the routine at 7287.
Movement on the J-drive
9966 LD A,($FC10) get ControlKey2
9969 BIT 6,A
996B PUSH AF
996C AND $BF
996E LD ($FC10),A set ControlKey2
9971 POP AF
9972 RET Z
9973 LD A,($88DC) =1 J-drive unavailable (sun nearby)
9976 DEC A
9977 RET Z
9978 LD A,($88DD) =1 J-drive unavailable (planet nearby)
997B DEC A
997C RET Z
997D LD A,($DA0F) get J_Drive_Ready4
9980 AND A
9981 RET NZ
9982 LD A,($7078) get J_Drive_Coriolis
9985 AND A
9986 RET Z
9987 XOR A
9988 LD ($DA0C),A set PilotMission2
998B LD IX,$6048 extraneous
998F CALL $7C14 zero out the list of nearby objects
9992 LD A,($9A09) get StarsRndCrd
9995 RRCA
9996 LD ($9A09),A set StarsRndCrd
9999 LD A,$01
999B JR C,$99A0
999D LD ($DA0E),A set FrameCounter
99A0 LD ($9965),A for stars only =1 J-drive running, stars move fast
99A3 LD IX,$7065 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
99A7 CALL $99B5 fast move away from/toward the sun/planet/station, only crd z changes
99AA LD IX,$706E planet coordinates in space
99AE CALL $99B5 fast move away from/toward the sun/planet/station, only crd z changes
99B1 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
99B5: MoveObjZOnly
Used by the routine at 9966.
Fast move away from/toward the sun/planet/station
only crd z changes
99B5 LD BC,$0020 ' '
99B8 LD H,(IX+$08) crd z
99BB LD L,(IX+$07)
99BE LD D,L
99BF BIT 7,H
99C1 JR NZ,$99CA
99C3 AND A
99C4 SBC HL,BC
99C6 JR C,$99D4
99C8 JR $99CD
99CA ADD HL,BC
99CB JR C,$99E9
99CD LD (IX+$08),H
99D0 LD (IX+$07),L
99D3 RET
99D4 LD HL,$2000
99D7 LD E,(IX+$06)
99DA AND A
99DB SBC HL,DE
99DD LD A,$80
99DF LD (IX+$08),A
99E2 LD (IX+$07),H
99E5 LD (IX+$06),L
99E8 RET

99EB LD (IX+$08),A
99EE LD A,$FF
99F0 LD (IX+$07),A
99F3 LD (IX+$06),A
99F6 RET
99F7: Data block at 99F7
StarsAngleLR 99F7 DEFB $00 star rotation (player's rotation angles) $00..$0F
StarsAngleDU 99F8 DEFB $00 $00..$0F
99F9 DEFB $00 for intermediate storage in the star routine
99FA DEFB $00 for stars =0 front/rear =1 left/right
AdrDataStar 99FB DEFW $0000 address of the current star's data
StarsSpeed 99FD DEFB $00 star speed $01..$10 (player's speed /2 +1)
wrkStarCrdXl 99FE DEFB $00 +3 DataStars
wrkStarCrdXh 99FF DEFB $00 +0 DataStars
wrkStarCrdYl 9A00 DEFB $00 +4 DataStars
wrkStarCrdYh 9A01 DEFB $00 +1 DataStars
wrkSpeedStar 9A02 DEFB $00 +2 DataStars
StarsPrevViewScr 9A03 DEFB $00 view-screen number the stars are calculated for (inlined into the routine)
StarsMax 9A04 DEFB $00 maximum number of stars displayed
AdrInStarsBuf 9A05 DEFW $0000 address in the buffer of print addresses for the current star
StarsFlg 9A07 DEFB $00 current star's flags 7,=1 CrdX negative 6,=1 CrdY negative 5,=1 =0/1 front/rear 4,=1 rotation negative =0 rotation positive 3,=1 moving down =0 moving up
StarsNumPrn 9A08 DEFB $00 number of stars generated for printing
StarsRndCrd 9A09 DEFB $55 byte to vary the generation of a new star's coordinates
StarsAdrPrnData 9A0A DEFB $20,$00,$8B,$8C,$4C,$59,$20,$00

9A46: InitVarLaunch
Used by the routine at 7AF8.
Print missiles, init variables, print cockpit attributes accounting for
the presence of an Escape Capsule
9A46 CALL $A6D3 print missiles on the cockpit
9A49 CALL $D563 clear all messages during flight
9A4C XOR A
9A4D LD ($A80F),A set Missile1
9A50 LD ($704E),A set TempLaser
9A53 LD ($704C),A set Temperature
9A56 LD ($707E),A set AutoDocking
9A59 LD A,$A0
9A5B LD ($7046),A set ShieldFront
9A5E LD ($7048),A set ShieldRear
9A61 LD ($7050),A set Altitude
9A64 LD HL,$7054 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
9A67 LD B,$04
9A69 LD (HL),A
9A6A INC HL
9A6B INC HL
9A6C DJNZ $9A69
9A6E: SetAtrCockpitDevice
Used by the routine at A5C0.
Set cockpit attributes on screen/in buffer
depending on whether an Escape Capsule is present
9A6E LD HL,$5A23
9A71 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
9A74 CP $04
9A76 JR C,$9A79
9A78 INC H
9A79 LD C,$17
9A7B LD A,($A84B) get PilotEscapePod
9A7E AND A
9A7F JR Z,$9A83
9A81 LD C,$1F
9A83 LD DE,$001B
9A86 PUSH HL
9A87 CALL $9A8F set cockpit indicator attributes C - Attribute HL - address in the attribute area DE =1Bh
9A8A POP HL
9A8B LD A,L
9A8C ADD A,$15
9A8E LD L,A
9A8F LD B,$07 set cockpit indicator attributes C - Attribute HL - address in the attribute area DE =1Bh
9A91 PUSH BC
9A92 LD B,$05
9A94 LD (HL),C
9A95 INC HL
9A96 DJNZ $9A94
9A98 POP BC
9A99 ADD HL,DE
9A9A DJNZ $9A91
9A9C RET

9E74: PrintStars
Used by the routines at 7287 and 7AF8.
Process and print stars accounting for player movement, including on the J-drive
In: ---
Out: hl,de,bc,hl',de',bc,a - undefined
9E74 LD A,($9965) for stars only =1 J-drive running, stars move fast
9E77 DEC A
9E78 JR NZ,$9E8D process and print stars accounting for player movement
9E7A LD ($9965),A for stars only =1 J-drive running, stars move fast
9E7D LD A,$1E
9E7F LD ($7044),A set HighDeltaSpeed
9E82 CALL $9E8D process and print stars accounting for player movement
9E85 LD A,$10
9E87 LD ($7044),A set HighDeltaSpeed
9E8A CALL $9E8D
9E8D: CalcAndPrnStars
Used by the routine at 9E74.
Process and print stars accounting for player movement
In: ---
Out: hl,de,bc,hl',de',bc,a - undefined
9E8D XOR A
9E8E LD ($9A08),A set StarsNumPrn
9E91 LD ($99FA),A for stars =0 front/rear =1 left/right
9E94 LD A,($7044) get HighDeltaSpeed
9E97 SRL A
9E99 INC A
9E9A LD ($99FD),A set StarsSpeed
9E9D LD HL,($7042) get HighAngleLR

9EBA CP B
9EBB LD ($9A03),A set StarsPrevViewScr
9EBE CALL NZ,$A44C swap the coordinates of each star

9EC4 LD ($9A05),HL set AdrInStarsBuf
9EC7 LD B,$00

A4BD: PrintIndDanger
Used by the routines at B057, D0B9 and D486.
Print the danger indicator and the missile indicator
In: (IndDanger) - indicator state
Out: b =$00
hl,de,c,a - undefined
A4BD LD HL,$7062 <>0 in flight (also the danger indicator color) =0 docked =$04=%00 000 100 leaving the station, in flight (green) =$63=%01 100 011 4th energy unit not full (flashing red)) =$02=%00 000 010 one of the front/rear shields empty (red) =$06=%00 000 110 something nearby (yellow)
A4C0 LD A,(HL)
A4C1 AND A
A4C2 RET Z
A4C3 LD A,($7046) get ShieldFront
A4C6 AND A
A4C7 JR Z,$A4CF
A4C9 LD A,($7048) get ShieldRear
A4CC AND A
A4CD JR NZ,$A4D6
A4CF LD A,(HL)
A4D0 CP $63 'c'
A4D2 JR Z,$A4D6
A4D4 LD (HL),$02
A4D6 LD A,(HL)
A4D7 CP $63 'c'
A4D9 JR NZ,$A4E8
A4DB LD HL,$A4BC counter for the danger indicator's blinking
A4DE INC (HL)
A4DF BIT 1,(HL)
A4E1 JR NZ,$A4E6
A4E3 XOR A
A4E4 JR $A4E8
A4E6 LD A,$02
A4E8 LD D,A
A4E9 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
A4EC CP $04
A4EE JR NC,$A511 menu displayed
A4F0 LD HL,$5A28 view screen on, danger-indicator color
A4F3 LD (HL),D
A4F4 INC HL
A4F5 LD (HL),D
A4F6 LD HL,$5A48
A4F9 LD (HL),D
A4FA INC HL
A4FB LD (HL),D
A4FC CALL $A553
A4FF LD A,($A80F) get Missile1
A502 CP $01
A504 JR NZ,$A508
A506 LD A,$04 =1 -> paper 4
A508 ADD A,A
A509 ADD A,A
A50A ADD A,A
A50B ADD A,$07
A50D LD ($5AE7),A
A510 RET
A511 LD HL,$BBBF menu displayed
A514 LD (HL),$00
A516 LD HL,$011B
A519 LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
A51C CP $09
A51E JR NC,$A53C
A520 LD A,D
A521 AND A
A522 JR Z,$A536
A524 CP $02
A526 JR NZ,$A538
A528 LD A,$21 '!'
A52A CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A52D LD A,$22 '"'
A52F CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A532 LD D,$17

A536 LD D,$02

A54D LD (HL),D
A54E LD HL,$BBBF

A553 LD A,$04
A555 LD ($7062),A set IndDanger
A558 RET
A559: Process_MissBombEPod
Used by the routine at 7287.
Control missiles, bombs, escape capsule
A559 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
A55C CP $04
A55E RET NC
A55F CALL $A660 Print indicators: danger, station, ECM on the cockpit
A562 CALL $A706 Use of a missile
A565 CALL $A77B Use of the escape capsule
A568 CALL $9260 Energy bomb explosion
A56B LD HL,$FC11 7,=1 - C -- Docking Computer 6,=1 - Q -- Escape Capsule 5,=1 - W -- Energy Bomb 4,=1 - E -- ECM System 3,=1 - U -- UnTarget 2,=1 - F -- Fire Missile 1,=1 - T -- Target Missile 0,=1 - A -- Fire Laser
A56E LD A,(HL)
A56F AND $91
A571 LD (HL),A
A572: Damage2Pilot
Used by the routine at 7287.
Deal damage to the player's ship
In: ---
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
A572 LD HL,$7062 <>0 in flight (also the danger indicator color) =0 docked =$04=%00 000 100 leaving the station, in flight (green) =$63=%01 100 011 4th energy unit not full (flashing red)) =$02=%00 000 010 one of the front/rear shields empty (red) =$06=%00 000 110 something nearby (yellow)
A575 XOR A
A576 CP (HL)
A577 RET Z
A578 LD A,($705A) get ShieldUnit4
A57B CP $A0
A57D JR Z,$A584
A57F LD A,$63 'c'
A581 LD ($7062),A set IndDanger
A584 CALL $A63A restore ship energy
A587 LD HL,$7046 front shield +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
A58A LD A,($DA18) get DamageFrontShield
A58D CALL $A5C0 decrease front/rear shield and energy-unit power
A590 LD HL,$7048 rear shield +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
A593 LD A,($DA19) get DamageRearShield
A596 CALL $A5C0 decrease front/rear shield and energy-unit power
A599 LD A,($A80C) get CloakingDeviceOn
A59C AND A
A59D LD A,($DA17) get DamageEnergyUnit
A5A0 JR Z,$A5A3
A5A2 INC A
A5A3 LD C,A
A5A4 LD B,$04
A5A6: DecEnergyPlayer
Used by the routine at A5C0.
Decrease ship energy
energy=energy-(b*c)
In: c - amount by which the player's energy will decrease
b - energy-reduction coefficient
Out: hl,d,b,a - undefined
A5A6 LD HL,$7054 +0 - current value -1 - value shown on screen
A5A9 LD D,$04
A5AB LD A,(HL)
A5AC SUB C
A5AD JR NC,$A5BC
A5AF LD (HL),$00
A5B1 INC HL
A5B2 INC HL
A5B3 DEC D
A5B4 JR NZ,$A5AB
A5B6 LD A,$01
A5B8 LD ($707A),A set PlayerGameOver
A5BB RET
A5BC LD (HL),A
A5BD DJNZ $A5AB
A5BF RET
A5C0: DecEnergyShieldFR
Used by the routine at A572.
Decrease front/rear shield and energy-unit power
In: hl - shield address
a - damage to the shield
Out: hl,b,a - undefined
A5C0 LD C,A
A5C1 AND A
A5C2 JR NZ,$A5CC
A5C4 LD HL,$A639 damage intensity; if the damage empties the front/rear shield, +5; if that makes it >=$0D, chance of destroying cargo/equipment
A5C7 LD A,(HL)
A5C8 AND A
A5C9 RET Z
A5CA DEC (HL)
A5CB RET
A5CC PUSH BC
A5CD PUSH HL
A5CE CP $46 'F'
A5D0 CALL NC,$A5F0 heavy damage. destruction of something (fuel/cargo, etc.)
A5D3 POP HL
A5D4 POP BC
A5D5 LD B,$05
A5D7 LD A,(HL)
A5D8 SUB C
A5D9 JR C,$A5DF
A5DB LD (HL),A
A5DC DJNZ $A5D7
A5DE RET
A5DF LD (HL),$00
A5E1 DEC B
A5E2 RET Z
A5E3 CALL $A5A6 decrease ship energy energy=energy-(b*c)
A5E6 LD HL,$A639 damage intensity; if the damage empties the front/rear shield, +5; if that makes it >=$0D, chance of destroying cargo/equipment
A5E9 LD A,(HL)
A5EA ADD A,$05
A5EC LD (HL),A
A5ED CP $0D
A5EF RET C

A5FA RRCA

A660: PrnDangerEcmDock
Used by the routine at A559.
Print indicators: danger, station, ECM on the cockpit
A660 LD HL,$BBBF
A663 LD (HL),$00 nop
A665 LD HL,$1108 upper-right quarter
A668 LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
A66B LD A,$26 '&'
A66D CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A670 LD HL,$1208 lower-right quarter
A673 LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
A676 LD A,$24 '$'
A678 CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A67B LD HL,$1607
A67E LD A,($DA16) get TimeWorkECM
A681 AND A
A682 JR Z,$A689
A684 LD DE,$A6C3 ECM on (4 bytes)
A687 JR $A68C
A689 LD DE,$A6C7 ECM off (4 bytes)
A68C CALL $A6A9 print the ECM system state, station icon, etc. depending on the message and coordinates HL - print coordinates DE - message address
A68F LD HL,$1615
A692 LD A,($7077) get CoriolisNear
A695 AND A
A696 JR Z,$A69D
A698 LD DE,$A6CB icon character for being near a station (4 bytes)
A69B JR $A6A0
A69D LD DE,$A6CF icon character for not being near a station (4 bytes)
A6A0 CALL $A6A9 print the ECM system state, station icon, etc. depending on the message and coordinates HL - print coordinates DE - message address
A6A3 LD HL,$BBBF
A6A6 LD (HL),$A9 xor c
A6A8 RET
A6A9 CALL $A6AC print the ECM system state, station icon, etc. depending on the message and coordinates HL - print coordinates DE - message address
A6AC LD ($A80D),HL
A6AF PUSH HL
A6B0 CALL $A6B6
A6B3 POP HL
A6B4 INC H
A6B5 RET
A6B6 CALL $A6B9
A6B9 LD A,(DE)
A6BA PUSH DE
A6BB CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A6BE POP DE
A6BF INC DE
A6C0 RET
A6C1: Data block at A6C1
A6C1 DEFB $26,$24
dataECMon A6C3 DEFB $3B,$3C,$3D,$3E ECM on (4 bytes)
dataECMoff A6C7 DEFB $20,$20,$40,$40 ECM off (4 bytes)
dataDocking A6CB DEFB $5B,$5C,$5D,$5E icon character for being near a station (4 bytes)
dataNoDocking A6CF DEFB $20,$5F,$40,$2B icon character for not being near a station (4 bytes)
A6D3: PrintMissiles
Used by the routines at 9A46 and A706.
Print missiles on the cockpit
In: ---
Out: b= $00
hl,de,c,a - undefined
A6D3 LD HL,$1702
A6D6 LD ($A80D),HL display the number of missiles on the cockpit
A6D9 LD HL,$BBBF
A6DC LD (HL),$00 nop
A6DE LD A,($A845) get PilotMissiles
A6E1 AND A
A6E2 JR Z,$A6EE
A6E4 LD B,A
A6E5 PUSH BC
A6E6 LD A,$25 '%'
A6E8 CALL $BA9D display the missile icon
A6EB POP BC
A6EC DJNZ $A6E5
A6EE LD A,($A845) get PilotMissiles
A6F1 NEG
A6F3 ADD A,$04
A6F5 RET Z
A6F6 LD B,A print blank space instead of a missile
A6F7 PUSH BC
A6F8 LD A,$40 '@'
A6FA CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
A6FD POP BC
A6FE DJNZ $A6F7
A700 LD HL,$BBBF
A703 LD (HL),$A9 xor c
A705 RET
A706: UseMissile
Used by the routine at A559.
Use of a missile
A706 LD A,($A845) get PilotMissiles
A709 AND A
A70A RET Z
A70B LD A,($A80F) get Missile1
A70E CP $01
A710 JR NZ,$A72E
A712 LD HL,$A89A =0 missile targeting was on <>0 reverse index of the object the missile is targeting (8 - slot 0)
A715 LD A,(HL)
A716 AND A
A717 JR NZ,$A72E
A719 LD A,($DA12) get ObjInTarget
A71C AND A
A71D JR Z,$A72E
A71F LD (HL),A
A720 LD HL,$A80F =0 missile not targeted =1 missile ready to lock target =2 missile targeted on something
A723 LD (HL),$02
A725 LD HL,$0070 'p' ; target-lock sound
A728 LD DE,$0060 '`'
A72B CALL $03B5
A72E LD HL,$FC11 7,=1 - C -- Docking Computer 6,=1 - Q -- Escape Capsule 5,=1 - W -- Energy Bomb 4,=1 - E -- ECM System 3,=1 - U -- UnTarget 2,=1 - F -- Fire Missile 1,=1 - T -- Target Missile 0,=1 - A -- Fire Laser
A731 LD A,(HL)
A732 LD HL,$A80F =0 missile not targeted =1 missile ready to lock target =2 missile targeted on something
A735 SRL A
A737 SRL A
A739 JR C,$A744 attempt to target the missile (T key)
A73B SRL A
A73D JR C,$A752 attempt to launch the missile (F key)
A73F SRL A
A741 JR C,$A74F untarget the missile (U key)
A743 RET
A744 LD A,(HL) attempt to target the missile (T key)
A745 CP $02
A747 RET Z
A748 LD (HL),$01
A74A XOR A
A74B LD ($A89A),A set nObjMissile
A74E RET
A74F LD (HL),$00 untarget the missile (U key)
A751 RET
A752 LD A,(HL) attempt to launch the missile (F key)
A753 CP $02
A755 RET NZ
A756 LD A,($A89A) get nObjMissile
A759 ADD A,A
A75A ADD A,A
A75B ADD A,A
A75C ADD A,A
A75D OR $02 a=xxxx0010=$x2
A75F PUSH HL
A760 CALL $F2CA
A763 POP HL
A764 JR Z,$A76C if successful
A766 LD HL,$A87F 7,=1 missile launch failed 6-0,= Y print coordinate of the message
A769 SET 7,(HL)
A76B RET
A76C LD (HL),$00
A76E LD HL,$707D which sound to play =$00 no sound (silent) 0,=1 energy bomb exploded 1,=1 missile launched 2,=1 colliding with an object/object destroyed 3,=1 laser fired 4,=1 laser fired 5,=1 ECM working 6,=1 hit by laser on the player/docking damage 7,=1
A771 SET 1,(HL)
A773 LD HL,$A845 number of missiles
A776 DEC (HL)
A777 JP $A6D3 print missiles on the cockpit

A7B7 AND $DF
A7B9 LD (HL),A

A7C8 LD A,$17

A7DA JP $B27B goods purchase menu
A7DD JP $B474 goods sale menu
A7E0 JP $B53F equipment purchase menu
A7E3 JP $AB4E print the galaxy map menu
A7E6 JP $A9CA print the local galaxy map
A7E9 JP $B991 print planet info
A7EC JP $D07D print the list of goods on the planet
A7EF JP $D0B9 print info for key 'L'
A7F2 JP $D279 print the cargo hold contents
A7F5 JP $B249 decrease the X coordinate and print the small cursor
A7F8 JP $B259 increase the X coordinate and print the small cursor
A7FB JP $B236 increase the Y coordinate and print the small cursor
A7FE JP $B224 decrease the Y coordinate and print the small cursor
A801 JP $B26A position the small cursor at the large cursor's coordinates
A804 JP $B1AC position the cursor on the nearest planet and print its name and distance to it
A807 JP $B0E9 search for a planet by its name
A80A: Data block at A80A
JumpDocking A80A DEFB $00 =1 interplanetary jump/docking =2 intergalactic jump
FlgJumpDocking A80B DEFB $00 =1 keypress handling disabled, enabled during: docking, the concentric-circles drawing routine is running
CloakingDeviceOn A80C DEFB $00 =1 Cloaking Device on =1 energy drain while in use
CrdXPrnSymbol A80D DEFB $00 character print coordinates
CrdYPrnSymbol A80E DEFB $00
Missile1 A80F DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00 =0 missile not targeted =1 missile ready to lock target =2 missile targeted on something
PrnCrdXCursor A813 DEFB $00 on-screen X coordinate of the small cursor
PrnCrdYCursor A814 DEFB $00 on-screen Y coordinate of the small cursor
RadiusCircleMap A815 DEFB $00
PilotName A816 DEFB $4A,$41,$4D,$45,$53,$4F,$4E,$20 1..11 pilot name
A81E DEFB $20,$20,$00
PilotLegalStatus A821 DEFB $00 pilot's legal status - clean 1..31h offender 32h..FFh fugitive
PilotRating0 A822 DEFB $00 pilot's combat rating 0,0,0...255,8,0 Harmless 0,9,0...255,8,0 M.Harmless 0,17,0...255,16,0 Poor 0,33,0...255,32,0 Average 0,65,0...255,64,0 Above Average 0,129,0...255,128,0 Competent 0,0,3...255,255,2 Dangerous 0,0,11...255,255,10 Deadly 0,0,26...255,255,25 Elite
PilotRating1 A823 DEFB $00
PilotRating2 A824 DEFB $00
JumpCounter A825 DEFW $0000 16-17 jump counter, including intergalactic byte_A872 bit 0-2,=??0 and low =20h..FFh - give mission 1 byte_A872 bit 0-2,=?01 and low =80h..FFh - give mission 2 byte_A872 bit 0-2,=011 and low =FEh..FFh - give mission 3
PilotNumGalaxy A827 DEFB $01 galaxy number
PilotCash2 A828 DEFW $0000 19-20 pilot's money (whole thousands) CASH 0..65535 - 0000..65535000
PilotCash1 A82A DEFW $0000 pilot's money (from 0 to 1000) CASH 0..65535 - 0.0..6553.5
PilotRnd A82C DEFB $00 ????
PilotCargo A82D DEFB $00,$00,$00 cargo present in the ship (17 bytes) +00 Food
cargoSlaves A830 DEFB $00,$00,$00 +03 Slaves
cargoNarc A833 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00 +06 narcotics
cargoFirearms A837 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00 +0A Firearms
cargoGemStones A83C DEFB $00,$00 +0F Gem-Stones
PilotFreeCargo A83E DEFB $00 remaining ship cargo capacity
PilotFrontLaser A83F DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00
PilotMission1 A843 DEFB $00 mission-1 byte =0 mission not given <>0 mission 1 given
Pilot_Fuel A844 DEFB $00 Fuel
PilotMissiles A845 DEFB $00 number of missiles
PilotLargeCargo A846 DEFB $00 +49 large cargo bay =$59 if mission 1 was taken
PilotECMSystem A847 DEFB $00,$00,$00 ECM System 7,=1 system on
PilotFuelScoops A84A DEFB $00 FuelScoops
PilotEscapePod A84B DEFB $00 Escape capsule
PilotEnergyBomb A84C DEFB $00 Energy bomb
PilotEnergyUnit A84D DEFB $00 Energy Unit
PilotDocComputer A84E DEFB $00 docking computer
PilotGyperdrive A84F DEFB $00,$00,$00 hyperdrive
PilotGalaxyData0 A852 DEFW $0000 galaxy data
PilotGalaxyData2 A854 DEFW $0000
PilotGalaxyData4 A856 DEFW $0000
PilotPlanetData A858 DEFB $00 ?????
Pilot_CrdYSystem A859 DEFB $00,$00 Y coordinate of the current system
Pilot_CrdXSystem A85B DEFB $00,$00,$00 X coordinate of the current system
PilotPlanetTechLvl A85E DEFB $00 ???
PilotPlanetGov A85F DEFB $00 ???
PilotPlanetProduct A860 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00 goods at the station +75
A868 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
Alien Items are never actually for sale: at the end of every GenerateProductPrice run, the freshly generated quantity for this last slot is overwritten with zero. The player can only acquire them by scooping them in combat, and can only sell them here, never buy them.
PilotPlProdAlienIt A870 DEFB $00 +91 =0 the station was destroyed as part of the mission
PilotMission3 A871 DEFB $00 +92 >0 - mission 3 given =1 mission 3 will be given on docking ( =1 time to give the mission, will become =2 after the jump) =2 mission 3 will be given on docking =3 mission 3 given (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =4 mission 3 given, the ship is in the target system (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =5 mission 3 completed, there will be congratulations on docking
PilotExtWeapon A872 DEFB $00 +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) =1 mission 3 was completed =1 - mission 3 completed =1 - mission 2 completed =1 - mission 1 completed
PilotDataOfSun A873 DEFB $27,$08,$00,$00,$0A,$19,$00,$00 sun data, 9 bytes, copied here when saving
A87B DEFB $3D
PilotMission1_1 A87C DEFB $00 =1 - mission 1 given (taken)
Pilot_CrdXTarget A87D DEFB $00 X coordinate of the small cursor on the large map
Pilot_CrdYTarget A87E DEFB $00 +103 (last byte to save) Y coordinate of the small cursor on the large map
MissileJammed A87F DEFB $08,$C9 7,=1 missile launch failed 6-0,= Y print coordinate of the message
ShipLaunchMisile A881 DEFB $07,$78 7,=1 the enemy fired a missile
UntargetMissile A883 DEFB $09,$C8 7,=1 missile untargeted (not by the player)
DestDeviceCargo A885 DEFB $02,$FD 7,=1 some equipment/cargo destroyed -> message number goes into nMsgDamage
CashBonus A887 DEFB $0C,$E2 7,=1 an object was destroyed =1 a cash bonus was received (bonus amount in LastBonusCash)
CargoFound A889 DEFB $0B,$00 7,1 =1 cargo picked up in space
RatingRightOn A88B DEFB $06,$65 7,=1 combat rating increased, show the message "right on commander"
FlgPrnNumber A88D DEFB $01 =$00 print the number with leading zeros (otherwise with spaces)
DigitsInNumber A88E DEFB $00 number of digits to print for the number (padded on the left with spaces or zeros)
NoFuel A88F DEFB $0A,$1F 7,=1 - fuel was zeroed out
FlgPrnSym1 A891 DEFB $00 print flag =1 - print in uppercase
FlgPrnSym2 A892 DEFB $01 print flag =1 there was a line feed
FlgPrnSym3 A893 DEFB $00 print flag =1 - capitalize first letter
FlgPrnDescr A894 DEFB $00,$00,$00 =1 generate the planet description line
A897: ResetFuelAfterEscape
A897 JP $D862 zero out fuel
A89A: Data block at A89A
nObjMissile A89A DEFB $00 =0 missile targeting was on <>0 reverse index of the object the missile is targeting (8 - slot 0)
A89B: CollectFoundCanister
A89B JP $A8B7 add a found canister to the cargo
A89E JP $A900 increase the money amount
A8A1 JP $A8EE increase combat rating
A8A4: Data block at A8A4
A8A4 DEFB $A7,$CD,$E4
A8A7: DestroyCargoFromDamage
A8A7 JP $A5F0 heavy damage. destruction of something (fuel/cargo, etc.)
A8AA: ActivateCloakingDevice
Used by the routine at A8B7.
Put on the Cloaking Device
A8AA LD HL,$A872 +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed
A8AD SET 6,(HL)
A8AF LD HL,$A824
A8B2 INC (HL)
A8B3 LD A,$7D '}' ; message number
A8B5 JR $A8CA
A8B7: AddFoundCargo
Used by the routines at A89B and EA51.
Add a found canister to the cargo
In: a - cargo type in the canister
=$FF cloaking device
=0..16 cargo
Out: hl,de,b,a - undefined
A8B7 PUSH AF
A8B8 CP $FF
A8BA JR Z,$A8AA put on the Cloaking Device
A8BC LD HL,$A83E remaining ship cargo capacity
A8BF DEC (HL)
A8C0 LD E,A
A8C1 LD D,$00
A8C3 LD HL,$A82D 24 cargo present in the ship (17 bytes) +00 Food
A8C6 ADD HL,DE
A8C7 INC (HL)
A8C8 ADD A,$D0 message number
This entry point is used by the routine at A8AA.
A8CA LD HL,$A889 7,1 =1 cargo picked up in space
A8CD SET 7,(HL)
A8CF INC HL
A8D0 LD (HL),A
A8D1 POP AF update legal status if needed
A8D2 LD B,$01
This entry point is used by the routine at B3A1.
SetLegalStatCargo A8D4 CP $0A
A8D6 JR Z,$A8E1
A8D8 CP $03
A8DA JR Z,$A8DF
A8DC CP $06
A8DE RET NZ
A8DF SLA B
A8E1 SLA B
A8E3 LD HL,$A821 pilot's legal status 0 - clean 1..31h offender 32h..FFh fugitive
A8E6 LD A,(HL)
A8E7 ADD A,B
A8E8 JR NC,$A8EC
A8EA LD A,$FF
A8EC LD (HL),A
A8ED RET
A8EE: IncBattleRating
Used by the routines at A89B, DA1D and F928.
Increase combat rating
In: de - rating increment
Out: hl,de - undefined
A8EE LD HL,$A822 13 pilot's combat rating 0,0,0...255,8,0 Harmless 0,9,0...255,8,0 M.Harmless 0,17,0...255,16,0 Poor 0,33,0...255,32,0 Average 0,65,0...255,64,0 Above Average 0,129,0...255,128,0 Competent 0,0,3...255,255,2 Dangerous 0,0,11...255,255,10 Deadly 0,0,26...255,255,25 Elite
A8F1 LD A,(HL)
A8F2 ADD A,E
A8F3 LD (HL),A
A8F4 INC HL
A8F5 LD A,(HL)
A8F6 ADC A,D
A8F7 LD (HL),A
A8F8 RET NC
A8F9 INC HL
A8FA INC (HL)
A8FB LD HL,$A88B 7,=1 combat rating increased, show the message "right on commander"
A8FE JR $A90D
A900: AddCashObj
Used by the routines at A89B and DA1D.
Increase the money amount
In: de - amount of money (from 0.1 to 6553.5)
Out: hl,de - undefined
A900 LD A,D
A901 OR E
A902 RET Z
A903 LD ($D449),DE cash reward for a combat kill
A907 CALL $B84D add money
A90A LD HL,$A887 7,=1 an object was destroyed =1 a cash bonus was received (bonus amount in LastBonusCash)
This entry point is used by the routine at A8EE.
A90D SET 7,(HL)
A90F RET
A910: Message at A910
PilotDataSaved A910 DEFM "JAMESON",$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$E8,$03,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$14,$01,$00,$00,$00,$00,"F",$03,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,"JZH",$02,"S"
A951 DEFM $B7,"8",$AD,$9C,$14,$1D,$15,$05,$03,$11,$12,$1A,$0E,"'",$08,$00,$00,$0A,$19,$00,$00,"=",$0E,$11,$0C,$00,$00,$00
A96D DEFM "'",$08,$01,"*M",$8F,$F2,$88,$05
A976: InitGame
Used by the routine at 716B.
A976 CALL $D563 clear all messages during flight
A979 CALL $7C2D restore pilot data from memory
A97C LD A,($A85B) get Pilot_CrdXSystem
A97F LD ($A87D),A set Pilot_CrdXTarget
A982 LD A,($A859) get Pilot_CrdYSystem
A985 LD ($A87E),A set Pilot_CrdYTarget
A988 CALL $ACC7 Find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data
A98B LD HL,$AF01
A98E LD (HL),$00
A990 PUSH HL
A991 CALL GenerateAnotherPrice generate prices without quantities at the station
A994 POP HL
A995 LD (HL),$77 'w'
A997 LD HL,$AC40 name of the planet the ship is set to
A99A LD DE,$AC37 name of the active planet
A99D LD BC,$0008
A9A0 LDIR
A9A2 LD HL,$A96D sun coordinates when leaving the station
A9A5 LD DE,$7065 data for printing the sun, 9 bytes long, crd low x planet
A9A8 LD BC,$0009
A9AB LDIR
A9AD XOR A
A9AE LD ($D573),A set JumpCountdown
A9B1 RET
A9B2: Data block at A9B2
BufferCrdY A9B2 DEFB $C9,$CD,$8C,$A9,$32,$D3,$A9,$11
A9BA DEFB $A0,$91,$3A,$37,$AB,$E6,$18,$83
A9C2 DEFB $5F,$30,$01,$14,$06,$08,$C5,$3A

A9D1 INC HL
A9D2 DJNZ $A9D0

A9D7 LD A,$BE
A9D9 CALL $B73E Print the galaxy map header
A9DC CALL $BBC6 Fill attribute row 3 from the bottom (row 21) with attribute =5
A9DF LD A,($A85B) get Pilot_CrdXSystem

A9E4 CALL C,$AC0A Set A=0
A9E7 LD ($AA51),A

A9ED ADD A,$13
A9EF CALL C,$AC0C Set A=0FFh
A9F2 LD ($AA55),A
A9F5 LD A,($A859) get Pilot_CrdYSystem
A9F8 SUB $22 '"'
A9FA CALL C,$AC0A Set A=0
A9FD LD ($AA5C),A
AA00 LD A,($A859) get Pilot_CrdYSystem
AA03 ADD A,$22 '"'
AA05 CALL C,$AC0C Set A=0FFh

AA0B LD A,$18
AA0D LD ($00C0),A

AA1A LD A,$61 'a'
AA1C LD ($A813),A set PrnCrdXCursor
AA1F LD A,$60 '`'
AA21 LD ($A814),A set PrnCrdYCursor
AA24 CALL $D886 print the cursor on the galaxy map screen

AA2A CALL $AA44 print stars/star names on the local map
AA2D LD HL,$AAE7 print the name of one star on the local map
AA30 CALL $AA44 print stars/star names on the local map
AA33 LD A,($A844) get Pilot_Fuel
AA36 LD ($A815),A
AA39 CALL $8461 draw on the galaxy map a circle of the flight radius reachable with available fuel

AA3E LD ($D883),A cursor width
AA41 JP $B1C0 print the small cursor on the global/local map

AA47 CALL $AE79 copy galaxy data into the working area
AA4A LD B,$00
AA4C PUSH BC
AA4D LD A,($AC18)
AA50 CP $00
AA52 JR C,$AA68

AA56 JR NC,$AA68

AA5D JR C,$AA68

AA74 LD B,A
AA75 LD A,($AC18)
AA78 SUB B
AA79 ADD A,A
AA7A ADD A,A
AA7B ADD A,C

AA7E LD C,A
AA7F LD D,$60 '`'
AA81 LD A,($A859) get Pilot_CrdYSystem
AA84 LD B,A
AA85 LD A,($AC16) data for generating a planet
AA88 SUB B
AA89 ADD A,A
AA8A NEG
AA8C ADD A,D
AA8D SUB $0E
AA8F LD B,A
AA90 PUSH BC
AA91 CALL $22AA
AA94 POP BC
AA95 RET

AAA9 LD B,$08

AAE6 DEFS $01

ABED ADD A,$18

ABF0 LD A,($AC18)
ABF3 LD C,A

ABF9 ADD A,A

ABFB ADD A,A

AC09 RET

AC56: NextGalaxy
Switch to the next galaxy
In: ----
Out: a - galaxy number
hl,de - ????
AC56 LD HL,$A827 galaxy number
AC59 LD A,(HL)
AC5A INC A
AC5B AND $07
AC5D LD (HL),A
AC5E LD HL,$A852 galaxy data
AC61 CALL $AC6D
AC64 LD HL,$A854
AC67 CALL $AC6D
AC6A LD HL,$A856
AC6D: Routine at AC6D
Used by the routine at AC56.
AC6D LD E,(HL)
AC6E RLC E
AC70 LD (HL),E
AC71 INC HL
AC72 LD D,(HL)
AC73 RLC D
AC75 LD (HL),D
AC76 RET
AC77: FindPlanetByName
Used by the routine at B0E9.
Search for a planet in the galaxy by name
In: InputText - planet name
Out: DataOfPlanet - planet data, plus its info has been generated
cy =1 planet not found
=0 found
AC77 CALL $AE79 copy galaxy data into the working area
AC7A XOR A
AC7B LD ($AC53),A
AC7E CALL $AE81 save the working area with galaxy data
AC81 CALL $AD6F generate the planet name and prepare data for generating the next name
AC84 LD HL,$AC25 entered text (pilot name when saving/loading, planet name when searching)
AC87 LD DE,$AC2E for generating the planet name
AC8A LD B,(HL)
AC8B LD A,(DE)
AC8C CP B
AC8D JR NZ,$AC96
AC8F AND A
AC90 JR Z,$AC9F
AC92 INC HL
AC93 INC DE
AC94 JR $AC8A
AC96 LD HL,$AC53
AC99 INC (HL)
AC9A JP NZ,$AC7E
AC9D SCF
AC9E RET
AC9F CALL $AE89 restore the working area with galaxy data
ACA2 CALL $B1C0 print the small cursor on the global/local map
ACA5 CALL $ADDF Build planet data (economy, government, tech level,
ACA8 CALL $B1C0 print the small cursor on the global/local map
ACAB CALL $ADD3 Copy the planet name into the working area
ACAE JP $AD08 calculate fuel consumption for the jump to the planet
ACB1: Unused
SaveHL_1 ACB1 DEFS $02
ACB3: SearchNearPlanet
Used by the routines at B1AC, B991, D574 and D5F5.
Find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data, set it as
the current target and draw the cursor if the map is displayed

ACB6 CP $07
ACB8 JR C,$ACC7 find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data and set it as the current target
ACBA CP $09
ACBC JR NC,$ACC7 find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data and set it as the current target
ACBE CALL $B1C0 print the small cursor on the global/local map
ACC1 CALL $ACC7 Find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data
ACC4 JP $B1C0 print the small cursor on the global/local map
ACC7: SearchPlanetNearCursor
Used by the routines at A976 and ACB3.
Find the planet nearest the cursor, build its data
and set it as the current target
ACC7 CALL $AE79 copy galaxy data into the working area
ACCA LD HL,$FFFF
ACCD LD ($ACB1),HL intermediate variable for calculating the distance from the cursor to the nearest planet
ACD0 LD B,$00
ACD2 PUSH BC
ACD3 LD A,($AC18)
ACD6 LD B,A
ACD7 LD A,($AC16) data for generating a planet
ACDA CALL $AD21 squared distance between the cursor and the planet
ACDD JR C,$ACEF
ACDF EX DE,HL
ACE0 LD HL,($ACB1) intermediate variable for calculating the distance from the cursor to the nearest planet
ACE3 AND A
ACE4 SBC HL,DE
ACE6 JR C,$ACEF
ACE8 LD ($ACB1),DE intermediate variable for calculating the distance from the cursor to the nearest planet
ACEC CALL $AE81 save the working area with galaxy data
ACEF CALL $AD17 modify data for planet generation
ACF2 POP BC
ACF3 DJNZ $ACD2
ACF5 LD HL,($ACB1) intermediate variable for calculating the distance from the cursor to the nearest planet
ACF8 AND A
ACF9 CALL $AD45 calculate fuel to reach the planet
ACFC CALL $AE89 restore the working area with galaxy data
ACFF CALL $ADDF Build planet data (economy, government, tech level,
AD02 CALL $AD6F generate the planet name and prepare data for generating the next name
AD05 CALL $ADD3 Copy the planet name into the working area
This entry point is used by the routine at AC77.
AD08 LD A,($A85B) calculate fuel consumption for the jump to the planet
AD0B LD B,A
AD0C LD A,($A859) get Pilot_CrdYSystem
AD0F CALL $AD21 squared distance between the cursor and the planet
AD12 CALL $AD45 calculate fuel to reach the planet
AD15 AND A
AD16 RET

AD1D POP BC

AD20 RET
AD21: CalcDistanceToCursor
Used by the routine at ACC7.
Squared distance between the cursor and the planet
In: b - planet's Y coordinate
a - planet's X coordinate
Out: hl - result
cy=1 - sum greater than $FFFF
de,bc,a - ????
AD21 PUSH AF
AD22 LD A,($A87D) get Pilot_CrdXTarget
AD25 CALL $AD3D
AD28 LD D,A
AD29 CALL $AD5C multiplication hl=d*a
AD2C POP BC
AD2D PUSH HL
AD2E LD A,($A87E) get Pilot_CrdYTarget
AD31 CALL $AD3D
AD34 SRL A
AD36 LD D,A
AD37 CALL $AD5C multiplication hl=d*a
AD3A POP DE
AD3B ADD HL,DE
AD3C RET
AD3D CP B
AD3E JR NC,$AD43
AD40 LD C,B
AD41 LD B,A
AD42 LD A,C
AD43 SUB B
AD44 RET
AD45: CalcFuelForDistance
Used by the routine at ACC7.
Calculate fuel to reach the planet
In: hl - squared distance from the planet to the cursor
cy=1 - hl > $FFFF
Out: hl,(FuelToPlanet) - fuel needed for the jump
AD45 JR C,$AD49
AD47 JR $AD4C
AD49 LD HL,$FFFF
AD4C LD A,H
AD4D OR L
AD4E JR Z,$AD58
AD50 CALL $DC53 extract the square root h=sqr(hl)
AD53 LD L,H
AD54 LD H,$00
AD56 ADD HL,HL
AD57 ADD HL,HL
AD58 LD ($AC49),HL fuel required to travel to the planet =distance*4
AD5B RET
AD5C: Mult_HL_D_A
Used by the routines at AD21 and ADDF.
Multiplication hl=d*a
In: d,a - factors
Out: hl - result
AD5C LD E,$00
AD5E LD B,$08
AD60 LD HL,$0000
AD63 SRL D
AD65 RR E
AD67 RL A
AD69 JR NC,$AD6C
AD6B ADD HL,DE
AD6C DJNZ $AD63
AD6E RET

ADDF: GenerateInfoPlanet
Used by the routines at AC77 and ACC7.
Build planet data (economy, government, tech level,
population, gross income) and set the small-cursor coordinates
ADDF LD HL,($AC15) data for generating a planet
ADE2 LD ($AC0F),HL 6 bytes for generating planet data
ADE5 LD A,H
ADE6 LD ($A87E),A set Pilot_CrdYTarget
ADE9 AND $07
ADEB LD ($AC4B),A Economy
ADEE LD HL,($AC17)
ADF1 LD ($AC11),HL
ADF4 LD A,H
ADF5 LD ($A87D),A set Pilot_CrdXTarget
ADF8 LD A,L
ADF9 SRL A
ADFB SRL A
ADFD SRL A
ADFF AND $07
AE01 LD ($AC4C),A government (government type)
AE04 SRL A
AE06 JR NZ,$AE10
AE08 LD A,($AC4B) Economy
AE0B OR $02
AE0D LD ($AC4B),A Economy
AE10 LD A,($AC4B) Economy
AE13 XOR $07
AE15 AND A
AE16 LD B,A
AE17 LD A,H
AE18 AND $03
AE1A ADD A,B
AE1B LD B,A
AE1C LD A,($AC4C) government (government type)
AE1F SRL A
AE21 ADC A,B
AE22 LD ($AC4D),A Tech/Level (tech level)
AE25 ADD A,A
AE26 ADD A,A
AE27 LD B,A
AE28 LD A,($AC4C) government (government type)
AE2B LD C,A
AE2C LD A,($AC4B) Economy
AE2F ADC A,B
AE30 ADC A,C
AE31 ADC A,$01
AE33 LD ($AC4E),A population n/10 of the form x.x
AE36 LD A,($AC4B) Economy
AE39 XOR $07
AE3B ADC A,$03
AE3D LD D,A
AE3E LD A,($AC4C) government (government type)
AE41 ADC A,$04
AE43 CALL $AD5C multiplication hl=d*a
AE46 LD A,($AC4E) population n/10 of the form x.x
AE49 LD D,L
AE4A CALL $AD5C multiplication hl=d*a
AE4D ADD HL,HL
AE4E ADD HL,HL
AE4F ADD HL,HL
AE50 LD ($AC4F),HL Gross productivity (gross income)
AE53 LD HL,($AC19)
AE56 LD ($AC13),HL
AE59 RET

AE95: Data block at AE95
SaveA6 AE95 DEFB $00
AE96: GenerateProductPrice
Used by the routine at D6B3.
Generate quantities and prices of goods at the station
In: ----
Called on arrival after every hyperspace jump, so the market re-rolls once per jump and then stays fixed while docked. Per good, with economy = PlanetEconomy (0-7) and rnd = PilotRnd: gradient = (economyFactor & $1F) * economy; quantity = (baseQty + (rnd & mask)) +/- gradient (sign from bit 7 of the economy factor byte), clamped to 0 and masked to 0-63; price = (basePrice + (rnd & mask)) +/- gradient with the OPPOSITE sign convention from quantity, then multiplied by 4 and stored in 0.1 Cr units (so an internal price of $13 displays as 19*4/10 = 7.6 Cr). The same single random byte seasons every good on the station -- goods differ only via their own mask/base values/economy gradient.
GenerateAnotherPrice is an alternate entry point that skips drawing a fresh random byte and reuses the saved PilotRnd instead, used by InitGame so a loaded commander rebuilds the exact same market it had when saved.
AE96 LD A,($7063) get RndWord
AE99 LD ($A82C),A commander data offset +23 (PilotRnd)
This entry point is used by the routine at A976.
GenerateAnotherPrice AE9C LD HL,$A860 the same but without setting a new rnd
AE9F LD DE,$AF0E goods prices at the station, in 0.1 Cr units
AEA2 EXX
AEA3 LD HL,$91F2 table for generating goods quantities and prices at the station: 17 x 4-byte templates in TableBasePrice (byte 0 = base price, byte 1 = economy factor: bits 0-4 gradient multiplier, bit 7 gradient sign, byte 2 = base quantity, byte 3 = random mask)
AEA6 LD B,$11
AEA8 PUSH BC
AEA9 LD E,(HL)
AEAA INC HL
AEAB LD A,(HL)
AEAC LD D,A
AEAD AND $1F
AEAF JR Z,$AEBA
AEB1 LD B,A
AEB2 LD A,($AC4B) Economy
AEB5 LD C,A
AEB6 XOR A
AEB7 ADD A,C
AEB8 DJNZ $AEB7
AEBA LD ($AE95),A
AEBD INC HL
AEBE LD B,(HL)
AEBF INC HL
AEC0 LD C,(HL)
AEC1 INC HL
AEC2 LD A,($A82C) commander data offset +23 (PilotRnd)
AEC5 AND C
AEC6 ADD A,B
AEC7 LD B,A
AEC8 LD A,($AE95)
AECB BIT 7,D
AECD JR NZ,$AED2
AECF INC A
AED0 NEG
AED2 ADD A,B
AED3 BIT 7,A
AED5 JR Z,$AED8
AED7 XOR A
AED8 AND $3F '?'
AEDA PUSH AF
AEDB LD A,($A82C) commander data offset +23 (PilotRnd)
AEDE AND C
AEDF ADD A,E
AEE0 LD B,A
AEE1 LD A,($AE95)
AEE4 BIT 7,D
AEE6 JR Z,$AEEB
AEE8 INC A
AEE9 NEG
AEEB ADD A,B
AEEC LD C,A
AEED LD B,$00
AEEF SLA C
AEF1 RL B
AEF3 SLA C
AEF5 RL B
AEF7 PUSH BC
AEF8 EXX
AEF9 POP BC
AEFA LD A,C
AEFB LD (DE),A
AEFC INC DE
AEFD LD A,B
AEFE LD (DE),A
AEFF INC DE
AF00 POP AF
AF01 LD (HL),A
AF02 INC HL
AF03 EXX
AF04 POP BC
AF05 DJNZ $AEA8
AF07 LD HL,$A870 +91 =0 the station was destroyed as part of the mission
AF0A LD (HL),B
AF0B RET
AF0C: Data block at AF0C
AF0C DEFB $00,$00
ProductPrice AF0E DEFB $BA,$3E,$20,$C3,$07,$BA,$CD,$07
AF16 DEFB $BA,$3E,$01,$32,$A7,$A7,$CD,$25
AF1E DEFB $AF,$3A,$23,$A7,$3C,$18,$11,$AF
AF26 DEFB $32,$22,$A7,$C9,$E1,$7E,$23,$E5
AF2E DEFB $32,$22
CurrentGoodIndex AF30 DEFB $00 good number
AdrPlayerHold AF31 DEFW $0000 address in the player's hold table (PilotCargo); starts one entry before the table and is pre-incremented by 1 per row
AdrStationPrice AF33 DEFW $0000 address in the station price table (ProductPrice); starts one entry before the table and is pre-incremented by 2 per row
AdrStationStock AF35 DEFW $0000 address in the station stock table (PilotPlanetProduct); starts one entry before the table and is pre-incremented by 1 per row
TempCrdPrn AF37 DEFW $0000 print coordinates

AF3E RET Z

AF55 RET

AF57 LD HL,$FC0E 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device 6,=1 - 0 -- ??? 5,=1 - I -- Global Map 4,=1 - O -- Local Map 3,=1 - P -- Planet Info 2,=1 - K -- Planet Price 1,=1 - L -- Commander Info 0,=1 - Enter -- Inventory

AF5E INC HL
AF5F DJNZ $AF5C
AF61 EI
AF62 RET
AF63: PrnOneProduct
Used by the routines at B2CE and D07D.
One Market Prices / Buy row: name, unit, price (4-digit nnn.n field), then stock as nn+unit, or a literal - when the stock byte is 0 (returns carry set = not traded here). The good's name is printed via message code $D0+index. The 17 goods, in index order: $00 Food(t) $01 Textiles(t) $02 Radioactives(t) $03 Slaves(t) $04 Liquor & Wines(t) $05 Luxuries(t) $06 Narcotics(t) $07 Computers(t) $08 Machinery(t) $09 Alloys(t) $0A Firearms(t) $0B Furs(t) $0C Minerals(t) $0D Gold(kg) $0E Platinum(kg) $0F Gem-Stones(g) $10 Alien Items(t).
Print the name, price and quantity of a good
In: (LLAF30) - good number
(LLAF33) - address in the goods price table
(LLAF35) - address in the goods quantity table
Out: cy=1 - no such good
hl,de,bc,a - ????
AF63 LD A,($AF30) good number
AF66 ADD A,$D0
AF68 CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
AF6B CALL $B00D set the X print coordinate

AF6E DEFB $0D
AF6F: Routine at AF6F
AF6F CALL $B06F print in lowercase t/kg/g/km

AF75 DEFB $0F

AF76 LD HL,($AF33) address in the goods price table
AF79 INC HL
AF7A INC HL
AF7B LD ($AF33),HL address in the goods price table
AF7E LD E,(HL)
AF7F INC HL
AF80 LD D,(HL)
AF81 EX DE,HL
AF82 SCF
AF83 LD A,$04
AF85 LD ($A88E),A ; number of digits to print for the number (padded on the left with spaces or zeros)
AF88 CALL $B760 print a number
AF8B CALL $B00D set the X print coordinate
AF8E: Data block at AF8E
AF8E DEFB $17
AF8F: Routine at AF8F
AF8F LD HL,($AF35) address in the goods quantity table
AF92 INC HL
AF93 LD A,(HL)
AF94 LD ($AF35),HL address in the goods quantity table
AF97 AND A
AF98 JR NZ,$AFA5
AF9A CALL $BA99 print one character (print a message by number

AF9E LD A,$2D '-'

AFA4 RET

Out: cy=1
a=$00

hl,de,bc - ????

B06F: GetProductUnitSuffix
Used by the routines at AF6F, AF9E, AFD0, B45E and BA20.
Chooses the unit suffix for a good purely from its index: $00-$0C and $10 print t, $0D-$0E print kg, $0F prints g.
Print in lowercase t/kg/g/km
In: (LLAF30) - message number
$00-$0C,$10 - t
$0D,$0E - kg
$0F - g
$11-$FF - km
Out: hl,de,bc,a
B06F LD A,$00
B071 LD ($A892),A set FlgPrnSym2
B074 LD A,($AF30) good number
B077 CP $0D
B079 JR C,$B087
B07B CP $0F
B07D JR C,$B08B
B07F JR Z,$B090
B081 CP $11
B083 JR C,$B087
B085 JR $B094
B087 LD A,$54 'T'
B089 JR $B09A
B08B LD A,$4B 'K'
B08D CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
B090 LD A,$47 'G'
B092 JR $B09A
B094 CALL $BA99 print one character (print a message by number
B097: Data block at B097
B097 DEFB $4B
B098: Routine at B098
B098 LD A,$4D 'M'
This entry point is used by the routine at B06F.
B09A JP $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
B09D: IsCargoBayGood
Used by the routines at B3A1 and B501.
Checks whether a good occupies cargo-bay space (only tonne-unit goods do; Gold/Platinum/Gem-Stones are kg/g and don't count against PilotFreeCargo).
B09D LD A,($AF30) good number
B0A0 CP $0D
B0A2 RET C
B0A3 CP $10
B0A5 JR NZ,$B0A9
B0A7 SCF
B0A8 RET
B0A9 AND A
B0AA RET

Pause of the 14h interrupt

B0AD JR $B0B1

B27B: Cmd_2_BuyCargo
Used by the routine at A77B.
While mission 1 is active, this screen is replaced by a special mission pitch (deliver contraband): accepting it (Y) wipes the hold, grants the Large Cargo Bay, and sets a "mission cargo aboard" flag in PilotExtWeapon bit 5, which subsequently locks the player out of the normal Buy screen until the mission resolves.
Goods purchase menu
B27B LD A,($A872) +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed

B280 JR NZ,$B2BF
B282 LD A,($A843) get PilotMission1
B285 AND A
B286 JR Z,$B2C7

B333: EnterQuantity
Used by the routines at B2CE, B4B7 and B5BB.
The shared quantity-entry engine behind every "how many?" prompt in the game (Buy Cargo, Sell Cargo, Equip Ship). In: hl = a validation/transaction callback address (stored in word_B472). Out: carry clear and byte_B471 = the entered 1-2 digit number, or carry set = aborted (a=2 means Enter was pressed with nothing typed).
Flow: waits for a first digit, stores it, echoes it, then calls the callback immediately -- in this game the callback performs the actual transaction (cash deduction, stock/hold update), so typing 1 executes a purchase of 1 on the spot. Waits for a second key: Enter accepts the 1-digit value; another digit computes byte_B471 = old*10+digit, calls RollbackTradeState to undo the first-digit transaction, then calls the callback again with the 2-digit total; backspace ($FE) also rolls back and restarts entry from the first digit.
B333 LD ($B472),HL
This entry point is used by the routine at B324.
B336 XOR A
B337 LD ($B471),A
B33A CALL $AF48 wait for a keypress
B33D CP $0D
B33F JR Z,$B391
B341 CP $31 '1'
B343 JR C,$B33A
B345 CP $FE
B347 JR Z,$B33A
B349 CP $3A ':'
B34B JR NC,$B393
B34D LD B,A
B34E SUB $30 '0'
B350 LD ($B471),A
B353 LD A,B
B354 CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
B357 LD HL,($B472)
B35A CALL $B3A0
B35D JR C,$B393
B35F CALL $AF48 wait for a keypress
B362 CP $0D
B364 RET Z
B365 CP $30 '0'
B367 JR C,$B35F
B369 CP $FE
B36B JR Z,$B32B
B36D CP $3A ':'
B36F JR NC,$B393
B371 LD D,A
B372 SUB $30 '0'
B374 LD B,A
B375 LD HL,$B471
B378 LD A,(HL)
B379 ADD A,A
B37A LD C,A
B37B ADD A,A
B37C ADD A,A
B37D ADD A,C
B37E ADD A,B
B37F LD (HL),A
B380 LD A,D
B381 CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
B384 CALL $B423
B387 LD HL,($B472)
B38A CALL $B3A0
B38D JR C,$B393
B38F AND A
B390 RET
B391 LD A,$02
B393 SCF
B394 RET

Used by the routine at B333.
B3A0 JP (HL)
B3A1: BuyGoodCallback
Transaction callback for the Buy screen, called by EnterQuantity after every digit. Snapshots state, then: stock -= qty (fail $B0 = quantity unavailable), pays qty x price via DecCASH in a loop (fail $C5 = insufficient cash), checks cargo-bay space via IsCargoBayGood and debits PilotFreeCargo if needed (fail $CE = no room), then PilotCargo[good] += qty (8-bit overflow also fails $CE), and finally calls SetLegalStatCargo. Any failure jumps to loc_B3E8 which prints the message and rolls the whole attempt back.
B3A1 CALL $B402
B3A4 LD A,($B471)
B3A7 LD B,A
B3A8 LD HL,($AF35) address in the goods quantity table
B3AB LD A,(HL)
B3AC SUB B
B3AD JR C,$B3DE
B3AF LD (HL),A
B3B0 LD HL,($AF33) address in the goods price table
B3B3 LD E,(HL)
B3B4 INC HL
B3B5 LD D,(HL)
B3B6 CALL $B877 decrease money (from 0 to 999.9)
B3B9 JR C,$B3E2
B3BB DJNZ $B3B0
B3BD CALL $B09D
B3C0 LD A,($B471)
B3C3 LD B,A
B3C4 JR NC,$B3CE
B3C6 LD HL,$A83E remaining ship cargo capacity
B3C9 LD A,(HL)
B3CA SUB B
B3CB JR C,$B3E6
B3CD LD (HL),A
B3CE LD HL,($AF31) address in the goods quantity table
B3D1 LD A,(HL)
B3D2 ADD A,B
B3D3 JR C,$B3E6
B3D5 LD (HL),A
B3D6 LD A,($AF30) good number
B3D9 CALL SetLegalStatCargo duplicate this routine
B3DC AND A
B3DD RET
This entry point is used by the routine at B501.
B3DE LD A,$B0
B3E0 JR $B3E8
B3E2: TradeFailHandler
Used by the routines at B3A1 and B702.
B3E2 LD A,$C5
B3E4 JR $B3E8
This entry point is used by the routine at B3A1.
B3E6 LD A,$CE
Shared fail handler for both buy and sell: prints the message code left in A at print-column 3 followed by '?', then calls RollbackTradeState to undo the attempted transaction.
