B3E9 CALL $B00D set the X print coordinate
B3EC INC BC
B3ED POP AF
B3EE CALL $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
B3F1 CALL $BA99 print one character (print a message by number

B3F4 DEFB $3F

B3F8: BeepPause24

Out: cy=1
a=$01

B400 SCF
B401 RET
B402: SnapshotTradeState
Used by the routines at B3A1, B501 and B5BB.
Saves PilotCash1, PilotCash2, the current good's stock byte, its hold byte, and PilotFreeCargo into word_B46A/word_B46C/byte_B46E/byte_B46F/byte_B470. Called first by every buy/sell transaction callback, so a later digit (or an abort) can cleanly undo a transaction already committed by a previous digit.
B402 LD HL,($A828) get PilotCash2
B405 LD ($B46C),HL
B408 LD HL,($A82A) get PilotCash1
B40B LD ($B46A),HL
B40E LD HL,($AF35) address in the goods quantity table
B411 LD A,(HL)
B412 LD ($B46F),A
B415 LD HL,($AF31) address in the goods quantity table
B418 LD A,(HL)
B419 LD ($B46E),A
B41C LD A,($A83E) get PilotFreeCargo
B41F LD ($B470),A
B422 RET
B423: RollbackTradeState
Used by the routines at B324, B333, B3F5, B6BB and B71E.
Restores the five fields saved by SnapshotTradeState. Called by EnterQuantity between digits and on backspace, and by every transaction failure path.
B423 LD HL,($B46C)
B426 LD ($A828),HL set PilotCash2
B429 LD HL,($B46A)
B42C LD ($A82A),HL set PilotCash1
B42F LD HL,($AF35) address in the goods quantity table
B432 LD A,($B46F)
B435 LD (HL),A
B436 LD HL,($AF31) address in the goods quantity table
B439 LD A,($B46E)
B43C LD (HL),A
B43D LD A,($B470)
B440 LD ($A83E),A set PilotFreeCargo
B443 RET

B4B0: SellGoodLoop
Used by the routine at B474.
Drives the Sell screen: loops over all 17 goods via PrnInventoryRow (which silently skips goods the player doesn't hold); for each held good, saves the print cursor, shows the quantity prompt, and runs EnterQuantity with SellGoodCallback. After the loop: two pauses, then tail-jumps to the Inventory screen.
B4B0 CALL $B49F
B4B3 CALL $BA99 print one character (print a message by number
B4B6: Data block at B4B6
B4B6 DEFB $CD
B4B7: PrnSellGoodHeader
B4B7 LD A,$CE
B4B9 CALL $B057 Print a message, the danger indicator (if in space), and 3 CrLf

B4C2 LD HL,($AF33) address in the goods price table
B4C5 INC HL
B4C6 INC HL
B4C7 LD ($AF33),HL address in the goods price table
B4CA CALL $AFB9 print the name and quantity of a good

B4CF CALL $B0B5 save print coordinates

B4D5 LD HL,$B501

B4DB JR C,$B4F8

B501: SellGoodCallback
Transaction callback for the Sell screen. Snapshots state, then: PilotCargo[good] -= qty (fail $B0 = not enough held), frees cargo-bay space via IsCargoBayGood if this is a tonne-unit good, and pays out qty x station price via IncCASH in a loop. Unlike buying: does NOT add the sold quantity back to the station's stock (PilotPlanetProduct is untouched, so the station won't resell what you dumped on it), and has no legal -status consequence -- only acquiring contraband is a crime, not offloading it.
B501 CALL $B402
B504 LD A,($B471)
B507 LD B,A
B508 LD HL,($AF31) address in the goods quantity table
B50B LD A,(HL)
B50C SUB B
B50D JP C,$B3DE
B510 LD (HL),A
B511 PUSH BC
B512 CALL $B09D
B515 POP BC
B516 JR NC,$B51F
B518 LD A,($A83E) get PilotFreeCargo
B51B ADD A,B
B51C LD ($A83E),A set PilotFreeCargo
B51F PUSH BC
B520 LD HL,($AF33) address in the goods price table
B523 LD E,(HL)
B524 INC HL
B525 LD D,(HL)
B526 CALL $B84D add money
B529 POP BC
B52A DJNZ $B51F
B52C AND A
B52D RET
B52E: ResetTradeWalkPointers
Used by the routines at B2CE and B4B7.
Resets the four walking pointers shared by all trade screens (byte_AF30 = 0, and word_AF31/AF33/AF35 each set one entry before their respective table so the same "advance, then use" loop body serves every row including the first).
B52E LD HL,$A82C commander data offset +23 (PilotRnd)
B531 LD ($AF31),HL address in the goods quantity table
B534 LD HL,$AF0C
B537 LD ($AF33),HL address in the goods price table
B53A XOR A
B53B LD ($AF30),A good number
B53E RET

B84D: IncCASH
Used by the routines at A900, B501 and B6BB.
Trading code never computes price x quantity as a single multiplication -- it calls this once per unit inside a djnz loop instead, sidestepping 16-bit overflow entirely (the priciest good at maximum quantity would overflow a 16-bit product) at the cost of a few hundred cycles nobody notices.
Add money
In: de - amount of money (from 0.1 to 6553.5)
Out: hl,de - undefined
B84D LD HL,($A82A) get PilotCash1
B850 ADD HL,DE
B851 LD ($A82A),HL set PilotCash1
B854 AND A
B855 LD DE,$2710
B858 SBC HL,DE
B85A RET C
B85B LD ($A82A),HL set PilotCash1
B85E LD DE,$0001
Trading code never computes price x quantity as a single multiplication -- it calls this once per unit inside a djnz loop instead, sidestepping 16-bit overflow entirely (the priciest good at maximum quantity would overflow a 16-bit product) at the cost of a few hundred cycles nobody notices.
IncCASH_1K B861 LD HL,($A828) get PilotCash2
B864 ADD HL,DE
B865 LD ($A828),HL set PilotCash2
B868 RET
B869: DecCASH_1000
Used by the routine at B5BB.
Trading code never computes price x quantity as a single multiplication -- it calls this once per unit inside a djnz loop instead, sidestepping 16-bit overflow entirely (the priciest good at maximum quantity would overflow a 16-bit product) at the cost of a few hundred cycles nobody notices.
Decrease money (whole thousands)
In: de - amount of money (from 1000 to 65535000)
Out: cy=1 - not enough money
hl,de - ????
B869 LD HL,($A828) get PilotCash2
B86C SBC HL,DE
B86E JR C,$B875
B870 LD ($A828),HL set PilotCash2
B873 AND A
B874 RET
B875 SCF
B876 RET
B877: DecCASH
Used by the routines at B3A1 and B5BB.
Trading code never computes price x quantity as a single multiplication -- it calls this once per unit inside a djnz loop instead, sidestepping 16-bit overflow entirely (the priciest good at maximum quantity would overflow a 16-bit product) at the cost of a few hundred cycles nobody notices.
Decrease money (from 0 to 999.9)
In: de - amount of money (from 0.1 to 6553.5)
Out: cy=1 - not enough money
hl,de,a - ????
B877 LD HL,($A82A) get PilotCash1
B87A AND A
B87B SBC HL,DE
B87D JR NC,$B895
B87F LD HL,($A828) get PilotCash2
B882 LD A,L
B883 OR H
B884 SCF
B885 RET Z

B887 LD ($A828),HL set PilotCash2

B88D AND A
B88E SBC HL,DE
B890 LD DE,($A82A) get PilotCash1
B894 ADD HL,DE
B895 LD ($A82A),HL set PilotCash1
B898 AND A
B899 RET

BBDF: SetAttrFrom_DE
Used by the routines at B2CE, B49F, B991, D07D, D0B9, D279 and D2E4.
Set screen attributes
de - address of the row with the setup mechanics
bit 7-3 - number of character cells horizontally, attribute =5
bit 2-1 - number of line feeds without setting attributes
=0 print two attributes =7 at 5822 and 5823, then exit

BBE2 LD A,(DE)

BBE7 RRCA

BBFB LD L,A

BBFE INC H
BBFF DJNZ $BBF8

BC03 LD HL,$5822
BC06 LD A,$07
BC08 LD (HL),A
BC09 INC HL
BC0A LD (HL),A
BC0B RET

BD17 CP B
BD18 JP Z,$BD5D print the built string
BD1B CALL $BDC6 find the first space from the end of the line

BD1F LD ($BCF8),A
BD22 AND A
BD23 JR Z,$BD52
BD25 LD HL,($BCF6)
BD28 INC HL
BD29 LD C,B
BD2A LD B,$00
BD2C LD DE,$7023
BD2F LDIR
BD31 LD HL,$7003
BD34 LD A,($BCF8)
BD37 LD E,A
BD38 LD D,$00
BD3A ADD HL,DE
BD3B LD ($BCFA),HL
BD3E LD A,($BCF8)
BD41 CP $1F
BD43 JP NC,$BD5D print the built string
BD46 LD HL,($BCF6)
BD49 LD B,A
BD4A LD (HL),$00
BD4C INC HL
BD4D DJNZ $BD4A
BD4F LD A,($BCF8)
BD52 PUSH AF
BD53 CALL $BDC6 find the first space from the end of the line
BD56 CALL $D000 search for a zero starting from address (AdrInMsgDescr2)
BD59 POP AF
BD5A DEC A
BD5B JR NZ,$BD52
BD5D: PrintMsgDescr
Used by the routines at BCFC and BDB9.
Print the built string
In: ---
Out: bc =$0020
hl,de,a - undefined
BD5D LD HL,$7004
BD60 LD B,$1E
BD62 PUSH BC
BD63 PUSH HL
BD64 LD A,(HL)
BD65 CALL $BB58 print one character
BD68 POP HL
BD69 INC HL
BD6A POP BC
BD6B DJNZ $BD62
BD6D CALL $AFFA set the print-register flag, reset X coordinate, increment Y coordinate
BD70 LD A,$00
BD72 LD ($A892),A set FlgPrnSym2
This entry point is used by the routine at BD9D.
BD75 LD HL,($BCFA)
BD78 LD ($BCF6),HL
BD7B LD HL,$7003
BD7E LD ($BCFA),HL
BD81 LD A,($BCF8)
BD84 LD ($BCF9),A
BD87 XOR A
BD88 LD ($BCF8),A
BD8B LD B,$1E
BD8D LD C,$20 ' '
BD8F LD HL,$7023
BD92 LD DE,$7004
BD95 LD A,(HL)
BD96 LD (DE),A
BD97 LD (HL),C
BD98 INC HL
BD99 INC DE
BD9A DJNZ $BD95
BD9C RET
BD9D: ClearDescrMsg
Clear the description lines and set variables
In: ---
Out: bc =$0020
hl,de,a - undefined
BD9D LD A,$01
BD9F LD ($A894),A set FlgPrnDescr
BDA2 LD B,$1F
BDA4 LD A,$20 ' '
BDA6 LD HL,$7023
BDA9 LD (HL),A
BDAA INC HL
BDAB DJNZ $BDA9
BDAD LD HL,$7003
BDB0 LD ($BCFA),HL
BDB3 XOR A
BDB4 LD ($BCF8),A
BDB7 JR $BD75
BDB9: CloseDescrMsg
Finish printing the planet description
In: ---
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
BDB9 LD A,$00
BDBB LD ($A894),A set FlgPrnDescr
BDBE LD A,($BCF9)
BDC1 AND A
BDC2 CALL NZ,$BD5D print the built string
BDC5 RET
BDC6: FindLastSpace
Used by the routine at BCFC.
Find the first space from the end of the line
In: (AdrInMsgDescr2) - address of the last character in the line
Out: b - number of characters from the end of the line to the space, including the space
(AdrInMsgDescr2) - address of the last space in the line
BDC6 LD HL,($BCF6)
BDC9 LD B,$00
BDCB DEC HL
BDCC LD A,(HL)
BDCD INC B
BDCE CP $20 ' '
BDD0 JR NZ,$BDCB
BDD2 LD ($BCF6),HL
BDD5 LD DE,$7004
BDD8 AND A
BDD9 SBC HL,DE
BDDB RET NC
BDDC LD HL,$7022
BDDF LD ($BCF6),HL
BDE2 JR $BDC6 find the first space from the end of the line
BDE4: ECM_On
Used by the routine at ED3F.
Turn on the ECM system, if available
BDE4 LD A,C
BDE5 AND A
BDE6 RET Z
BDE7 BIT 4,B
BDE9 RET Z
BDEA OR $80
BDEC LD ($A847),A set PilotECMSystem
BDEF LD A,$0A
BDF1 LD ($DA16),A set TimeWorkECM
BDF4 RET
BDF5: Unused

D000: FindZeroByte
Used by the routine at BCFC.
Search for a zero starting from address (AdrInMsgDescr2)
D000 LD B,$20 ' '
D002 LD HL,($BCF6)
D005 LD A,(HL)
D006 LD C,A
D007 LD (HL),B
D008 LD B,C
D009 INC HL
D00A XOR A
D00B CP C
D00C JR NZ,$D005
D00E RET
D00F: PrintTypePopulation2
Used by the routine at BA8A.
Used only in Cmd_P_PlanetInfo
D00F LD A,H
D010 SRL A
D012 SRL A
D014 PUSH AF
D015 AND $07
D017 CP $03
D019 JR NC,$D020
D01B ADD A,$E3
D01D CALL $AFEF Message: LARGE
D020 POP AF
D021 SRL A
D023 SRL A
D025 SRL A
D027 CP $06
D029 JR NC,$D030
D02B ADD A,$E6
D02D CALL $AFEF Message: GREEN
D030 LD HL,($AC0F) 6 bytes for generating planet data
D033 LD A,H
D034 LD B,A
D035 LD HL,($AC11)
D038 LD A,H
D039 XOR B
D03A AND $07
D03C PUSH AF
D03D CP $06
D03F JR NC,$D046
D041 ADD A,$EC
D043 CALL $AFEF Message: SLIMY
D046 LD HL,($AC13)
D049 LD A,H
D04A AND $03
D04C LD B,A
D04D POP AF
D04E ADD A,B
D04F AND $07
D051 ADD A,$F2 one of: RODENT FROG LIZARD LOBSTER BIRD HUMANOID FELINE INSECT
This entry point is used by the routine at BA8A.
D053 JP $BA9D print a message from group 1 in the buffer/on screen
D056: PrintRadiusPlanet
Used by the routine at BA0C.
Print the planet's radius
D056 LD HL,($AC13)
D059 LD A,H
D05A AND $0F
D05C ADD A,$0B
D05E LD HL,($AC11)
D061 LD L,H
D062 LD H,A
D063 LD A,$05
D065 LD ($A88E),A ; number of digits to print for the number (padded on the left with spaces or zeros)
D068 AND A
D069 JP $B760 print a number
D06C: CompareGalaxyData
Used by the routines at BA20 and D6B3.
Compare galaxy data with HL
In: hl - address of the data to compare
Out: cy =1 not equal
cy =0 identical
D06C LD DE,$A852 galaxy data
D06F LD B,$06
D071 LD A,(DE)
D072 CP (HL)
D073 JR NZ,$D07B
D075 INC DE
D076 INC HL
D077 DJNZ $D071
D079 AND A
D07A RET
D07B SCF
D07C RET
D07D: Cmd_K_MarketPrice
Used by the routine at A77B.
Print the list of goods on the planet
D07D CALL $B4AB Print the menu frame
D080 LD A,$A7
D082 CALL $B057 print the menu title
D085 LD HL,$0310
D088 LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
D08B LD A,$FF
D08D CALL $AFF7 print a message, with LF/CR at the end
D090 LD DE,$FC0B 27 attributes +2 line feeds
D093 CALL $BBDF Set screen attributes
D096 LD A,($7061) get InFreeSpace
D099 AND A
D09A RET NZ
D09B XOR A
D09C LD ($AF30),A good number
D09F LD HL,$AF0C
D0A2 LD ($AF33),HL address in the goods price table
D0A5 LD HL,$A85F 74 ???
D0A8 LD ($AF35),HL address in the goods quantity table
D0AB CALL $AF63 print the name, price and quantity of a good
D0AE LD HL,$AF30 good number
D0B1 INC (HL)
D0B2 LD A,(HL)
D0B3 CP $11
D0B5 JR C,$D0AB
D0B7 RET
D0B8: Data block at D0B8
D0B8 DEFB $00
D0B9: Cmd_L_PilotInfo
Used by the routines at 716B, 7287, A77B, B5BB, B71E and D574.
Print info for key 'L'
D0B9 LD A,$0B
D0BB LD ($7041),A set WhatOnScreen
D0BE LD A,$96
D0C0 CALL $BAD7 print a message from group 2 in the buffer/on screen
D0C3 CALL $A4BD print the danger indicator and the missile indicator
D0C6 LD DE,$FB9E
D0C9 CALL $BBDF Set screen attributes
D0CC CALL $B00D set the X print coordinate
D0CF: Data block at D0CF

D563: ResetBattleMessages
Used by the routines at 9A46, A976 and D5F5.
Clear all messages during flight
In: ---
Out: a =$00
b =$00
hl,MissileJammed+9
D563 LD B,$09
D565 LD HL,$A87F 7,=1 missile launch failed 6-0,= Y print coordinate of the message
D568 RES 7,(HL)
D56A INC HL
D56B INC HL
D56C DJNZ $D568
D56E XOR A
D56F LD ($D448),A =FD something has already been destroyed (message number) (some cargo)
D572 RET
D573: Data block at D573
JumpCountdown D573 DEFB $00 countdown to the jump =$00 no countdown yet
D574: GoJump
Used by the routine at 7287.
Handle hyperjump keys G, H
D574 LD HL,$FC10 7,=1 - CapsS -- Pause 6,=1 - J -- Jump Drive 5,=1 - G -- Galactic Hyper Drive 4,=1 - H -- Hyper Drive 3,=1 - 4 -- Right View/Equip Ship 2,=1 - 3 -- Left View/Sell Cargo 1,=1 - 2 -- Rear View/Buy Cargo 0,=1 - 1 -- Front View/Launch
D577 LD A,(HL)
D578 AND $30
D57A RET Z
D57B LD B,A
D57C LD A,(HL)
D57D AND $CF
D57F LD (HL),A
D580 LD A,$01
D582 LD ($7C39),A =$01 G or H pressed (jump)
D585 LD A,($D573) get JumpCountdown
D588 AND A
D589 RET NZ
D58A LD A,($7062) get IndDanger
D58D AND A
D58E JR Z,$D5D6 docked, jump not possible
D590 BIT 5,B
D592 JR Z,$D5A6 hyperjump
D594 BIT 4,B
D596 RET Z
D597 LD A,($A84F) 58 hyperdrive
D59A AND A
D59B RET Z no hyperdrive
D59C LD A,$02
D59E LD ($A80A),A set JumpDocking
D5A1 LD BC,$7406
D5A4 JR $D5C2
D5A6 CALL $ACB3 hyperjump

D5AC LD A,H
D5AD AND A
D5AE JR NZ,$D5D0 not enough fuel for the jump
D5B0 LD A,L
D5B1 AND A
D5B2 RET Z
D5B3 LD B,A
D5B4 LD A,($A844) get Pilot_Fuel
D5B7 SUB B
D5B8 JR C,$D5D0 not enough fuel for the jump
D5BA LD A,$01
D5BC LD ($A80A),A set JumpDocking
D5BF LD BC,$C005 coordinates to print the countdown digits
D5C2 LD A,$15
D5C4 LD ($D573),A set JumpCountdown
D5C7 LD ($D445),A lifetime (number of print frames) of an in-flight message =$00 nothing needs to be printed
D5CA CALL $D4B8
D5CD JP $AF39 sound beep
D5D0 LD BC,$C106 not enough fuel for the jump
D5D3 JP $D4AE
D5D6 CALL $BC9E docked, jump not possible
D5D9 LD HL,$070B
D5DC LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
D5DF LD A,$01
D5E1 LD ($A891),A set FlgPrnSym1
D5E4 LD A,$CD
D5E6 CALL $BAD7 print a message from group 2 in the buffer/on screen
D5E9 CALL $AF39 sound beep
D5EC CALL $B0AF Pause of the 24h interrupt
D5EF CALL $AF56 clear the pressed-key bits
D5F2 JP $D0B9 print info for key 'L'
D5F5: PrnCountdownJump
Used by the routine at D486.
Print the countdown before the jump
D5F5 LD HL,$D573 countdown to the jump =$00 no countdown yet
D5F8 LD A,(HL)
D5F9 AND A
D5FA RET Z
D5FB DEC (HL)
D5FC JR Z,$D610 the jump itself
D5FE LD B,A
D5FF LD A,$02
D601 LD ($A88E),A ; number of digits to print for the number (padded on the left with spaces or zeros)
D604 LD HL,$0101
D607 LD ($A80D),HL set CrdXPrnSymbol
D60A LD A,B
D60B SRL A
D60D JP $B75C print a number
D610 CALL $D563 the jump itself
D613 LD A,($A80A) get JumpDocking
D616 SUB $02

D624 XOR A

D643 CALL $D64F update bytes for the next galaxy

hl - undefined

D655 RLC D

D658 INC HL

D7CF LD HL,$A871 +92 >0 - mission 3 given =1 mission 3 will be given on docking ( =1 time to give the mission, will become =2 after the jump) =2 mission 3 will be given on docking =3 mission 3 given (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =4 mission 3 given, the ship is in the target system (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =5 mission 3 completed, there will be congratulations on docking

D7F8 LD A,(DE)
D7F9 RRCA bit 0

D7FC RRCA bit 1
D7FD JR NC,$D849 give mission 2
D7FF RRCA bit 2

DA0B: Data block at DA0B
RadiusBlast DA0B DEFB $00 circle radius when printing an explosion
PilotMission2 DA0C DEFB $00,$00 =3 the Asp from mission 2 is destroyed / there is a canister with the cloak in space =2 the Asp's invisibility is off =1 mission 2 given =0 while moving on the J-drive
FrameCounter DA0E DEFB $00 ship-appearance counter???? frame counter
J_Drive_Ready4 DA0F DEFB $00 =1 an object nearby is blocking the J-drive <>0 J-drive unavailable
EnergyBombUsed DA10 DEFB $00 <>0, =1, =$FF energy bomb exploded
FlgObjNear DA11 DEFB $00 <>0 there is some object nearby
ObjInTarget DA12 DEFB $00,$00 <>0 there is something in the crosshair (object number???)
PlayerPirate DA14 DEFB $00 =1 the player attacked a peaceful trader/station/police. enable aggression
ManualDocking DA15 DEFB $00 =0 when leaving the planet <>0 manual docking started????
TimeWorkECM DA16 DEFB $00 remaining system runtime =0 ECM off
DamageEnergyUnit DA17 DEFB $00 damage to the energy units
DamageFrontShield DA18 DEFB $00 damage to the front shields
DamageRearShield DA19 DEFB $00 damage to the rear shields
FlgCoriolisNear DA1A DEFB $00 <>0 there is a station nearby
nMissileObj DA1B DEFB $00 number of missiles in space >=4, random ships don't appear
InverseNumSlotIX DA1C DEFB $00 reverse slot number of the object whose slot address is in IX (possibly targeted)
DA1D: MoveAllObjects
Used by the routine at 7287.
Process all objects in space
In: ---
Out: ix,iy,hl,de,bc,a - undefined
DA1D LD A,($A871) get PilotMission3
DA20 CP $04
DA22 JR NZ,$DA29
DA24 LD HL,$6069 2,=1 Coriolis is hostile
DA27 SET 2,(HL)
DA29 LD A,($707B) get TypeOfPrint
DA2C AND A
DA2D LD A,$B1 or c
DA2F JR Z,$DA33 adjust the line-printing routine
DA31 LD A,$A9 xor c
DA33 LD ($F203),A adjust the line-printing routine
DA36 LD ($F231),A
DA39 LD ($F27E),A
DA3C LD ($F252),A
DA3F LD A,($DA0E) get FrameCounter
DA42 DEC A
DA43 LD ($DA0E),A set FrameCounter
DA46 LD B,A
DA47 LD A,($7077) get CoriolisNear
DA4A AND A
DA4B JR NZ,$DA5F
DA4D LD A,($7061) get InFreeSpace
DA50 AND A
DA51 JR NZ,$DA5F
DA53 LD A,($DA1B) get nMissileObj
DA56 CP $04
DA58 JR NC,$DA5F
DA5A LD A,B
DA5B AND A
DA5C CALL Z,$F2CA set rnd ship
DA5F XOR A
DA60 LD ($DA17),A set DamageEnergyUnit
DA63 LD ($DA19),A set DamageRearShield
DA66 LD ($DA18),A set DamageFrontShield
DA69 LD ($DA12),A set ObjInTarget
DA6C LD ($DA1A),A set FlgCoriolisNear
DA6F LD ($DA11),A set FlgObjNear
DA72 LD ($DA0F),A set J_Drive_Ready4
DA75 LD C,A c=0
DA76 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
DA7A LD DE,$0027 '''
DA7D LD B,$06 c=0
DA7F ADD IX,DE
DA81 LD A,(IX+$22)
DA84 AND A
DA85 JR Z,$DA8E next
DA87 BIT 6,(IX+$20)
DA8B JR Z,$DA8E next (not a missile)
DA8D INC C
DA8E DJNZ $DA7F
DA90 LD A,C a,c number of missiles
DA91 LD ($DA1B),A set nMissileObj
DA94 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
DA98 LD A,$08
DA9A PUSH AF
DA9B LD B,A
DA9C LD ($DA1C),A set InverseNumSlotIX
DA9F LD A,(IX+$22)
DAA2 AND A
DAA3 JP Z,$DBBE next object
DAA6 LD ($DA11),A set FlgObjNear
DAA9 BIT 2,(IX+$26)
DAAD JR NZ,$DAB2
DAAF LD ($DA1A),A set FlgCoriolisNear
DAB2 LD A,($DA0E) get FrameCounter
DAB5 LD C,A
DAB6 BIT 3,(IX+$26)
DABA JR NZ,$DAC1
DABC LD A,$01
DABE LD ($DA0F),A set J_Drive_Ready4
DAC1 LD A,($DA10) get EnergyBombUsed
DAC4 AND A
DAC5 JR Z,$DAD8
DAC7 BIT 2,(IX+$26)
DACB JR NZ,$DAD8
DACD LD A,(IY+$06) iy - start address of the ship descriptor, the previous one???
DAD0 CP $27 '''
DAD2 JR Z,$DAD8
DAD4 SET 4,(IX+$20)
DAD8 BIT 2,(IX+$21)
DADC JR Z,$DAEA object is not aggressive
DADE LD A,(IX+$21)
DAE1 AND $60 '`'
DAE3 JR Z,$DAEA
DAE5 LD A,$01
DAE7 LD ($DA14),A set PlayerPirate
DAEA LD A,($617B) missile descriptor +$22
DAED AND A
DAEE JR NZ,$DAF6 if there is at least one missile, enable aggression
DAF0 LD A,($DA14) get PlayerPirate
DAF3 AND A if the invisibility cloak is on, enable aggression
DAF4 JR Z,$DAFA
DAF6 SET 2,(IX+$21)
DAFA LD H,(IX+$24)
DAFD LD L,(IX+$23)
DB00 PUSH HL
DB01 POP IY iy address of the object data
DB03 LD A,(IX+$20)
DB06 AND $30 '0'
DB08 JR Z,$DB71 ????
DB0A SUB $20 ' '
DB0C JP NZ,$DBA3 ????
DB0F RES 5,(IX+$20)
DB13 LD (IX+$1E),A =0
DB16 LD A,$C0
DB18 LD (IX+$1D),A
DB1B BIT 0,(IX+$26)
DB1F JR NZ,$DB43 branch if it's a missile
DB21 LD HL,$707D which sound to play =$00 no sound (silent) 0,=1 energy bomb exploded 1,=1 missile launched 2,=1 colliding with an object/object destroyed 3,=1 laser fired 4,=1 laser fired 5,=1 ECM working 6,=1 hit by laser on the player/docking damage 7,=1
DB24 SET 2,(HL)
DB26 LD A,($DA0C) get PilotMission2
DB29 AND A
DB2A JR Z,$DB43 destroy the object
DB2C CP $03
DB2E JR Z,$DB38 the Asp from mission 2 was destroyed
DB30 BIT 6,(IX+$26)
DB34 JR Z,$DB43 destroy the object
DB36 JR $DB3F
DB38 LD A,(IY+$01) the Asp from mission 2 was destroyed
DB3B CP $1E
DB3D JR NZ,$DB43 destroy the object
DB3F XOR A
DB40 LD ($DA0C),A set PilotMission2
DB43 XOR A destroy the object
DB44 LD (IX+$22),A
DB47 CALL $EB65 object has disappeared from space (destroyed/flown off). Untarget the missile and destroy any missiles fired at this object
DB4A LD D,(IY+$09)
DB4D LD E,(IY+$08)
DB50 CALL $A900 increase the money amount
DB53 LD D,(IY+$16)
DB56 LD E,(IY+$15)
DB59 SRL D
DB5B RR E
DB5D CALL $A8EE increase combat rating
DB60 LD A,(IX+$21)
DB63 AND $60 '`'
DB65 JR Z,$DBBE next object
DB67 LD A,($A821) pilot's legal status 0 - clean 1..31h offender 32h..FFh fugitive
DB6A OR $40 '@'
DB6C LD ($A821),A pilot's legal status 0 - clean 1..31h offender 32h..FFh fugitive
DB6F JR $DBBE next object
DB71 LD A,B b - reverse slot number of the object whose slot address is in IX c - ship-appearance counter????
DB72 XOR C
DB73 AND $0F
DB75 BIT 6,(IX+$20)
DB79 JR Z,$DB8B object is not controlled
DB7B BIT 0,(IX+$26)
DB7F JR Z,$DB86 not a missile
DB81 CALL $F928 this is a missile
DB84 JR $DB8B
DB86 AND $03
DB88 CALL Z,$F733 object's reaction to the player. Launching missiles, ships, escape capsule, etc.
DB8B CALL $DCE0 recalculate object coordinates
DB8E CALL $E791 align the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
DB91 PUSH IX
DB93 LD DE,$0009
DB96 ADD IX,DE
DB98 CALL $DCA3 x=x*l y=y*l z=z*l, ix=ix+6
DB9B CALL $DCA3 x=x*l y=y*l z=z*l, ix=ix+6
DB9E CALL $DCA3 x=x*l y=y*l z=z*l, ix=ix+6
DBA1 POP IX
DBA3 CALL $EA51 check for collision of the object with the player, and handle it
DBA6 CALL $E008 compute visibility and print the object
DBA9 RES 3,(IX+$20)
DBAD LD A,(IX+$20)
DBB0 AND $30 '0'
DBB2 CP $10
DBB4 JR NZ,$DBBE next object
DBB6 SET 5,(IX+$20)
DBBA RES 4,(IX+$20)
DBBE POP AF next object
DBBF LD DE,$0027 '''
DBC2 ADD IX,DE
DBC4 DEC A
DBC5 JP NZ,$DA9A
DBC8 XOR A
DBC9 LD ($DA10),A set EnergyBombUsed
DBCC LD A,($DA0F) get J_Drive_Ready4
DBCF AND A
DBD0 RET NZ
DBD1 LD ($DA14),A set PlayerPirate
DBD4 RET
DBD5: CalcLenVector
Used by the routine at F9FA.
Compute vector length and normalize the vector
In: ix - address of the vector's per-axis lengths
Out: de=sqrt(dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2) vector length
(ix) - normalized vector coordinates
a =$00
DBD5 PUSH IX
DBD7 CALL $DBFA squaring
DBDA PUSH HL
DBDB CALL $DBFA squaring
DBDE PUSH HL
DBDF CALL $DBFA squaring
DBE2 POP DE
DBE3 ADD HL,DE
DBE4 POP DE
DBE5 ADD HL,DE
DBE6 POP IX hl=dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2
DBE8 CALL $DC53 extract the square root h=sqr(hl)
DBEB EX DE,HL de=sqrt(dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2)
DBEC PUSH IX
DBEE CALL $DC31 dX = dX / sqrt(dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2) / 2
DBF1 CALL $DC31 dY = dY / sqrt(dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2) / 2
DBF4 CALL $DC31 dZ = dZ / sqrt(dX^2+dY^2+dZ^2) / 2
DBF7 POP IX
DBF9 RET
DBFA: Sqr_HL_wordAdrIX
Used by the routine at DBD5.
Squaring
In: HL = high(word(ix+0/1)^2)
DBFA LD B,(IX+$01)
DBFD LD C,(IX+$00)
DC00 RES 7,B
DC02 PUSH IX
DC04 LD IX,$0000
DC08 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
DC0B XOR A
DC0C LD D,A
DC0D ADD HL,HL
DC0E RL D
DC10 LD E,H
DC11 ADD IX,DE
DC13 LD H,L
DC14 LD L,A
DC15 PUSH HL
DC16 LD H,$BE
DC18 LD L,B
DC19 LD E,(HL)
DC1A INC H
DC1B LD D,(HL)
DC1C DEC H
DC1D ADD IX,DE
DC1F LD L,C
DC20 LD E,(HL)
DC21 INC H
DC22 LD D,(HL)
DC23 POP HL
DC24 ADD HL,DE
DC25 JR NC,$DC29
DC27 INC IX
DC29 EX (SP),IX
DC2B POP HL
DC2C INC IX
DC2E INC IX
DC30 RET
DC31: CaclNormalVector
Used by the routine at DBD5.
Normalize the vector
In: ix - address of the dX/dY/dZ variable
de - vector length (divisor)
a =$00
Out: (ix) - normalized vector coordinate
ix=ix+2
bc - undefined
a =$00
DC31 LD H,(IX+$01)
DC34 LD L,(IX+$00)
DC37 PUSH HL
DC38 RES 7,H
DC3A CALL $E8CF fixed-point division hl.a/de
DC3D SRL B
DC3F RR C b.c = hl.a/de/2
DC41 POP HL
DC42 BIT 7,H
DC44 JR Z,$DC48
DC46 SET 7,B
DC48 LD (IX+$00),C
DC4B LD (IX+$01),B
DC4E INC IX
DC50 INC IX
DC52 RET
DC53: Sqrt_H_HL
Used by the routines at 8A5A, AD45 and DBD5.
Extract the square root h=sqr(hl)
In: hl - number
Out: h - result
l - remainder
a=0
de,bc - undefined
DC53 LD A,H
DC54 CP $10
DC56 JR NC,$DC7A
DC58 AND A
DC59 JR Z,$DC6C
DC5B LD B,$04
DC5D ADD HL,HL
DC5E DJNZ $DC5D
DC60 CALL $DC7A
DC63 SRL H
DC65 RR L
DC67 SRL H
DC69 RR L
DC6B RET
DC6C LD H,L
DC6D LD L,A
DC6E CALL $DC7A
DC71 LD B,$04
DC73 SRL H
DC75 RR L
DC77 DJNZ $DC73
DC79 RET
DC7A XOR A
DC7B PUSH HL
DC7C PUSH HL
DC7D SRL H
DC7F RR L
DC81 RRA
DC82 LD DE,$8000
DC85 CALL $DC97
DC88 POP DE
DC89 CALL $DC8D
DC8C POP DE
DC8D RR H
DC8F RR L
DC91 EX DE,HL
DC92 SRL H
DC94 RR L
DC96 RRA
DC97 PUSH DE
DC98 CALL $E8CF fixed-point division hl.a/de
DC9B SRL B
DC9D RR C
DC9F XOR A
DCA0 POP HL
DCA1 ADD HL,BC
DCA2 RET
DCA3: CorrAxisVector
Used by the routines at DA1D and DFDD.
Normalize the vector (correcting small computation errors)
In: ix - vector start address
Out: ix=ix+6
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
DCA3 LD H,$BF
DCA5 LD L,(IX+$01) hl address of the squares table (high bytes)
DCA8 SLA L
DCAA LD A,(HL)
DCAB LD L,(IX+$03)
DCAE SLA L
DCB0 ADD A,(HL)
DCB1 LD L,(IX+$05)
DCB4 SLA L
DCB6 ADD A,(HL)
DCB7 SRA A
DCB9 SRA A hi(dx)^2+hi(dy)^2+hi(dz)^2
DCBB NEG
DCBD XOR $80
DCBF LD B,A
DCC0 CALL $DCC6
DCC3 CALL $DCC6
DCC6: Routine at DCC6
Used by the routine at DCA3.
DCC6 LD C,(IX+$01)
DCC9 SLA C
DCCB CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
DCCE SLA H
DCD0 SLA (IX+$01)
DCD4 RR H
DCD6 LD (IX+$01),H
DCD9 INC IX
DCDB INC IX
DCDD RET
DCDE: Unused
AngleLR4Calc DCDE DEFS $01
AngleDU4Calc DCDF DEFS $01
DCE0: ReCalcCrdObj
Used by the routines at 779D and DA1D.
Recalculate object coordinates
In: ix - start address of the object slot
(InverseNumSlotIX) - its reverse index in the slot list
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
DCE0 CALL $DED1 update object coordinates from player movement
DCE3 BIT 2,(IX+$26)
DCE7 JR NZ,$DCEE check the object's distance along Z
DCE9 LD A,L check the object's distance along Z
DCEA CP $60 '`'
DCEC JR NC,$DCF3 the object has moved far away, remove it from space
DCEE LD A,H check the object's distance along Z
DCEF AND $7F
DCF1 JR Z,$DCFA check the object's distance along X
DCF3 XOR A the object has moved far away, remove it from space
DCF4 LD (IX+$22),A
DCF7 JP $EB65 object has disappeared from space (destroyed/flown off). Untarget the missile and destroy any missiles fired at this object
DCFA LD A,(IX+$02) check the object's distance along X
DCFD AND $7F
DCFF JR NZ,$DCF3 the object has moved far away, remove it from space
DD01 LD A,(IX+$05) check the object's distance along Y
DD04 AND $7F
DD06 JR NZ,$DCF3 the object has moved far away, remove it from space
DD08 LD HL,($7042) get HighAngleLR
DD0B LD ($DCDE),HL ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DD0E PUSH IX
DD10 LD DE,$0009
DD13 ADD IX,DE ix start address of the rotation angles
DD15 CALL $DE0B rotate the OCS axis vector by the given angles
DD18 CALL $DE0B rotate the OCS axis vector by the given angles
DD1B CALL $DE0B rotate the OCS axis vector by the given angles

DD26 JR NZ,$DD33
DD28 AND $0F
DD2A LD A,$00

DD2E LD A,B
DD2F DEC A
DD30 LD (IX+$1D),A
DD33 LD A,$0C
DD35 BIT 6,(IX+$1D)
DD39 JR Z,$DD3D
DD3B ADD A,$80
DD3D LD ($DCDE),A ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DD40 LD A,(IX+$1E)
DD43 LD B,A
DD44 BIT 7,A
DD46 JR NZ,$DD53
DD48 AND $0F
DD4A LD A,$00
DD4C JR Z,$DD5D
DD4E LD A,B
DD4F DEC A
DD50 LD (IX+$1E),A
DD53 LD A,$08
DD55 BIT 6,(IX+$1E)
DD59 JR Z,$DD5D
DD5B ADD A,$80
This entry point is used by the routine at DFDD.
DD5D LD ($DCDF),A up/down rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DD60 PUSH IX
DD62 CALL $DE88 rotate the like-named coordinates of the OCS axis vectors by the given angles
DD65 CALL $DE88 rotate the like-named coordinates of the OCS axis vectors by the given angles
DD68 CALL $DE88 rotate the like-named coordinates of the OCS axis vectors by the given angles
DD6B POP IX
DD6D LD A,(IX+$1C)
DD70 AND A
DD71 JR Z,$DD92
DD73 LD B,(IX+$1B)
DD76 ADD A,B
DD77 BIT 7,A

DD7B XOR A

DD7E LD C,(IY+$06)

DD85 JR Z,$DD8B
DD87 SLA C
DD89 SLA C
DD8B CP C

DD8F LD (IX+$1B),A
DD92 PUSH IX

DDAB: CalcNewCrdObjSpeed
Used by the routine at DCE0.
Update the object's global coordinates from its speed
In: ix=iy address of the object descriptor
c - object speed (ix+oSpeed)
Out: hl,de,a - undefined
DDAB PUSH BC
DDAC LD D,(IX+$0A)
DDAF LD E,(IX+$09)
DDB2 CALL $DDD6 compute the increment for the object's global coordinate high((d.e * speed*4)/$100+$FF) =34b =389/425t
DDB5 LD A,(IY+$02)
DDB8 LD H,(IY+$01)
DDBB LD L,(IY+$00)
DDBE CALL $DDF8 coordinate increment bh.l = ah.l + d.e
DDC1 LD (IY+$02),B
DDC4 LD (IY+$01),H
DDC7 LD (IY+$00),L
DDCA INC IY
DDCC INC IY
DDCE INC IY
DDD0 INC IX
DDD2 INC IX
DDD4 POP BC
DDD5 RET
DDD6: CaclDeltaCrdObj
Used by the routine at DDAB.
Compute the increment for the object's global coordinate
high((d.e * speed*4)/$100+$FF)
=34b
=389/425t
In: de - coordinate n of the OCS X axis
c - object speed (ix+oSpeed)
Out: d.e - increment of the object's global coordinate
DDD6 SLA D
DDD8 RL C
DDDA RL C
DDDC SRL D
DDDE LD HL,$0000
DDE1 LD B,$06
DDE3 SRL D
DDE5 RR E
DDE7 SLA C
DDE9 JR NC,$DDEC
DDEB ADD HL,DE
DDEC DJNZ $DDE3
DDEE LD DE,$00FF
DDF1 ADD HL,DE
DDF2 LD E,H
DDF3 SLA C
DDF5 RR D
DDF7 RET
DDF8: CrdNAddDeltaCrd
Used by the routine at DDAB.
Coordinate increment bh.l = ah.l + d.e
In: ah.l - coordinate (7,a = sign)
d.e - coordinate increment (7,d = sign)
Out: bh.l - new coordinate (7,b - new sign)
DDF8 LD B,A
DDF9 LD C,D
DDFA XOR C
DDFB RES 7,D
DDFD JR NZ,$DE01
DDFF ADD HL,DE
DE00 RET
DE01 SBC HL,DE
DE03 RET NC
DE04 ADD HL,DE
DE05 EX DE,HL
DE06 AND A
DE07 SBC HL,DE
DE09 LD B,C
DE0A RET
DE0B: RotateAxisSCO
Used by the routine at DCE0.
Rotate the OCS axis vector by the given angles
In: ix - address of the OCS axis vector
Out: ix=ix+6
de=$0006
bc,a - undefined
DE0B LD A,($DCDF) up/down rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE0E XOR $80
DE10 SRA A
DE12 RES 6,A
DE14 LD C,A
DE15 LD B,(IX+$03)
DE18 LD D,(IX+$05)
DE1B CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DE1E LD (IX+$05),A
DE21 LD A,($DCDE) ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE24 XOR $80
DE26 LD C,A
DE27 LD B,(IX+$03)
DE2A LD D,(IX+$01)
DE2D CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DE30 LD (IX+$01),A
DE33 LD A,($DCDE) ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE36 LD C,A
DE37 LD B,(IX+$01)
DE3A LD D,(IX+$03)
DE3D CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DE40 LD (IX+$03),A
DE43 LD A,($DCDF) up/down rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE46 SRA A
DE48 RES 6,A
DE4A LD C,A
DE4B LD B,(IX+$05)
DE4E LD D,(IX+$03)
DE51 CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DE54 LD (IX+$03),A
DE57 LD DE,$0006
DE5A ADD IX,DE
DE5C RET
DE5D: CalcNewCrdNAxis
Used by the routines at DE0B and DE88.
Compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
In: a,c - rotation angle
b - Y coordinate of the OCS axis
d - Z coordinate of the OCS axis
Out: a = crd(d) + crd(b) * angle(c)*2 (signs accounted for)
7,a - sign
de,bc - undefined
DE5D PUSH DE
DE5E XOR B
DE5F SLA B
DE61 RES 7,C
DE63 LD E,A
DE64 CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
DE67 POP BC
DE68 LD C,A
DE69 LD A,E
DE6A XOR B
DE6B SLA B
DE6D RR E
DE6F SLA C
DE71 RLA
DE72 LD A,B
DE73 JR C,$DE7D
DE75 ADD A,C
DE76 JP NC,$DE84
DE79 LD A,$FF
DE7B JR $DE84
DE7D SUB C
DE7E JR NC,$DE84
DE80 NEG
DE82 RL E
DE84 RL E
DE86 RRA
DE87 RET
DE88: RotateCrdNSCO
Used by the routine at DCE0.
Rotate the like-named coordinates of the OCS axis vectors by the given angles
In: ix - address of the first OCS axis vector's coordinate
Out: ix=ix+2
de,bc,a - undefined
DE88 LD A,($DCDF) up/down rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE8B XOR $80
DE8D LD C,A
DE8E LD B,(IX+$10)
DE91 LD D,(IX+$0A)
DE94 CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DE97 LD (IX+$0A),A
DE9A LD A,($DCDE) ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DE9D XOR $80
DE9F LD C,A
DEA0 LD B,(IX+$10)
DEA3 LD D,(IX+$16)
DEA6 CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DEA9 LD (IX+$16),A
DEAC LD A,($DCDE) ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DEAF LD C,A
DEB0 LD B,(IX+$16)
DEB3 LD D,(IX+$10)
DEB6 CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DEB9 LD (IX+$10),A
DEBC LD A,($DCDF) up/down rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DEBF LD C,A
DEC0 LD B,(IX+$0A)
DEC3 LD D,(IX+$10)
DEC6 CALL $DE5D compute the new coordinate of the object's coordinate-system axis
DEC9 LD (IX+$10),A
DECC INC IX
DECE INC IX
DED0 RET
DED1: CalcNewCrdObj_Pl
Used by the routines at 778F and DCE0.
Update object coordinates from player movement
In: ix - start address of the object slot
Out: hl.a - crd Z
DED1 LD A,($7043) get HighAngleDU
DED4 XOR $80
DED6 LD C,A
DED7 LD DE,$0003
DEDA LD HL,$0006
DEDD CALL $DF46 crdZ=crdY*-angleDU/$100+crdZ
DEE0 LD A,($7042) get HighAngleLR
DEE3 XOR $80
DEE5 CALL $BC6C multiply by 2, preserving the sign
DEE8 LD DE,$0003
DEEB LD H,D
DEEC LD L,D
DEED CALL $DF46 crdX=crdY*-angleLR*2/$100+crdX
DEF0 LD A,($7042) get HighAngleLR
DEF3 CALL $BC6C multiply by 2, preserving the sign
DEF6 LD HL,$0003
DEF9 LD D,H
DEFA LD E,H
DEFB CALL $DF46 crdY=crdX*angleLR*2/$100+crdY

DF02 LD HL,$0003
DF05 LD DE,$0006
DF08 CALL $DF46 crdY=crdZ*angleDU/$100+crdY
DF0B LD A,($7044) get HighDeltaSpeed

DF10 LD B,A

DF42 LD (IX+$07),L
DF45 RET
DF46: CalcNewCrdNobj
Used by the routine at DED1.
Compute the object's new coordinate from an element of the player's movement vector
In: ix - start address of the object's coordinates
hl =0/3/6 coordinate X/Y/Z
de =0/3/6 coordinate X/Y/Z
c - rotation angle
Out: crd(hl) = crd(de) * c/$100 + crd(hl) (signs accounted for)
hi(crd(de))*c + med(crd(de))*c/$100 + low(crd(de))*c/$10000 + crd(hl)
DF46 PUSH IX
DF48 PUSH IX
DF4A EX (SP),IY
DF4C ADD IX,DE
DF4E EX DE,HL
DF4F ADD IY,DE
DF51 LD A,(IX+$02)
DF54 LD D,(IX+$01)
DF57 LD E,(IX+$00)

DF5F RES 7,C
DF61 RES 7,B

DF69 LD B,D
DF6A PUSH DE
DF6B CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C

DFDD: RotateIntroObj
Used by the routine at 716B.
Rotate and print an object into the buffer for the intro/title screen
In: ---
Out: iy,ix,hl,de,bc,a - undefined
DFDD LD IX,$6159 slot for the missile +00
DFE1 LD H,(IX+$24)
DFE4 LD L,(IX+$23)
DFE7 PUSH HL
DFE8 POP IY iy address of the object data
DFEA LD A,$94
DFEC LD ($DCDE),A ship rotation angle 7,n sign $00.$0F
DFEF LD A,$8C
DFF1 CALL $DD5D
DFF4 CALL $E791 align the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
DFF7 LD IX,$6162
DFFB CALL $DCA3 normalize the vector (correcting small computation errors)
DFFE CALL $DCA3 normalize the vector (correcting small computation errors)
E001 LD IX,$6159 slot for the missile +00
E005 JP $E008
E008: PrnObjIfVisible
Used by the routines at DA1D and DFDD.
Compute visibility and print the object
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object data
(sp) - reverse index of the object
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
E008 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
E00B LD B,A
E00C CP $04
E00E RET NC exit if not the view screen
E00F PUSH IX
E011 POP HL
E012 BIT 1,A
E014 JR NZ,$E01A left/right
E016 LD DE,$0006 front/rear
E019 ADD HL,DE
E01A LD E,(HL)
E01B INC HL
E01C LD D,(HL)
E01D INC HL
E01E LD C,(HL) cde - distance to the object
E01F SRL A
E021 XOR B
E022 RRCA
E023 XOR C
E024 AND $80
E026 RET NZ
E027 LD ($E2E0),DE set DistanceToObj
E02B LD H,$00
E02D LD L,(IY+$01) distance to collision
E030 PUSH HL
E031 AND A
E032 SBC HL,DE
E034 JR C,$E038 de - distance to the object along the view-screen axis (sp) - distance to collision
E036 POP HL too close.
E037 RET
E038 PUSH DE de - distance to the object along the view-screen axis (sp) - distance to collision
E039 PUSH DE
E03A BIT 1,B
E03C JR NZ,$E063 left/right
E03E EX DE,HL
E03F LD D,(IX+$01)
E042 LD E,(IX+$00)
E045 XOR A
E046 LD C,A cde - crdX hl - distance to the object along the view-screen axis
E047 CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E04A LD A,C
E04B LD ($E2E9),A set CrdXProjection
E04E POP HL
E04F LD D,(IX+$04)
E052 LD E,(IX+$03)
E055 XOR A
E056 LD C,A
E057 CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E05A LD A,C
E05B LD ($E2EA),A set CrdYProjection
E05E LD B,(IX+$02)
E061 JR $E086
E063 EX DE,HL
E064 LD D,(IX+$07)
E067 LD E,(IX+$06)
E06A XOR A
E06B LD C,A
E06C CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E06F LD A,C
E070 LD ($E2E9),A set CrdXProjection
E073 POP HL
E074 LD D,(IX+$04)
E077 LD E,(IX+$03)
E07A XOR A
E07B LD C,A
E07C CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E07F LD A,C
E080 LD ($E2EA),A set CrdYProjection
E083 LD B,(IX+$08)
E086 LD A,(IX+$05)
E089 SLA B
E08B RLA
E08C RLA
E08D LD ($E2EB),A set CrdXYsign
E090 POP HL
E091 POP DE
E092 XOR A
E093 LD C,A
E094 CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E097 LD H,C
E098 LD A,C
E099 LD ($DA0B),A set RadiusBlast
E09C LD E,C
E09D LD A,($E2EA) get CrdYProjection
E0A0 LD C,A
E0A1 SUB H
E0A2 JR C,$E0A9
E0A4 SUB $40 '@'
E0A6 JP NC,$E206 print the enemy laser beam
E0A9 LD A,($E2E9) get CrdXProjection
E0AC LD B,A
E0AD SUB H
E0AE JR C,$E0B5
E0B0 SUB $80
E0B2 JP NC,$E206 print the enemy laser beam
E0B5 LD A,B
E0B6 ADD A,C
E0B7 LD D,A
E0B8 JR C,$E0FA
E0BA SUB E
E0BB JR NC,$E0CB
E0BD LD A,(IX+$07)
E0C0 CP $2C ','
E0C2 JR NC,$E0CB
E0C4 POP HL
E0C5 POP AF
E0C6 PUSH AF
E0C7 PUSH HL
E0C8 LD ($DA12),A set ObjInTarget
E0CB BIT 6,(IX+$20)
E0CF JR Z,$E0FA
E0D1 BIT 2,(IX+$21)
E0D5 JR Z,$E0FA
E0D7 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
E0DA BIT 1,A
E0DC LD A,(IX+$0E)
E0DF LD E,(IX+$07)

E0ED CP $40 '@'

E0F2 CP $20 ' '
E0F4 JR NC,$E0FA

E0FA BIT 4,(IX+$20)
E0FE JP NZ,$E1D4 set values for printing the object's explosion
E101 LD A,($DA0C) get PilotMission2

E105 JR NZ,$E10E
E107 BIT 6,(IX+$26)
E10B JP NZ,$E206 print the enemy laser beam
E10E LD HL,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E111 LD A,H
E112 CP (IY+$0B)
E115 JR C,$E175 print the object and an enemy laser shot
E117 LD A,($E2E9) get CrdXProjection
E11A CP $7F
E11C JP NC,$E206 print the enemy laser beam
E11F LD C,A
E120 LD A,($E2EA) get CrdYProjection
E123 CP $3F '?'
E125 JP NC,$E206 print the enemy laser beam
E128 LD B,A
E129 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen
E12C BIT 0,A
E12E LD A,($E2EB) get CrdXYsign
E131 JR Z,$E135
E133 XOR $02
E135 LD D,A
E136 BIT 1,D
E138 JR NZ,$E140
E13A LD A,C

E156 CALL $E164 print a single dot

E15A CALL $E164 print a single dot
E15D DEC B
E15E CALL $E164 print a single dot

E164: objPrnPixel
Used by the routine at E008.

E168 LD D,A

E175: objPrnObj
Used by the routine at E008.

E177 CALL $E7F3 build the OCS rotation matrix
E17A POP IY
E17C LD HL,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E17F ADD HL,HL
E180 LD B,$1F
E182 JR C,$E189
E184 LD A,H
E185 CP B
E186 JR NC,$E189

E189 LD A,B

E197 CALL $E923 build the face-visibility table from the normals
E19A CALL $E2F1 build the table of print coordinates for the object's visible vertices
E19D CALL $E729 print the object
E1A0 BIT 3,(IX+$20)
E1A4 RET Z
E1A5 LD A,(IY+$14)
E1A8 CALL $E778 get a vertex's print coordinates from the table by index
E1AB LD C,A
This entry point is used by the routine at E206.
E1AC CALL $ED24 print the enemy laser beam
E1AF LD L,A
E1B0 SUB $FF
E1B2 NEG
E1B4 LD H,A
E1B5 LD A,C
E1B6 CPL
E1B7 AND $03

E1BB SLA C
E1BD OR C
E1BE LD C,A
E1BF LD A,($F116)
E1C2 PUSH AF
E1C3 SET 7,A
E1C5 LD ($F116),A
E1C8 CALL $EE10 Print a line in the buffer???
E1CB POP AF
E1CC LD ($F116),A
E1CF RES 3,(IX+$20)
E1D3 RET
E1D4: objDestroyed
Used by the routine at E008.

E1DA LD A,($E2EA) get CrdYProjection

E1E3 LD (IX+$1F),A
E1E6 LD A,($7041) get WhatOnScreen

E1F9 RLA

E206: objPrintLaser
Used by the routine at E008.

E2A2 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
E2AA DEFB $00,$00
VergesVisible E2AC DEFB $01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01
E2B4 DEFB $01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01,$01
MatrixRotate E2BC DEFB $7E,$32,$81,$8D,$F3,$24,$81,$8D
E2C4 DEFB $7E,$32,$F3,$24,$F2,$A4,$F2,$A4
E2CC DEFB $F3,$24
data_E2CE E2CE DEFB $F3,$24,$F2,$A4,$F2,$A4,$F3,$24
E2D6 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
E2DE DEFB $00,$00
DistanceToObj E2E0 DEFW $0000 distance to the object along the view-screen axis
PointTablCrdPrn E2E2 DEFB $01 pointer to the current entry in the vertex print-coordinate table
VertexCrdX E2E3 DEFW $0000 vertex coordinates after OCS rotation
VertexCrdY E2E5 DEFW $0000
VertexCrdZ E2E7 DEFW $0000
CrdXProjection E2E9 DEFB $00 X coordinate of the object's projection
CrdYProjection E2EA DEFB $00 Y coordinate of the object's projection
CrdXYsign E2EB DEFB $00 signs of the object's projection coordinates
VertexSignCrdX E2EC DEFB $00 signs of the vertex coordinates after OCS rotation
VertexSignCrdY E2ED DEFB $00
VertexSignCrdZ E2EE DEFB $00
VertexIncFactor E2EF DEFB $00 the number of left-shifts a close vertex's raw (unrotated) coordinates can tolerate before a bit would be shifted out of a byte, computed by E5CB when (DistanceToObj) < 4 so near objects retain precision instead of drawing as a tiny cluster of nearly-identical rotated coordinates; consumed by E353 (RotateCrdN_Vertex) and E447.
VisiblePrevVertex E2F0 DEFB $00 visibility of the previous vertex <>0 the previous vertex is not visible
E2F1: MakeTablPrnCrdVertex
Used by the routine at E175.
Build the table of print coordinates for the object's visible vertices
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object data
Out: bc=$0006
a=$00
hl,de - undefined
For each vertex, first checks E338 (TestVergesVisible) against the vertex's two adjacent-face indices - a vertex is skipped entirely (no rotation/projection done) unless at least one of its two faces is currently visible, so the expensive per-vertex rotation math only ever runs for vertices that actually matter this frame. A visible vertex is then rotated (E49C) and projected (E5CB) and its result cached into TablCrdPrnVertex (TablCrdPrnVertex), indexed by vertex number.
E2F1 PUSH IY
E2F3 PUSH IY
E2F5 LD A,(IY+$03) a number of vertices
E2F8 EX (SP),IX
E2FA LD D,(IY+$0D)
E2FD LD E,(IY+$0C)
E300 ADD IX,DE ix start address of the vertices
E302 LD IY,$E2BC
This entry point is used by the routine at E5CB.
E306 PUSH AF
E307 LD A,($E2E2) get PointTablCrdPrn
E30A ADD A,$03
E30C LD ($E2E2),A set PointTablCrdPrn
E30F LD A,(IX+$03)
E312 AND $1F
E314 CP $1F
E316 JR Z,$E31F
E318 LD B,A
E319 LD A,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E31C CP B
E31D JR NC,$E330
E31F LD A,(IX+$04)
E322 CALL $E338 check face visibility
E325 JR NZ,$E32D
E327 LD A,(IX+$05)
E32A CALL $E338 check face visibility
E32D JP NZ,$E5CB calculate the vertex's print coordinates
E330 INC A
E331 LD ($E2F0),A set VisiblePrevVertex
E334 POP AF
E335 JP $E71A
E338: TestVergesVisible
Used by the routines at E2F1 and E729.
Check face visibility
In: a =$nm - two face numbers
Out: a=1/0 face visible/invisible
z=1 - face invisible
hl,de,b - undefined
E338 LD B,A
E339 SRL B b=b/16
E33B SRL B
E33D SRL B
E33F SRL B
E341 AND $0F
E343 CALL $E348 a=(E2AC+a)
E346 RET NZ
E347 LD A,B
E348 LD HL,$E2AC a=(E2AC+a)
E34B ADD A,L
E34C LD L,A
E34D JR NC,$E350
E34F INC H
E350 LD A,(HL)
E351 AND A
E352 RET
E353: RotateCrdN_Vertex
Used by the routine at E5CB.
Rotate one vertex coordinate
In: ix - start address of the vertex coordinates
iy =MatrixRotate+n address of a row in the rotation matrix
Out: (MatrixRotate+$12) - matrix of products of the vertex coordinate and an element
of the rotation matrix
hl - new vertex coordinate (coordinate multiplied by a row of the
rotation matrix)
iy=iy+6
b=$00
de,c,a - undefined
E353 LD A,($E2F0) get VisiblePrevVertex
E356 AND A
E357 JR NZ,$E3B4
E359 LD A,(IX+$00)
E35C CP (IX-$06)
E35F JR NZ,$E3B4

E364 CP (IX-$05)
E367 JR NZ,$E3B4
E369 LD A,(IX+$02)
E36C CP (IX-$04)
E36F JR NZ,$E3B4

E377 LD B,A
E378 LD L,(IY+$12)
E37B LD H,(IY+$13)
E37E LD E,(IY+$14)
E381 LD D,(IY+$15)

E385 AND $80
E387 LD H,A
E388 LD (IY+$13),H
E38B RL B
E38D LD A,B
E38E XOR D
E38F AND $80
E391 LD D,A
E392 LD (IY+$15),D
E395 CALL $E424 signed addition
E398 LD L,A
E399 LD E,(IY+$16)
E39C LD D,(IY+$17)
E39F LD A,B
E3A0 RLA
E3A1 XOR D
E3A2 AND $80
E3A4 LD D,A
E3A5 LD (IY+$17),D
E3A8 CALL $E424 signed addition
E3AB LD L,A
E3AC LD DE,$0006
E3AF ADD IY,DE
E3B1 JP $E40C
E3B4 LD B,(IX+$00)
E3B7 CALL $E447 multiply the coordinate by a matrix element, accounting for the vertex coordinate scaling factor
E3BA LD A,(IX+$03)
E3BD XOR (IY+$01)
E3C0 RLA
E3C1 RR H
E3C3 LD (IY+$12),L
E3C6 LD (IY+$13),H
E3C9 EX DE,HL
E3CA INC IY
E3CC INC IY
E3CE LD B,(IX+$01)
E3D1 CALL $E447 multiply the coordinate by a matrix element, accounting for the vertex coordinate scaling factor
E3D4 LD A,(IX+$03)
E3D7 RLA
E3D8 XOR (IY+$01)
E3DB RLA
E3DC RR H
E3DE LD (IY+$12),L
E3E1 LD (IY+$13),H
E3E4 CALL $E424 signed addition
E3E7 LD L,A
E3E8 EX DE,HL
E3E9 INC IY
E3EB INC IY
E3ED LD B,(IX+$02)
E3F0 CALL $E447 multiply the coordinate by a matrix element, accounting for the vertex coordinate scaling factor
E3F3 LD A,(IX+$03)
E3F6 RLA
E3F7 RLA
E3F8 XOR (IY+$01)
E3FB RLA
E3FC RR H
E3FE LD (IY+$12),L
E401 LD (IY+$13),H
E404 CALL $E424 signed addition
E407 LD L,A
E408 INC IY
E40A INC IY
E40C LD A,($E2EF) get VertexIncFactor
E40F LD B,A
E410 XOR A
E411 LD C,H
E412 RES 7,H
E414 SRL H
E416 RR L
E418 RRA
E419 DJNZ $E414
E41B JR NC,$E41E
E41D INC HL
E41E BIT 7,C
E420 RET Z
E421 SET 7,H
E423 RET
E424: AddSign_L_E
Used by the routines at E353, E4E9, E52A and FB1F.
Signed addition
In: hl - first addend $00..$FF (7,h sign)
de - second addend $00..$FF (7,d sign)
Out: a - sum
7,h - sign
cy =1 sum overflow
E424 LD A,H
E425 XOR D
E426 RLA
E427 JR C,$E42C
E429 LD A,L
E42A ADD A,E
E42B RET

E42D SUB E
E42E RET NC
E42F NEG

E433 RET
E434: Mult_A_hiB_C
Used by the routines at DE5D, DF46, E447, E52A, E7B2, E9E4 and FB10.
A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E434 LD A,B
E435 SUB C
E436 JR NC,$E43A
E438 NEG
E43A SRL A
E43C LD H,$BF
E43E LD L,A
E43F LD D,(HL)
E440 LD A,B
E441 ADD A,C
E442 RRA
E443 LD L,A
E444 LD A,(HL)
E445 SUB D
E446 RET
E447: MultCrdNMatrixEl
Used by the routine at E353.
Multiply the coordinate by a matrix element, accounting for the scaling factor
of vertex coordinates
In: b - abs(crd N) of the vertex
iy =MatrixRotate+n address of an element in the rotation matrix
(VertexIncFactor) - vertex coordinate scaling factor
Out: hl = 0.b*2^(VertexIncFactor) * 0.MatrixElement
bc,a - undefined
E447 LD A,($E2EF) get VertexIncFactor
E44A SLA B
E44C DEC A
E44D JR NZ,$E44A
E44F LD C,(IY+$01)
E452 RES 7,C
E454 PUSH DE
E455 CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E458 LD L,A
E459 LD H,$00
E45B POP DE
E45C RET
E45D: Mult_HL_B_C
Used by the routines at DBFA, DCC6, DF46, E7F3, E88B, E9E4 and F0BE.
Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
computes: ((b+c)/2)^2 - ((b-c)/2)^2
should be: ((b+c)^2 - (b-c)^2)/4
E45D LD A,B
E45E SUB C
E45F JR NC,$E463
E461 NEG
E463 SRL A
E465 LD H,$BE
E467 LD L,A
E468 LD E,(HL)
E469 INC H
E46A LD D,(HL)
E46B DEC H
E46C LD A,B
E46D ADD A,C
E46E RRA
E46F LD L,A
E470 LD A,(HL)
E471 INC H
E472 LD H,(HL)
E473 LD L,A
E474 AND A
E475 SBC HL,DE
E477 RET
E478: AddCrdNobj_CrdNvertex
Used by the routine at E5CB.
Add the object coordinate and the vertex coordinate
In: (sp) - X coordinate of the rotated vertex (low byte*2)
cy - X coordinate of the rotated vertex (bit 7)
bh.l - object's crdX
de - X coordinate of the rotated vertex
Out: ah.l = CrdNobject + CrdNvertex * 2
E478 LD C,D
E479 RL E
E47B RL D
E47D LD A,B
E47E XOR C
E47F JR NZ,$E484
E481 ADD HL,DE
E482 LD A,B
E483 RET
E484 SBC HL,DE
E486 JR NC,$E48F
E488 ADD HL,DE
E489 EX DE,HL
E48A AND A
E48B SBC HL,DE
E48D LD A,C
E48E RET
E48F LD A,H
E490 OR L
E491 LD A,C
E492 RET Z
E493 POP DE
E494 POP AF
E495 NEG
E497 PUSH AF
E498 PUSH DE
E499 DEC HL
E49A LD A,B
E49B RET
E49C: RotateCrdVertex
Used by the routine at E5CB.
Rotate a vertex's three coordinates through the OCS matrix (distance to object > 4; see VertexIncFactor for the near-object variant).
In: ix - vertex address
(VisiblePrevVertex) - <>0 if the previous vertex processed is not visible
Out: (VertexCrdX/Y/Z) - rotated vertex coordinates
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
If the previous vertex was visible and this vertex's raw |X|,|Y|,|Z| magnitude bytes are byte-for-byte identical to the previous vertex's (some ships share duplicate vertex positions across different parts of their geometry), the full per-axis rotation (E52A, three calls) is skipped in favour of E4E9, which reuses the previous vertex's already-computed matrix products and just re-applies any sign difference between the two vertices on each axis - cheaper than recomputing three matrix-row multiplications from scratch.
E49C LD A,($E2F0) get VisiblePrevVertex
E49F AND A
E4A0 PUSH IX
E4A2 JR NZ,$E4C8
E4A4 LD B,$03
E4A6 LD A,(IX+$00)
E4A9 CP (IX-$06)
E4AC JR NZ,$E4C8
E4AE INC IX
E4B0 DJNZ $E4A6
E4B2 POP IX
E4B4 CALL $E4E9
E4B7 LD ($E2E3),HL set VertexCrdX
E4BA CALL $E4E9
E4BD LD ($E2E5),HL
E4C0 CALL $E4E9
E4C3 LD ($E2E7),HL
E4C6 JR $E4DC
E4C8 POP IX
E4CA CALL $E52A rotate one vector coordinate
E4CD LD ($E2E3),HL set VertexCrdX
E4D0 CALL $E52A rotate one vector coordinate
E4D3 LD ($E2E5),HL
E4D6 CALL $E52A rotate one vector coordinate
E4D9 LD ($E2E7),HL
E4DC XOR A
E4DD LD ($E5C8),A set VertexCrdXlow
E4E0 LD ($E5C9),A
E4E3 LD ($E5CA),A
E4E6 JP $E607
E4E9: RotateCrdN_equ_Prev
Used by the routine at E49C.
Reuse the previous vertex's rotated coordinate on one axis, for a vertex whose raw magnitude bytes are identical to the previous one (see E49C). Rather than recomputing the three matrix-row products from scratch, it takes the previous vertex's already-stashed per-term products from (MatrixRotate+$12), re-signs each one to account for any sign difference between this vertex's and the previous vertex's coordinate on this axis (via XOR of the sign bits), and re-sums them via E424.
In: ix - address of this vertex's coordinate (and its sign byte at ix+3)
iy - address of the previous vertex's stashed per-term products
Out: hl - new vertex coordinate
iy=iy+6
de,bc,a - undefined
E4E9 LD A,(IX+$03)
E4EC XOR (IX-$03)
E4EF LD B,A
E4F0 LD L,(IY+$12)
E4F3 LD H,(IY+$13)
E4F6 LD E,(IY+$14)
E4F9 LD D,(IY+$15)
E4FC XOR H
E4FD AND $80
E4FF LD H,A
E500 LD (IY+$13),H
E503 RL B
E505 LD A,B
E506 XOR D
E507 AND $80
E509 LD D,A
E50A LD (IY+$15),D
E50D CALL $E424 signed addition
E510 LD L,A
E511 LD E,(IY+$16)
E514 LD D,(IY+$17)
E517 LD A,B
E518 RLA
E519 XOR D
E51A AND $80
E51C LD D,A
E51D LD (IY+$17),D
E520 CALL $E424 signed addition
E523 LD L,A
E524 LD DE,$0006
E527 ADD IY,DE
E529 RET
E52A: RotateCrdN
Used by the routines at E49C and EA03.
Rotate one vector coordinate
In: ix - start address of the vector (normal) coordinates
iy =MatrixRotate+n address of a row in the rotation matrix
Out: (MatrixRotate+$12) - matrix of products of the vector coordinate and an element
of the rotation matrix
hl - new vector coordinate (vector multiplied by a row of the rotation matrix)
iy=iy+6
de = $0006
bc,a - undefined
E52A LD B,(IX+$00)
E52D LD C,(IY+$01)
E530 RES 7,C
E532 CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E535 LD L,A
E536 LD A,(IX+$03)
E539 XOR (IY+$01)
E53C AND $80
E53E LD H,A
E53F LD (IY+$12),L
E542 LD (IY+$13),H
E545 EX DE,HL
E546 LD B,(IX+$01)
E549 LD C,(IY+$03)
E54C RES 7,C
E54E PUSH DE
E54F CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E552 POP DE
E553 LD L,A
E554 LD A,(IX+$03)
E557 RLA
E558 XOR (IY+$03)
E55B AND $80
E55D LD H,A
E55E LD (IY+$14),L
E561 LD (IY+$15),H
E564 CALL $E424 signed addition
E567 LD L,A
E568 EX DE,HL
E569 LD B,(IX+$02)
E56C LD C,(IY+$05)
E56F RES 7,C
E571 PUSH DE
E572 CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E575 POP DE
E576 LD L,A
E577 LD A,(IX+$03)
E57A RLA
E57B RLA
E57C XOR (IY+$05)
E57F AND $80
E581 LD H,A
E582 LD (IY+$16),L
E585 LD (IY+$17),H
E588 CALL $E424 signed addition
E58B LD L,A
E58C LD DE,$0006
E58F ADD IY,DE
E591 RET
E592: Div_C_DEc_HLa
Used by the routines at E008 and E5CB.
Division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if cde>ahl -> c=$FF)
In: de.c - dividend
hl.a - divisor
Out: 0.c=dec/hla
cde>ahl -> c=$FF
hl,de,b,a - undefined
E592 LD B,$09
E594 DEC B
E595 JR Z,$E5AD
E597 RLA
E598 ADC HL,HL
E59A JR C,$E5A9
E59C RL C
E59E RL E
E5A0 RL D
E5A2 JR NC,$E594
E5A4 RR D
E5A6 RR E
E5A8 SCF
E5A9 RR H
E5AB RR L
E5AD LD A,D
E5AE LD C,$FF
E5B0 CP H
E5B1 RET NC
E5B2 LD B,H
E5B3 DEC C
E5B4: CalcDeltaCrdLine2
Used by the routine at F0CC.
Calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
In: a - smaller coordinate difference
b - larger coordinate difference
c=$FE
e=$00
Out: c - increment coefficient along the shorter axis
E5B4 SLA E
E5B6 RLA
E5B7 JR C,$E5C1
E5B9 CP B
E5BA JR NC,$E5C1
E5BC SLA C
E5BE RET NC
E5BF JR $E5B4 calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
E5C1 SUB B
E5C2 SCF
E5C3 RL C
E5C5 RET NC
E5C6 JR $E5B4 calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
E5C8: Data block at E5C8
VertexCrdXlow E5C8 DEFB $00 vertex coordinates after OCS rotation (low byte)
VertexCrdYlow E5C9 DEFB $00
VertexCrdZlow E5CA DEFB $00
E5CB: CalcPrnCrdVertex
Used by the routine at E2F1.

In: ix - vertex address
(sp-2) - object slot address
E5CB PUSH IY
E5CD LD A,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E5D0 CP $04
E5D2 JP NC,$E49C distance greater than 4
E5D5 LD A,(IX+$00)
E5D8 OR (IX+$01)
E5DB OR (IX+$02)
E5DE LD B,$FF
E5E0 INC B

E5E2 JR NC,$E5E0
E5E4 LD A,B
E5E5 LD ($E2EF),A set VertexIncFactor
E5E8 AND A
E5E9 JP Z,$E49C distance greater than 4
E5EC CALL $E353 rotate one vertex coordinate

E5F5 CALL $E353 rotate one vertex coordinate

E5FB LD ($E2E5),HL
E5FE CALL $E353 rotate one vertex coordinate
E601 LD ($E5CA),A
E604 LD ($E2E7),HL
This entry point is used by the routine at E49C.
E607 XOR A
E608 LD ($E2F0),A set VisiblePrevVertex
E60B POP IY
E60D POP HL
E60E EX (SP),IX
E610 PUSH HL
E611 LD L,(IX+$00)
E614 LD H,(IX+$01)
E617 LD B,(IX+$02)
E61A LD DE,($E2E3) get VertexCrdX
E61E LD A,($E5C8) get VertexCrdXlow
E621 RLA
E622 PUSH AF
E623 CALL $E478 add the object coordinate and the vertex coordinate
E626 LD ($E2E3),HL set VertexCrdX
E629 LD ($E2EC),A set VertexSignCrdX
E62C POP AF
E62D LD ($E5C8),A set VertexCrdXlow
E630 LD L,(IX+$03)
E633 LD H,(IX+$04)
E636 LD B,(IX+$05)
E639 LD DE,($E2E5)
E63D LD A,($E5C9)
E640 RLA
E641 PUSH AF
E642 CALL $E478 add the object coordinate and the vertex coordinate
E645 LD ($E2E5),HL
E648 LD ($E2ED),A
E64B POP AF

E655 LD B,(IX+$08)

E65C LD A,($E5CA)
E65F RLA
E660 PUSH AF
E661 CALL $E478 add the object coordinate and the vertex coordinate
E664 LD ($E2E7),HL
E667 LD ($E2EE),A
E66A POP AF
E66B LD ($E5CA),A

E671 BIT 1,A
E673 JR NZ,$E6AA
E675 LD DE,($E2E3) get VertexCrdX
E679 LD A,($E5C8) get VertexCrdXlow
E67C LD C,A
E67D LD A,($E5CA)
E680 CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)

E687 LD DE,($E2E5)
E68B LD HL,($E2E7)
E68E LD A,($E5C9)
E691 LD C,A
E692 LD A,($E5CA)
E695 CALL $E592 division: 0.c=cde/ahl (if dec>hla -> c=$FF)
E698 LD A,C
E699 LD ($E2EA),A set CrdYProjection
E69C LD A,($E2EC) get VertexSignCrdX
E69F RLA

E6A2 LD A,($E2EE)
E6A5 RLA
E6A6 RL B
E6A8 JR $E6DD

E6AB LD HL,($E2E3) get VertexCrdX

E6E1 RL C

E6EF LD ($E2EB),A set CrdXYsign
E6F2 LD A,($E2E2) get PointTablCrdPrn

E70E RRA
E70F LD A,($E2EB) get CrdXYsign

E716 LD (HL),A
E717 POP AF
E718 EX (SP),IX
This entry point is used by the routine at E2F1.
E71A LD BC,$0006
E71D ADD IX,BC
E71F INC DE
E720 DEC A
E721 JP NZ,$E306

E729: PrintObject
Used by the routine at E175.
Print the object
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object descriptor
Out: de=$0004
b=$00
iy,hl,c,a - undefined
Walks the edge table four bytes at a time, skipping an edge whose distance-cull value says it is too far away to bother with, then skipping it again unless E338 says at least one of its two bordering faces is currently visible. Because visibility is tested per-edge against the same face-visibility flags computed once per frame by E923, an edge between two faces where at least one is front-facing gets drawn even if the other is back-facing - wireframe rendering shows silhouette and near-side edges, not a strict hidden-line-removed result.
E729 LD E,(IY+$0E)
E72C LD D,(IY+$0F) de - offset to the edges
E72F LD B,(IY+$04) b number of edges
E732 PUSH IY
E734 EX (SP),IX
E736 ADD IX,DE start address of the edges
E738 PUSH BC
E739 LD A,(IX+$00)
E73C CP $1F
E73E JR Z,$E747
E740 LD C,A
E741 LD A,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E744 CP C
E745 JR NC,$E76D next edge
E747 LD A,(IX+$03)
E74A CALL $E338 check face visibility
E74D JR Z,$E76D next edge
E74F LD A,(IX+$01)
E752 CALL $E778 get a vertex's print coordinates from the table by index
E755 JR NZ,$E76D next edge
E757 LD C,A
E758 PUSH DE
E759 LD A,(IX+$02)
E75C CALL $E778 get a vertex's print coordinates from the table by index
E75F EX DE,HL
E760 POP DE
E761 JR NZ,$E76D next edge
E763 RRCA
E764 RRA
E765 RL C
E767 RLA
E768 RL C
E76A CALL $EE10 Print a line in the buffer???
E76D POP BC next edge
E76E LD DE,$0004
E771 ADD IX,DE
E773 DJNZ $E738
E775 POP IX
E777 RET
E778: GetPrnCrdVertex
Used by the routines at E175 and E729.
Get a vertex's print coordinates from the table by index
In: a - vertex number
Out: de - vertex print coordinates
a - coordinate signs
z =1 the vertex is printed
hl - undefined
E778 LD B,A
E779 ADD A,A
E77A LD HL,$E222 +0,1 coordinates +2 direction
E77D JR NC,$E780
E77F INC H
E780 ADD A,B
E781 JR NC,$E784
E783 INC H
E784 ADD A,L
E785 LD L,A
E786 JR NC,$E789
E788 INC H
E789 LD D,(HL)
E78A INC HL
E78B LD E,(HL)
E78C INC HL
E78D LD A,(HL)
E78E BIT 7,A
E790 RET
E791: CorrAxirY_SKO
Used by the routines at DA1D and DFDD.
Align the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
In: ix - address of the object slot
Out: ix=ix+2
iy=iy+2
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
E791 PUSH IX
E793 PUSH IX
E795 EX (SP),IY
E797 LD DE,$0009
E79A ADD IX,DE
E79C LD E,$0F
E79E ADD IY,DE
E7A0 CALL $FB1F dot product of two vectors with one vector's direction flipped
E7A3 LD E,A
E7A4 CALL $E7B2 align coordinate n of the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
E7A7 CALL $E7B2 align coordinate n of the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
E7AA CALL $E7B2 align coordinate n of the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
E7AD POP IY
E7AF POP IX
E7B1 RET
E7B2: CorrCrdN_AxisY
Used by the routine at E791.
Align coordinate n of the Y axis to be orthogonal to the X axis
In: ix - coordinate n of the object's X-axis vector
iy - coordinate n of the object's Y-axis vector
e - dot product of the object's X-axis and Y-axis vectors
Out: ix=ix+2
iy=iy+2
E7B2 LD C,(IX+$01)
E7B5 LD B,E
E7B6 SLA B
E7B8 SLA C
E7BA CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E7BD LD C,A
E7BE LD A,E
E7BF XOR (IX+$01)
E7C2 RLA
E7C3 RR C
E7C5 LD B,(IY+$01)
E7C8 LD A,B
E7C9 XOR C
E7CA SLA C
E7CC RL H
E7CE SLA B
E7D0 RL H
E7D2 RLA
E7D3 LD A,B
E7D4 JR C,$E7DD
E7D6 ADD A,C
E7D7 JR NC,$E7E4
E7D9 LD A,$FF
E7DB JR $E7E4
E7DD SUB C
E7DE JR NC,$E7E4
E7E0 NEG
E7E2 RR H
E7E4 RR H
E7E6 RRA
E7E7 LD (IY+$01),A
E7EA INC IX
E7EC INC IX
E7EE INC IY
E7F0 INC IY
E7F2 RET
E7F3: CalcMatrixRotate
Used by the routine at E175.
Build the OCS rotation matrix
In: ix - address of the object descriptor slot
Out: iy=MatrixRotate
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
Each of the 9 matrix elements is a cross-multiplication of two pairs of the object's stored orientation vector components (e.g. one element is (ix+$0C)*(ix+$14) - (ix+$0E)*(ix+$12)-style), computed via E45D with sign tracked separately via XOR of the two operands' sign bits rather than relying on two's-complement arithmetic. The orientation itself is stored as three vectors (nine bytes: three 2-byte sign-magnitude values per vector) in the object slot rather than as Euler angles - effectively two rows of a 3x3 rotation matrix plus redundancy used for renormalization (see E791/DCA3).
E7F3 LD IY,$E2BC
E7F7 LD B,(IX+$0C)
E7FA LD C,(IX+$14)
E7FD LD D,(IX+$0E)
E800 LD E,(IX+$12)
E803 CALL $E849
E806 LD (IX+$16),H
E809 LD (IY+$01),H
E80C LD B,(IX+$0E)
E80F LD (IY+$11),B
E812 LD C,(IX+$10)
E815 LD (IY+$03),C
E818 LD D,(IX+$0A)
E81B LD E,(IX+$14)
E81E LD (IY+$0F),E
E821 CALL $E849
E824 LD (IX+$18),H
E827 LD (IY+$07),H
E82A LD B,(IX+$0A)
E82D LD (IY+$05),B
E830 LD C,(IX+$12)
E833 LD (IY+$09),C
E836 LD D,(IX+$0C)
E839 LD (IY+$0B),D
E83C LD E,(IX+$10)
E83F CALL $E849
E842 LD (IX+$1A),H
E845 LD (IY+$0D),H
E848 RET
E849 SLA B
E84B RRA
E84C XOR C
E84D SLA C
E84F AND $80
E851 LD H,A
E852 SLA D
E854 RRA
E855 XOR E
E856 SLA E
E858 AND $80
E85A XOR H
E85B SLA H
E85D RRA
E85E XOR $80
E860 PUSH AF
E861 PUSH DE
E862 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
E865 POP BC
E866 PUSH HL
E867 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
E86A POP DE
E86B POP AF
E86C BIT 6,A
E86E JR Z,$E878
E870 ADD HL,DE
E871 JR NC,$E885
E873 LD HL,$FFFF
E876 JR $E885
E878 EX DE,HL
E879 AND A
E87A SBC HL,DE
E87C JR NC,$E885
E87E ADD HL,DE
E87F EX DE,HL
E880 AND A
E881 SBC HL,DE
E883 XOR $80
E885 RLA
E886 RR H
E888 RR L
E88A RET

E88B PUSH IX
E88D LD IX,$0000
E891 LD B,A
E892 PUSH BC
E893 PUSH DE
E894 PUSH BC
E895 PUSH DE
E896 PUSH BC
E897 PUSH DE
E898 LD B,E
E899 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C

E89D ADD IX,DE
E89F POP DE

E8A2 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C

E8AF POP BC

E8CA POP DE
E8CB ADD HL,DE
E8CC POP IX
E8CE RET
E8CF: Div_BC_HLAdivDE
Used by the routines at 89E8, 8C29, DC31 and DC53.
Fixed-point division hl.a/de
In: hla - dividend
de - divisor
Out: b.c - =hl.a/de
E8CF LD BC,$FFFE
E8D2 ADD A,A
E8D3 RL L
E8D5 RL H
E8D7 JR C,$E8E5
E8D9 SBC HL,DE
E8DB JR NC,$E8E8
E8DD ADD HL,DE
E8DE SLA C
E8E0 RL B
E8E2 RET NC

E8E6 SBC HL,DE
E8E8 SCF
E8E9 RL C
E8EB RL B
E8ED RET NC
E8EE JR $E8D2
E8F0: CalcDeltaCrd
Used by the routines at 712C and F928.
Compute the distance along one axis
In: hl - X/Y/Z coordinate of the first object (7,h sign)
de - X/Y/Z coordinate of the second object (7,d sign)
Out: 7,h =1 the first object's coordinate is smaller (to the left)
hl - distance along the given axis /2
de,bc,a - undefined
E8F0 LD A,D
E8F1 XOR $80
E8F3 LD D,A
E8F4: AddSign_HL_DE
Used by the routine at E9AE.
Signed addition
In: hl - first addend (7,h sign)
de - second addend (7,d sign)
Out: hl - sum/2 (7,h sign)
de,bc,a - undefined
E8F4 LD A,H
E8F5 XOR D
E8F6 LD B,H
E8F7 LD C,D
E8F8 RES 7,H
E8FA RES 7,D
E8FC AND $80
E8FE JR NZ,$E903
E900 ADD HL,DE
E901 JR $E90D
E903 SBC HL,DE
E905 JR NC,$E90D
E907 ADD HL,DE
E908 EX DE,HL
E909 AND A
E90A SBC HL,DE
E90C LD B,C
E90D SRL H
E90F RR L
E911 BIT 7,B
E913 RET Z
E914 SET 7,H
E916 RET
E917: Data block at E917
AdrSlotObj E917 DEFW $0000 address of the object descriptor in space
IY_dFactor E919 DEFB $00 (iy+$13)
AddNormalCrdX E91A DEFW $0000 sum of the normal vector and the object's vector (coordinates)
AddNormalCrdY E91C DEFW $0000
AddNormalCrdZ E91E DEFW $0000
NormalCrdX E920 DEFB $00 normal vector after OCS rotation
NormalCrdY E921 DEFB $00
NormalCrdZ E922 DEFB $00
E923: CalcVisibleVerges
Used by the routine at E175.
Build the face-visibility table from the normals
In: ix - address of the object descriptor slot
iy - address of the object data
(MatrixRotate) - OCS rotation matrix
Out: (MatrixRotate+$12) - matrix of products of the vector coordinate and an element
of the rotation matrix
(VergesVisible) - face-visibility table from normals
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
E923 PUSH IX
E925 PUSH IY
E927 PUSH IY
E929 EX (SP),IX ix=iy
E92B POP HL hl=ix
E92C LD ($E917),HL set AdrSlotObj
E92F LD D,(IX+$11)
E932 LD E,(IX+$10) de offset to the normals
E935 ADD IX,DE ix=start address of the normals
E937 LD DE,$E2AC face visibility =0 not visible (normal points away from the player)
E93A LD A,(IY+$13)
E93D LD ($E919),A set IY_dFactor
E940 LD A,(IY+$05)
E943 AND A
E944 LD IY,$E2BC
E948 JR NZ,$E94E a - number of normals de =VergesVisible ix start address of the normals iy =MatrixRotate
E94A INC A
E94B LD (DE),A
E94C JR $E9A9 exit
E94E PUSH AF a - number of normals de =VergesVisible ix start address of the normals iy =MatrixRotate
E94F PUSH DE
E950 LD A,(IX+$03)
E953 AND $1F
E955 CP $1F
E957 JR Z,$E964
E959 LD B,A
E95A LD A,($E2E0) get DistanceToObj
E95D CP B
E95E JR C,$E964
E960 POP DE
E961 XOR A
E962 JR $E99E next face
E964 PUSH IY
E966 LD HL,($E917) get AdrSlotObj
E969 PUSH HL
E96A CALL $EA03
E96D LD ($E920),A set NormalCrdX
E970 LD ($E91A),HL set AddNormalCrdX
E973 CALL $EA03
E976 LD ($E921),A
E979 LD ($E91C),HL
E97C CALL $EA03
E97F LD ($E922),A
E982 LD ($E91E),HL
E985 POP HL
E986 PUSH IX
E988 LD IY,$E91A sum of the normal vector and the object's vector (coordinates)
E98C LD IX,$E920 normal vector after OCS rotation
E990 CALL $E9AE dot product of vectors
E993 POP IX
E995 POP IY
E997 POP DE
E998 XOR A
E999 SLA H
E99B JR NC,$E99E next face
E99D INC A
E99E LD (DE),A next face
E99F INC DE
E9A0 LD BC,$0004
E9A3 ADD IX,BC
E9A5 POP AF
E9A6 DEC A
E9A7 JR NZ,$E94E a - number of normals de =VergesVisible ix start address of the normals iy =MatrixRotate
E9A9 POP IY exit
E9AB POP IX
E9AD RET
E9AE: ScalarMultVectors16_8
Used by the routine at E923.
Dot product of vectors
In: iy - start address of the first vector (OCS origin vector + normal vector)
ix - start address of the second vector (normal vector in the OCS)
Out: h.l = crdZ1*crdZ2 + crdY1*crdY2 + crdX1*crdX2
de,bc,a - undefined
E9AE LD E,(IY+$01)
E9B1 LD C,(IY+$00)
E9B4 LD A,(IX+$00)
E9B7 CALL $E9E4 Multiplication: h.l = e.c * 0.a * 2 (7,h - sign)
E9BA PUSH HL
E9BB LD E,(IY+$03)
E9BE LD C,(IY+$02)
E9C1 LD A,(IX+$01)
E9C4 CALL $E9E4 Multiplication: h.l = e.c * 0.a * 2 (7,h - sign)
E9C7 PUSH HL
E9C8 LD E,(IY+$05)
E9CB LD C,(IY+$04)
E9CE LD A,(IX+$02)
E9D1 CALL $E9E4 Multiplication: h.l = e.c * 0.a * 2 (7,h - sign)
E9D4 POP DE
E9D5 CALL $E8F4 signed addition
E9D8 ADC HL,HL
E9DA BIT 7,B
E9DC JR Z,$E9E0
E9DE SET 7,H
E9E0 POP DE
E9E1 JP $E8F4 signed addition
E9E4: Mult_HL_EC_A
Used by the routine at E9AE.
Multiplication: h.l = e.c * 0.a * 2 (7,h - sign)
E9E4 LD B,A
E9E5 SLA B
E9E7 AND $80
E9E9 XOR E
E9EA LD E,A
E9EB CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
E9EE LD D,E
E9EF LD E,A
E9F0 LD C,D
E9F1 RES 7,C
E9F3 PUSH DE
E9F4 CALL $E45D Approximate table-based multiplication HL=B*C
E9F7 POP DE
E9F8 LD A,D
E9F9 LD D,$00
E9FB ADD HL,DE
E9FC ADD HL,HL
E9FD RLA
E9FE RR H
EA00 RR L
EA02 RET
EA03: RotateCrdN_Normal

In: ix - start address of the normal's coordinates
iy =MatrixRotate+n address of a row in the rotation matrix
(sp) - address of the object coordinate
Out: (MatrixRotate+$12) - matrix of products of the vector coordinate and an element
of the rotation matrix
hl - sum of the object vector and normal coordinates
a - rotated coordinate n of the normal (7,a=sign)
(sp)=(sp)+3
iy=iy+6

EA51: CollisionWithObj
Used by the routine at DA1D.
Check for collision of the object with the player, and handle it
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object data
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
EA51 LD A,($707A) get PlayerGameOver
EA54 AND A
EA55 RET NZ
EA56 LD D,$00
EA58 LD E,(IY+$01)
EA5B BIT 0,(IX+$26)
EA5F JR Z,$EA69
EA61 LD A,(IX+$25)
EA64 AND A
EA65 JR NZ,$EA69
EA67 LD D,$01 d=1, this is a missile and it was fired at the player
EA69 BIT 1,(IX+$26)
EA6D JR Z,$EA71
EA6F LD E,$B4
EA71 LD L,(IX+$00)
EA74 LD H,(IX+$01)
EA77 SBC HL,DE
EA79 RET NC along the X axis, far from collision
EA7A AND A
EA7B LD L,(IX+$03)
EA7E LD H,(IX+$04)
EA81 SBC HL,DE
EA83 RET NC along the Y axis, far from collision
EA84 AND A
EA85 LD L,(IX+$06)
EA88 LD H,(IX+$07)
EA8B SBC HL,DE
EA8D RET NC along the Z axis, far from collision
EA8E LD A,(IX+$08)
EA91 AND $7F
EA93 RET NZ
EA94 BIT 1,(IX+$26)
EA98 JR Z,$EAD8 collision with the object???
EA9A BIT 7,(IX+$05) object is above the Y axis
EA9E JP Z,$EB23 destroy the object (we collide)
EAA1 LD A,($A84A) object is below the Y axis, we pick up cargo
EAA4 AND A
EAA5 JP Z,$EB23 destroy the object (we collide)
EAA8 XOR A we pick up the canister
EAA9 LD (IX+$22),A
EAAC CALL $EB65 object has disappeared from space (destroyed/flown off). Untarget the missile and destroy any missiles fired at this object
EAAF LD A,($A83E) get PilotFreeCargo
EAB2 AND A
EAB3 JR Z,$EB23 destroy the object (we collide)
EAB5 LD A,(IY+$00)
EAB8 AND $F0
EABA JR Z,$EAC3

EABD RRCA
EABE RRCA
EABF RRCA

EACE JR $EAD5

b =$00

EB8E POP IY

EB95 RET NZ

EB9A RET NZ

ED24: IntRnd_A2
Used by the routines at E175, EA51, EBC7, F2FB, F3CE, F44A, F4E6, F4FF, F61D, F733 and F9FA.
Random number
A - result
The entire state is a single 16-bit word held in (word_ED35), seeded at assembly time to a fixed non-zero value. Each call doubles it with carry propagating from the low byte into the high byte and back around (a=h; adc a,a; b=a; a=adc(a,l); h=a; l=b) - a simple additive/shift-based generator, not a table lookup or anything hardware-derived. Its output at any point in the game depends entirely on the exact sequence of PRNG-consuming calls that happened before it, with no direct tie to elapsed real time or frame count. This one shared generator feeds every "roll a random byte" need in the game: spawn-position jitter (F2FB), laser-fire timing, enemy AI decisions, and more.
ED24 PUSH HL
ED25 PUSH BC
ED26 LD HL,($ED35)
ED29 LD A,H
ED2A ADC A,A
ED2B LD B,A
ED2C ADC A,L
ED2D LD H,A
ED2E LD L,B
ED2F LD ($ED35),HL
ED32 POP BC
ED33 POP HL
ED34 RET
ED35: Data block at ED35
ED35 DEFW $F562
ED37: Process_LaserECM2
Used by the routine at ED3F.
ED37 XOR A
ED38 LD ($7043),A set HighAngleDU
ED3B LD ($7044),A set HighDeltaSpeed
ED3E RET

ED67 LD C,E
ED68 CALL $EE10 Print a line in the buffer???

ED6E LD DE,$0008
ED71 LD C,D
ED72 CALL $EE10 Print a line in the buffer???
ED75 LD HL,$0010
ED78 LD DE,$0008
ED7B LD C,$05
ED7D CALL $EE10 Print a line in the buffer???
ED80 CALL $EBC7 player fires the laser
ED83 LD A,($A872) +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed
ED86 BIT 7,A
ED88 JR Z,$ED90
ED8A LD A,($A871) get PilotMission3
ED8D CP $04
ED8F RET Z
ED90 LD HL,$FC11 7,=1 - C -- Docking Computer 6,=1 - Q -- Escape Capsule 5,=1 - W -- Energy Bomb 4,=1 - E -- ECM System 3,=1 - U -- UnTarget 2,=1 - F -- Fire Missile 1,=1 - T -- Target Missile 0,=1 - A -- Fire Laser
ED93 LD B,(HL)
ED94 RES 4,(HL)
ED96 LD A,($A847) get PilotECMSystem
ED99 LD C,A
ED9A LD A,($DA16) get TimeWorkECM
ED9D AND A
ED9E JP Z,$BDE4 turn on the ECM system, if available
EDA1 DEC A
EDA2 LD ($DA16),A set TimeWorkECM
EDA5 JR NZ,$EDA9
EDA7 RES 7,C
EDA9 LD HL,$707D which sound to play =$00 no sound (silent) 0,=1 energy bomb exploded 1,=1 missile launched 2,=1 colliding with an object/object destroyed 3,=1 laser fired 4,=1 laser fired 5,=1 ECM working 6,=1 hit by laser on the player/docking damage 7,=1
EDAC SET 5,(HL)
EDAE LD A,C
EDAF AND A
EDB0 RET Z
EDB1 LD ($A847),A set PilotECMSystem
EDB4 LD A,($DA17) get DamageEnergyUnit
EDB7 INC A
EDB8 LD ($DA17),A set DamageEnergyUnit
EDBB RET
EDBC: Data block at EDBC
A 4-row lookup (rows: 0,4,2,3 / 0,4,6,5 / 0,8,2,1 / 0,8,6,7) mapping a point's raw 2-bit sign-quadrant code onto a clip-region code (0 = on-screen; 1/2/4/8 and combinations = off-screen in a specific direction). A second 16-row region of this same table (following the first 4 rows) is used to combine both points' codes (as b*8+c) to decide whether an off-screen/off-screen line can possibly still cross the screen.
TablQuadrant EDBC DEFB $00,$04,$02,$03,$00,$04,$06,$05
EDC4 DEFB $00,$08,$02,$01,$00,$08,$06,$07
EDCC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$02,$01,$02,$00,$00
EDD4 DEFB $00,$00,$00,$02,$02,$01,$02,$02
EDDC DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$02,$01,$02
EDE4 DEFB $02,$02,$00,$00,$00,$02,$02,$01
EDEC DEFB $01,$02,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$02
EDF4 DEFB $02,$01,$02,$02,$00,$00,$00,$02
EDFC DEFB $00,$02,$01,$02,$00,$00,$00,$00
EE04 DEFB $00,$02,$02,$01,$02,$02,$00,$00
VarForClipLine2 EE0C DEFW $0000
VarForClipLine1 EE0E DEFW $0000
EE10: PrnLineInBuf_clip
Used by the routines at E175, E729, EBC7 and ED3F.
Entry point for every line drawn in the game (ship edges, compass needle, orbit circles, etc). After two cheap trivial-reject checks (endpoints equal to $FF mean "don't print this vertex"), builds a quadrant code per point via TablQuadrant and dispatches to one of three cases: both points on-screen (draw directly), one point off-screen (PrnLine_OneCrdClip clips against a single edge), or both off-screen (the two quadrant codes are combined and looked up a second time to see if the line can possibly cross the screen at all; if not, it's discarded with no pixels drawn, otherwise PrnLine_TwoCrdClip clips against two edges).
0-1,c quadrant for printing the first point
2-3,c quadrant for printing the second point
hl - coordinates of the first point (x1y1)
de - coordinates of the second point (x2y2)
EE10 LD A,H
EE11 AND E
EE12 INC A
EE13 RET Z x1=y2=$FF
EE14 LD A,L
EE15 AND D
EE16 INC A
EE17 RET Z x2=y1=$FF
EE18 PUSH DE
EE19 PUSH HL
EE1A PUSH BC
EE1B LD A,C
EE1C AND $0C
EE1E LD B,A 2-3,b - signs for x2y2
EE1F LD A,D
EE20 CP $80
EE22 JR C,$EE26
EE24 SET 0,B 0,b =1 x2>=$80
EE26 LD A,E
EE27 CP $40 '@'
EE29 JR C,$EE2D
EE2B SET 1,B 1,b =1 y2>=$40
EE2D LD A,C

EE32 LD C,A 2-3,c - signs for x1y1

EE38 SET 0,C 0,c =1 x1>=$80
EE3A LD A,L

EE3D JR C,$EE41
EE3F SET 1,C 1,c =1 y1>=$40
EE41 LD HL,$EDBC table for determining a point's quadrant from its print coordinates

EE45 AND $03
EE47 JR Z,$EE87 x2y2 within the screen
EE49 LD A,B
EE4A ADD A,L
EE4B LD L,A

EE4E INC H
EE4F LD B,(HL) b - quadrant of point x2y2
EE50 LD HL,$EDBC table for determining a point's quadrant from its print coordinates

EE73 POP HL the whole line is outside the boundaries
EE74 POP HL
EE75 POP HL
EE76 RET
EE77 POP BC swap the coordinates of the points, x1y1 within the screen, x2y2 outside it

EE7F SRL C
EE81 OR C

EE85 JR $EE8F x2y2 within the screen, x1y1 outside it

F0BE: Mult_C_hiAmulCadd80
Used by the routine at EE10.
Multiplication c=high(a*c+$80)
F0BE PUSH DE
F0BF PUSH HL
F0C0 LD B,A
F0C1 CALL $E45D computes: ((b+c)/2)^2 - ((b-c)/2)^2 should be: ((b+c)^2 - (b-c)^2)/4
F0C4 LD DE,$0080
F0C7 ADD HL,DE
F0C8 LD C,H
F0C9 POP HL
F0CA POP DE
F0CB RET
F0CC: CalcDeltaCrdLine
Used by the routines at EE10, F130 and F175.
Calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
In: a - smaller coordinate difference
b - larger coordinate difference
Out: c - increment coefficient along the shorter axis
F0CC LD C,$FE
F0CE PUSH DE
F0CF LD E,$00
F0D1 CALL $E5B4 calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
F0D4 POP DE
F0D5 RET
F0D6: CalcFirstAdr4Line
Used by the routine at EE10.
Compute the print address and data for setting the first point
In: de - coordinates of the second point (x2y2)
7,b =1 line length along X > $FF
=0 line length along X =< $FF
6,b =1 line length along Y > $FF
=0 line length along Y =< $FF
4,b =1 dX >= dY
4,b =0 dX < dY
3,b =1 coordinate X2 is left of coordinate X1
=0 coordinate X2 is right of coordinate X1
2,b =1 coordinate Y2 is below coordinate Y1
=0 coordinate Y2 is above coordinate Y1
c - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
Out: d' - byte for setting a point
e' - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
hl' - print address on screen/in buffer
b' - number of points in the first character cell along Y
a,c' - undefined
other registers are unchanged
The whole routine runs in the alternate register set (push, exx in, compute, exx back), so the caller's own bc/de are left completely undisturbed; the real results only become visible to the caller after it also does an exx. It recovers actual X/Y from the sign-coded deltas via X = $7F +/- dX and Y = $3F +/- dY (add if the sign bit is clear, subtract if set), i.e. reconstructing bitmap coordinates from a 127/63-centred reference point rather than from 0.
F0D6 PUSH BC
F0D7 PUSH BC
F0D8 PUSH DE
F0D9 EXX
F0DA POP BC
F0DB POP DE
F0DC BIT 1,D
F0DE JR NZ,$F0E5
F0E0 LD A,$7F
F0E2 ADD A,B
F0E3 JR $F0E8
F0E5 LD A,$7F
F0E7 SUB B
F0E8 LD B,A
F0E9 BIT 0,D
F0EB JR Z,$F0F2
F0ED LD A,$3F '?'
F0EF ADD A,C
F0F0 JR $F0F5
F0F2 LD A,$3F '?'
F0F4 SUB C
F0F5 LD C,B
F0F6 LD B,A
F0F7 LD A,C
F0F8 AND $07
F0FA LD D,B
F0FB LD B,A
F0FC INC B
F0FD LD A,$01
F0FF RRCA

F103 LD D,A
F104 SRL C
F106 SRL C
F108 SRL C
F10A LD A,B
F10B RRA
F10C RRA
F10D RRA
F10E AND $18
F110 LD H,A
F111 LD A,B
F112 AND $07
F114 OR H
Self-modified operand: patched elsewhere to $40 or $C0 depending on whether the destination address being computed is the real screen or the off-screen buffer (bit 7 of the buffer's high byte selects the buffer, matching the third/row/column layout of the real screen but relocated into high memory).
F115 OR $C0
F117 LD H,A
F118 LD A,B
F119 AND $38 '8'
F11B ADD A,A
F11C ADD A,A
F11D OR C
F11E LD L,A
F11F LD A,H
F120 AND $07
F122 INC A
F123 LD B,A
F124 LD C,D
F125 POP AF
F126 BIT 2,A
F128 JR NZ,$F12E
F12A LD A,$09
F12C SUB B
F12D LD B,A
F12E EXX
F12F RET
F130: PrnLine_OneCrdClip
Used by the routine at EE10.
Print a line, clipped at the screen edge
In: de - coordinates of the second point (x2y2)
the on-screen point where printing starts
4,b =1 dX >= dY
4,b =0 dX < dY
3,b =1 coordinate X2 is left of coordinate X1
=0 coordinate X2 is right of coordinate X1
2,b =1 coordinate Y2 is below coordinate Y1
=0 coordinate Y2 is above coordinate Y1
1,b - sign of x2
0,b - sign of y2
c - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
hl' - print address on screen/in buffer
b' - number of points in the first character cell along Y
c' - byte for setting a point
e' - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
Out: line printed

F136 RRA

F175: CalcVars4Line
Used by the routines at EE10 and F130.
Determine the coordinate difference and print direction
In: hl - coordinates of the first point (x1y1)
de - coordinates of the second point (x2y2)
0,b - sign of y1
1,b - sign of x1
2,b - sign of y2
3,b - sign of x2
Out: 7,b =1 line length along X > $FF
=0 line length along X =< $FF
6,b =1 line length along Y > $FF
=0 line length along Y =< $FF
4,b =1 dX >= dY
4,b =0 dX < dY
3,b =1 coordinate X2 is left of coordinate X1
=0 coordinate X2 is right of coordinate X1
2,b =1 coordinate Y2 is below coordinate Y1
=0 coordinate Y2 is above coordinate Y1
1,b - sign of x2
0,b - sign of y2
c - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
h - coordinate difference along X
l - coordinate difference along Y
