F1D4 RRC B
F1D6 RR C
F1D8 LD A,H
F1D9 RRC B
F1DB RRA
F1DC CP C
F1DD JR C,$F1EA
F1DF SET 4,B
F1E1 PUSH BC
F1E2 BIT 7,B
F1E4 JR Z,$F1F4
F1E6 LD B,A
F1E7 LD A,C
F1E8 JR $F1FA
F1EA RES 4,B
F1EC PUSH BC
F1ED BIT 6,B
F1EF JR Z,$F1F8
F1F1 LD B,C
F1F2 JR $F1FA
F1F4 LD A,L
F1F5 LD B,H
F1F6 JR $F1FA
F1F8 LD A,H
F1F9 LD B,L
F1FA CALL $F0CC calculate the coordinate step coefficient when printing a line
F1FD LD A,C
F1FE POP BC
F1FF LD C,A
F200 RET
F201: PrnLineDown2Up_vert_Entry
F201 DEC H
This entry point is used by the routine at EE10.
PrnLineDown2Up_vert F202 LD A,(HL)
F203 OR C
F204 LD (HL),A
F205 DEC D
F206 RET Z
F207 LD A,E
F208 ADD A,$00
F20A LD E,A
F20B JR NC,$F211
F20D RRC C
F20F JR C,$F224
"boundary crossing" (every 8th row): because of the third-interleaved screen layout, advancing one pixel row is not a uniform +1/-1 to a linear address. Most of the time (within a character cell) it's just L +/- $20, but every 8th row this reloads b=8 and adjusts the high byte by +/-7 and the low byte by -/+$20 instead, with a further carry-driven -b correction to the high byte if that 8-row group also crosses a third boundary.
F211 DJNZ $F201
F213 LD B,$08
F215 LD A,H DownScrAdr_HL
F216 ADD A,$07
F218 LD H,A
F219 LD A,L
F21A SUB $20 ' '
F21C LD L,A
F21D JR NC,PrnLineDown2Up_vert
F21F LD A,H
F220 SUB B
F221 LD H,A
F222 JR PrnLineDown2Up_vert
self-modified operand: PrnLineInBuf_clip pokes the literal byte $2C (inc l) or $2D (dec l) into this exact spot before jumping in, choosing the stepping direction once, before the loop starts, rather than branching on every pixel of the hot per-pixel loop.
F224 INC L
F225 JP $F211
F228: PrnLineDown2Up_hor_Entry
Print a line bottom-to-top, dY<dX
In: hl - address in buffer/on screen
d - line length
e - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
b - number of points in the first character cell along Y
c - byte for setting the first point
Out: d =$00
hl,e,bc,a - undefined
self-modified operand: PrnLineInBuf_clip pokes the literal byte $2C (inc l) or $2D (dec l) into this exact spot before jumping in, choosing the stepping direction once, before the loop starts, rather than branching on every pixel of the hot per-pixel loop.
F228 INC L
F229 JP PrnLineDown2Up_hor
F22C RRC C
F22E JR C,$F228 print a line bottom-to-top, dY<dX
This entry point is used by the routine at EE10.
PrnLineDown2Up_hor F230 LD A,(HL)
F231 OR C
F232 LD (HL),A
F233 DEC D
F234 RET Z
F235 LD A,E
"boundary crossing" (every 8th row): because of the third-interleaved screen layout, advancing one pixel row is not a uniform +1/-1 to a linear address. Most of the time (within a character cell) it's just L +/- $20, but every 8th row this reloads b=8 and adjusts the high byte by +/-7 and the low byte by -/+$20 instead, with a further carry-driven -b correction to the high byte if that 8-row group also crosses a third boundary.
F236 ADD A,$00
F238 LD E,A
F239 JR NC,$F22C
F23B DEC H
F23C DJNZ $F22C
F23E LD B,$08
F240 LD A,H
F241 ADD A,B
F242 LD H,A
F243 LD A,L
F244 SUB $20 ' '
F246 LD L,A
F247 JP NC,$F22C
F24A LD A,H
F24B SUB B
F24C LD H,A
F24D JP $F22C
F250: PrnLineUp2Down_vert_Entry
F250 INC H
This entry point is used by the routine at EE10.
PrnLineUp2Down_vert F251 LD A,(HL) hl - address in the buffer c - data byte d - height e - X increment b - height of the first character cell
F252 OR C
F253 LD (HL),A
F254 DEC D
F255 RET Z
F256 LD A,E
F257 ADD A,$00
F259 LD E,A
F25A JR NC,$F260
F25C RRC C shift the data bit
F25E JR C,$F275
"boundary crossing" (every 8th row): because of the third-interleaved screen layout, advancing one pixel row is not a uniform +1/-1 to a linear address. Most of the time (within a character cell) it's just L +/- $20, but every 8th row this reloads b=8 and adjusts the high byte by +/-7 and the low byte by -/+$20 instead, with a further carry-driven -b correction to the high byte if that 8-row group also crosses a third boundary.
F260 DJNZ $F250
F262 LD B,$08
F264 LD A,H DownScrAdr_HL
F265 SUB $07
F267 LD H,A
F268 LD A,L
F269 ADD A,$20 ' '
F26B LD L,A
F26C JP NC,PrnLineUp2Down_vert hl - address in the buffer c - data byte d - height e - X increment b - height of the first character cell
F26F LD A,H
F270 ADD A,B
F271 LD H,A
F272 JP PrnLineUp2Down_vert hl - address in the buffer c - data byte d - height e - X increment b - height of the first character cell
self-modified operand: PrnLineInBuf_clip pokes the literal byte $2C (inc l) or $2D (dec l) into this exact spot before jumping in, choosing the stepping direction once, before the loop starts, rather than branching on every pixel of the hot per-pixel loop.
F275 INC L
F276 JP $F260
F279: PrnLineUp2Down_hor_Entry
Print a line top-to-bottom, dY<dX
In: hl - address in buffer/on screen
d - line length
e - coordinate increment coefficient along the shorter axis
b - number of points in the first character cell along Y
c - byte for setting the first point
Out: d =$00
hl,e,bc,a - undefined
F279 RRC C
F27B JR C,$F29D
This entry point is used by the routine at EE10.
PrnLineUp2Down_hor F27D LD A,(HL)
F27E OR C
F27F LD (HL),A
F280 DEC D
F281 RET Z
F282 LD A,E
"boundary crossing" (every 8th row): because of the third-interleaved screen layout, advancing one pixel row is not a uniform +1/-1 to a linear address. Most of the time (within a character cell) it's just L +/- $20, but every 8th row this reloads b=8 and adjusts the high byte by +/-7 and the low byte by -/+$20 instead, with a further carry-driven -b correction to the high byte if that 8-row group also crosses a third boundary.
F283 ADD A,$00
F285 LD E,A
F286 JR NC,$F279 print a line top-to-bottom, dY<dX
F288 INC H
F289 DJNZ $F279 print a line top-to-bottom, dY<dX
F28B LD B,$08
F28D LD A,H
F28E SUB B
F28F LD H,A
F290 LD A,L
F291 ADD A,$20 ' '
F293 LD L,A
F294 JP NC,$F279 print a line top-to-bottom, dY<dX
F297 LD A,H
F298 ADD A,B
F299 LD H,A
F29A JP $F279 print a line top-to-bottom, dY<dX
self-modified operand: PrnLineInBuf_clip pokes the literal byte $2C (inc l) or $2D (dec l) into this exact spot before jumping in, choosing the stepping direction once, before the loop starts, rather than branching on every pixel of the hot per-pixel loop.
F29D INC L
F29E JP PrnLineUp2Down_hor
F2A1: Data block at F2A1
PatternObject F2A1 DEFB $00,$32,$00,$00,$32,$00,$00,$32 position: X/Y/Z, each a 2-byte magnitude plus a 1-byte sign, preset to the fixed placeholder ($3200,0, $3200,0, $3200,0) - far from the origin, immediately overwritten by whatever spawn logic actually places the object.
F2A9 DEFB $00
F2AA DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$FF,$FF,$00,$00 remaining status/state bytes reset to a known baseline before any ship-specific or randomised data is layered on top: mostly zero, except offsets +$D,+$E,+$11,+$15,+$16 preset to $FF (the conventional "none"/"no target" sentinel used elsewhere in the object-slot format, e.g. "no parent object", "no active target"), and offset +$12 preset to $7F (a maximum/full value for whichever status field that byte represents).
F2B2 DEFB $FF,$7F,$00,$00,$FF,$FF,$00,$00
F2BA DEFB $00,$00,$00,$00,$00,$00
SKO4Intro F2C0 DEFB $58,$44,$41,$98,$64,$CB,$5A,$A7
F2C8 DEFB $D1
F2C9 DEFB $00 alternation
F2CA: CallFunctionObj
Used by the routines at 716B, 79A3, A706, A77B, DA1D, EBA4, EBC7 and F876.
In: A - bits 3-0 handler function number?
=0 SetRndShip
=1 Set3_4Thargons
=2 SetObjMissile
=3 *not used
=4 ShipSetShip
=5 Set2Alloys
=6 SetCargo
=7 SetShipEscapePod
=8 Set2Minerals
=9 SetShip4Intro
bits 7-4 number =0 - slave
=1 - alloys
=2 - canister
=3 - alien items
=4 - asteroid
=5 - asteroid
=6 - policeman
=7 - cobra
=8 - python
=9 - python
=A - cobra
=B - asp
=C - fer-de-lance
=D - thargon
=E - sidewinder
=F - krait
F2CA LD C,A
F2CB SRL C
F2CD SRL C
F2CF SRL C
F2D1 SRL C
F2D3 AND $0F
F2D5 JP Z,$F61D a = $x0
F2D8 DEC A
F2D9 JP Z,$F3CE 3-0 =1
F2DC DEC A
F2DD JP Z,$F402 launch a missile a = $x2 x =0 launched by an enemy <>0 by the player
F2E0 DEC A
F2E1 RET Z 3-0 =3
F2E2 DEC A
F2E3 JP Z,$F44A a = $x4 x - ship number
F2E6 DEC A
F2E7 JP Z,$F4F7 3-0 =5
F2EA DEC A
F2EB JP Z,$F555 3-0 =6
F2EE DEC A
F2EF JP Z,$F55D 3-0 =7
F2F2 DEC A
F2F3 JP Z,$F5B0 3-0 =8
F2F6 DEC A
F2F7 JP Z,$F5D8 3-0 =9
F2FA RET 3-0 =A..F
F2FB: SetObjInSlot
Used by the routines at F381, F3B3, F3CE, F5D8 and F61D.
Place an object into a slot
In: a - object number
ix address of the object slot
Out: a=$00
Instantiates a live object in the given slot from a ship-type number: (1) looks up the type in AdrDataShips (AdrDataShips) and stores the resulting blueprint address into the slot (+$23/+$24) - the only link between a slot and its static geometry/stats, nothing else about the blueprint is copied in; (2) copies the full 31-byte PatternObject (PatternObject) template into the slot; (3) seeds the instance's current state from the blueprint's maximums: max energy (field_7) becomes current energy (+$22), some flag bits (field_12) are copied into status byte +$21, the launch-capability bits (field_A) are packed together with three bits of the flags byte into status byte +$20, and max speed (field_6), halved, becomes the initial current speed (+$1B); (4) sets status bit 6 of +$20 unconditionally; (5) randomises the starting X/Y position (never Z): the low bytes of X and Y are always re-rolled to a fresh 8-bit random value, while the high bytes are only re-rolled (to a 0-7 range) if status bit 2 of +$21 is clear - so some ship types spawn scattered across a wider area than others, controlled by a flag baked into that ship type's own static data; (6) one further PRNG call has its result bit-rotated into the sign bits of the X and Y position, via two rra/rr pairs sharing a single random byte across both fields.
F2FB LD HL,$6180
F2FE LD B,A
F2FF AND $3F mask to bits 0-5: a ship-type index 0-63, into AdrDataShips's 19 valid entries
F301 ADD A,A
F302 ADD A,A
F303 ADD A,L
F304 LD L,A
F305 JR NC,$F308
F307 INC H
F308 INC HL
F309 LD E,(HL)
F30A INC HL
F30B LD D,(HL)
F30C PUSH DE
F30D POP IY iy address of the object data
F30F LD (IX+$24),D
F312 LD (IX+$23),E
F315 PUSH IX
F317 POP DE
F318 LD BC,$001F
F31B LD HL,$F2A1 +0 template of an object's slot in space
F31E LDIR
F320 LD A,(IY+$07)
F323 LD (IX+$22),A
F326 LD A,(IY+$12)
F329 LD C,A
F32A AND $6F mask blueprint flags (field_12) down to the bits kept in slot status byte +$21
F32C LD (IX+$21),A
F32F LD A,(IY+$0A)
F332 AND $07
F334 RLA
F335 RL C
F337 RRA
F338 LD (IX+$20),A
F33B LD A,(IY+$06)
F33E SRL A
F340 LD (IX+$1B),A
F343 LD A,$7E immediate $7E stored at slot +$25 (not ship-data-derived; unrelated to any doc field)
F345 LD (IX+$25),A
F348 XOR A
F349 LD (IX+$26),A
F34C SET 6,(IX+$20)
F350 BIT 2,(IX+$21)
F354 JR NZ,$F366
F356 CALL $ED24 roll a 0-7 high byte for the X position (only reached if status bit 2 of +$21 is clear)
F359 AND $07
F35B LD (IX+$01),A
F35E CALL $ED24 roll a 0-7 high byte for the Y position
F361 AND $07
F363 LD (IX+$04),A
F366 CALL $ED24 roll the X position's low byte (always re-rolled)
F369 LD (IX+$00),A
F36C CALL $ED24 roll the Y position's low byte (always re-rolled)
F36F LD (IX+$03),A
F372 CALL $ED24 roll one byte whose bits are rotated into the X and Y position sign bits via a shared rra/rr pair
F375 RRA
F376 RR (IX+$02)
F37A RRA
F37B RR (IX+$05)
F37F XOR A
F380 RET
F381: ShipSetObject
Used by the routines at F402, F44A, F592 and F5B8.
Place an object into a slot at another object's coordinates (a ship
launching an enemy object, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
In: a - object number
ix address of the object slot
Out: Z=1 success, -> ix - slot address of the placed object
bc =$0000
F381 LD C,A
F382 PUSH IX
F384 CALL $F399 find a free slot to place an object
F387 POP HL
F388 RET NZ
F389 PUSH HL
F38A LD A,C
F38B CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot
F38E POP HL
F38F PUSH IX
F391 POP DE
F392 LD BC,$001B
F395 LDIR
F397 XOR A
F398 RET
F399: FindSlot4Obj
Used by the routines at F381, F3AD, F3CE, F402 and F61D.
Find a free slot to place an object
In: ---
Out: z=1 free slot found -> ix - its address
de =LenSlotObj
b,a - undefined
F399 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
F39D LD DE,$0027 '''
F3A0 LD B,$06
F3A2 ADD IX,DE
F3A4 LD A,(IX+$22)
F3A7 AND A
F3A8 RET Z
F3A9 DJNZ $F3A2
F3AB INC B
F3AC RET
F3AD: SetPlayerObj
Used by the routine at F4FF.
Place an object in space at the player's coordinates, into a free slot
In: a - object number
Out: z=1 free slot found -> ix - address
F3AD LD C,A
F3AE CALL $F399 find a free slot to place an object
F3B1 RET NZ
F3B2 LD A,C
F3B3: SetPlayerObjSlot
Used by the routines at F402 and F55D.
Place an object into a slot at the player's coordinates
In: a - object number
ix address of the object slot
Out: a=$00
F3B3 CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot
F3B6 RES 7,(IX+$0E)
F3BA RES 7,(IX+$16)
F3BE LD (IX+$00),A
F3C1 LD (IX+$01),A
F3C4 LD (IX+$04),A
F3C7 LD (IX+$06),A
F3CA LD (IX+$07),A
F3CD RET

Used by the routine at F2CA.
Place 3-4 Thargons
In: ---
Out: z=1 success
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F3CE CALL $ED24 Random number
F3D1 AND $01
F3D3 ADD A,$03 3/4 thargon
F3D5 LD C,A
F3D6 CALL $F399 find a free slot to place an object
F3D9 RET NZ
F3DA LD A,$0F thargon
F3DC PUSH BC
F3DD CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot
F3E0 SET 0,(IX+$25)
F3E4 CALL $ED24 Random number
F3E7 AND $0F
F3E9 OR $04
F3EB LD (IX+$07),A
F3EE RRA
F3EF RRA
F3F0 RRA
F3F1 AND $01
F3F3 LD (IX+$01),A
F3F6 XOR A
F3F7 LD (IX+$04),A
F3FA CALL $F4E6 generate a random movement direction for the object
F3FD POP BC
F3FE DEC C
F3FF JR NZ,$F3D6
F401 RET
F402: SetObjMissile
Used by the routine at F2CA.
Launch a missile
In: c - missile target
=0 player (launched by an enemy)
<>0 number of the object the player fired the missile at
Out: Z=1 success
F402 LD A,C
F403 AND A
F404 JR Z,$F432 =1 missile
F406 LD IX,$6048 length 27h
F40A LD DE,$0111 =$27*7
F40D ADD IX,DE ix=objMissile
F40F LD A,(IX+$22)
F412 AND A
F413 JR Z,$F419 =1 - missile
F415 CALL $F399 find a free slot to place an object
F418 RET NZ
F419 LD A,$01 =1 - missile
F41B PUSH BC
F41C CALL $F3B3 place an object into a slot at the player's coordinates
F41F POP BC
F420 LD A,$6A 'j'
F422 LD (IX+$06),A
F425 LD A,$29 ')'
F427 LD (IX+$03),A
F42A LD A,$80
F42C LD (IX+$05),A
F42F LD A,C
F430 JR $F441
F432 LD A,$01 =1 missile
F434 CALL $F381 place an object into a slot at another object's coordinates (a ship launching an enemy object, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
F437 RET NZ
F438 LD A,($7044) get HighDeltaSpeed
F43B ADD A,(IX+$1B)
F43E LD (IX+$1B),A
F441 LD (IX+$25),C
F444 SET 0,(IX+$26)
F448 XOR A
F449 RET
F44A: ShipSetShip
Used by the routine at F2CA.
Launch a ship from an object
In: c - number of the ship being placed
=1 Viper
=2 Cobra/Python
=3 Tharglet
=4 Thargon
=5 Sidewinder/Krait
>5 place nothing
ix - slot address of the parent object
Out: z=1 success
ix - slot address of the placed ship
iy - address of the parent slot
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F44A LD A,$08
F44C DEC C A=8, viper
F44D JR Z,$F470 Set Viper
F44F CALL $ED24 Random number
F452 AND $01
F454 ADD A,$09 a=9/10, cobra/python
F456 DEC C
F457 JR Z,$F470 Set Cobra/Python
F459 LD A,$12 a=18, tharglet
F45B DEC C
F45C JR Z,$F470 Set Tharglet
F45E LD A,$0F a=15, thargon
F460 DEC C
F461 JR Z,$F470 Set Thargon
F463 DEC C
F464 RET NZ >5, ships
F465 CALL $ED24 Random number
F468 BIT 0,A
F46A LD A,$10 a=16, sidewinder
F46C JR Z,$F470 a - object number
F46E LD A,$11 a=17, krait
F470 PUSH IX a - object number
F472 CALL $F381 place an object into a slot at another object's coordinates (a ship launching an enemy object, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
F475 POP IY
F477 RET NZ
F478 BIT 2,(IY+$26)
F47C CALL Z,$F48A rotate the object's OCS
F47F BIT 2,(IY+$21) inherit aggression
F483 RET Z
F484 SET 2,(IX+$21)
F488 XOR A
F489 RET
F48A: RotateSKOobj
Used by the routines at F44A and F5B8.
Rotate the object's OCS
In: ix - address of the object slot
Out: b=$00
c,a - undefined
F48A LD A,($F2C9) alternation
F48D XOR $01
F48F LD ($F2C9),A alternation
F492 JR Z,$F4BB
F494 PUSH IX
F496 CALL $F4A2
F499 CALL $F4A2
F49C CALL $F4A2
F49F POP IX
F4A1 RET

F4A5 LD B,(IX+$10)
F4A8 LD (IX+$10),A
F4AB LD (IX+$0A),B
F4AE LD A,(IX+$16)
F4B1 XOR $80
F4B3 LD (IX+$16),A
F4B6 INC IX
F4B8 INC IX
F4BA RET
F4BB LD A,(IX+$10)
F4BE XOR $80
F4C0 LD B,(IX+$0A)
F4C3 LD (IX+$0A),A
F4C6 LD (IX+$10),B
F4C9 LD A,(IX+$12)
F4CC XOR $80
F4CE LD B,(IX+$0C)
F4D1 LD (IX+$0C),A
F4D4 LD (IX+$12),B
F4D7 LD A,(IX+$14)
F4DA XOR $80
F4DC LD B,(IX+$0E)
F4DF LD (IX+$0E),A
F4E2 LD (IX+$14),B
F4E5 RET

F555: SetCargo

F56F LD A,$80
F571 LD (IX+$05),A
F574 LD A,$1C
F576 LD (IX+$03),A
F579 RES 6,(IX+$20)
F57D LD A,$4C 'L'
F57F LD (IX+$06),A
F582 LD A,$C0
F584 LD (IX+$1E),A
F587 LD A,($7044) get HighDeltaSpeed
F58A ADD A,(IX+$1B)
F58D LD (IX+$1B),A
F590 XOR A
F591 RET
F592: SetShipEPodCargo
Used by the routines at F555 and F55D.
Place EscapePod/Cargo into a slot at another object's coordinates (an enemy
ship launching EscapePod/Cargo, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from
the parent)
In: a - object number
ix address of the object slot
Out: z=1 success
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F592 PUSH IX place an EscapePod at the enemy's coordinates
F594 CALL $F381 place an object into a slot at another object's coordinates (a ship launching an enemy object, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
F597 EX (SP),IX
F599 POP HL
F59A RET NZ
F59B PUSH HL
F59C EX (SP),IX
F59E RES 6,(IX+$20)
F5A2 LD A,$0A
F5A4 LD (IX+$26),A
F5A7 LD A,$80
F5A9 LD (IX+$1D),A
F5AC XOR A
F5AD POP IX
F5AF RET
F5B0: Set2Minerals
Used by the routine at F2CA.
Place two Minerals objects into a slot at another object's coordinates
In: ---
ix address of the parent object's slot
Out: z=1 success
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F5B0 LD A,$05
F5B2 CALL $F5B8 place Minerals/Alloys into a slot at another object's coordinates (debris of an asteroid/ship inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
F5B5 RET NZ
F5B6 LD A,$05
F5B8: SetShipMinAlloys
Used by the routines at F54B and F5B0.
Place Minerals/Alloys into a slot at another object's coordinates (debris
of an asteroid/ship inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
In: a - number of the object being placed
ix address of the parent object's slot
Out: z=1 success
hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F5B8 PUSH IX
F5BA CALL $F381 place an object into a slot at another object's coordinates (a ship launching an enemy object, which inherits coordinates, OCS and speed from the parent)
F5BD EX (SP),IX
F5BF POP HL
F5C0 RET NZ
F5C1 PUSH HL
F5C2 EX (SP),IX
F5C4 CALL $F48A rotate the object's OCS
F5C7 LD A,$0A
F5C9 LD (IX+$26),A
F5CC RES 6,(IX+$20)
F5D0 SET 7,(IX+$1D)
F5D4 POP IX
F5D6 XOR A
F5D7 RET
F5D8: SetShip4Intro
Used by the routine at F2CA.
Place an object into the missile slot for the intro screen
In: c - object number
Out: ix,hl,de,bc,a - undefined
Always targets the one fixed slot otherwise used for the player's own missile (objMissile), reused here since it's never needed for both purposes at once. Calls F2FB (SetObjInSlot) exactly as any other spawn - so the PRNG-driven X/Y jitter it performs does happen - then immediately overwrites the position anyway: X and Y are explicitly zeroed and Z is set to a fixed $0200, undoing the randomisation a few instructions later and putting the intro ship at a fixed, repeatable distance directly ahead. Also clears status bit 6 of +$20 that SetObjInSlot had just set, then copies the fixed 9-byte orientation seed SKO4Intro (SKO4Intro) into the slot's OCS fields, giving the title-screen ship a consistent, non-randomised starting attitude every boot.
F5D8 LD IX,$6159 slot for the missile +00
F5DC LD A,C
F5DD ADD A,$02
F5DF CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot
F5E2 LD A,$C0
F5E4 LD (IX+$1D),A
F5E7 LD (IX+$1E),A
F5EA XOR A
F5EB LD (IX+$06),A
F5EE LD (IX+$00),A crd X =0
F5F1 LD (IX+$01),A
F5F4 LD (IX+$03),A crd y =0
F5F7 LD (IX+$04),A
F5FA LD (IX+$08),A crd z = 003200
F5FD LD (IX+$1B),A
F600 RES 6,(IX+$20)
F604 INC A
F605 INC A
F606 LD (IX+$07),A crd z = 000200
F609 PUSH IX
F60B POP HL
F60C LD DE,$0009
F60F LD B,D
F610 LD C,E
F611 ADD HL,DE
F612 EX DE,HL
F613 LD HL,$F2C0 object's OCS for the intro
F616 INC DE
F617 LDI
F619 JP PE,$F616
F61C RET
F61D: SetRndShip
Used by the routine at F2CA.
Place a random ship/group of ships
In: ---
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F61D LD A,($A830) get cargoSlaves
F620 LD C,A number of slaves
F621 LD A,($A833) get cargoNarc
F624 ADD A,C
F625 ADD A,A
F626 LD C,A c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2
F627 LD A,($A837) +0A Firearms
F62A ADD A,C
F62B LD C,A c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2 + amount of weapons
F62C LD A,($DA0C) get PilotMission2
F62F AND A
F630 JR NZ,$F660 c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2 + amount of weapons
F632 CALL $ED24 Random number
F635 CP $FF
F637 JR NC,$F660 c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2 + amount of weapons
F639 LD A,($A872) +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed
F63C AND $42
F63E CP $02
F640 JR NZ,$F660 c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2 + amount of weapons
F642 DEC A
F643 LD ($DA0C),A set PilotMission2
F646 LD A,$0F
F648 LD HL,$F655
F64B PUSH HL
F64C PUSH AF

F655 SET 6,(IX+$26)
F659 SET 2,(IX+$20)
F65D JP $F70E place 2-4 Sidewinder/Krait
F660 CALL $ED24 c = (number of slaves + amount of narcotics) * 2 + amount of weapons
F663 SUB C
F664 JR C,$F684 rnd < banned cargo

F668 RET NC rnd >= banned cargo rnd >=$5A
F669 CP $50 'P'
F66B JR C,$F684 rnd >= banned cargo rnd < $50
F66D CALL $F399 rnd >= banned cargo rnd >= $50 rnd < $5A rnd=$50..$59
F670 RET NZ
F671 LD A,$06 set asteroid
F673 CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot
F676 SET 7,(IX+$0E)
F67A RES 6,(IX+$20)
F67E LD A,$C0
F680 LD (IX+$1D),A
F683 RET

F685 AND $07
F687 LD E,A
F688 LD A,($A871) get PilotMission3
F68B CP $04
F68D LD D,A
F68E LD C,$0F
F690 JR Z,$F6DB set thargon, if the ship is captured by pirates
F692 LD A,D
F693 CP $01
F695 LD C,$0A
F697 JR Z,$F6DB set python, if it's time to give mission 3
F699 LD A,($A85F) 74 ???
F69C AND $07
F69E JR Z,$F6A3 if anarchy
F6A0 INC E
F6A1 CP E
F6A2 RET NC exit if rnd >= the planet's government
F6A3 LD C,A
F6A4 BIT 3,B
F6A6 JP Z,$F6E2
F6A9 CALL $F399 place a random ship
F6AC RET NZ
F6AD CALL $ED24 Random number
F6B0 AND $3F '?'
F6B2 LD B,$0F thargon
F6B4 JR Z,$F6C6

F6BE JR C,$F6C6

F6C1 SUB $10
F6C3 JR C,$F6C6

F6C6 LD A,B
F6C7 PUSH AF
F6C8 CALL $F2FB place an object into a slot

F6CC CP $0F

F6D0 CALL $ED24 probability of aggression?
F6D3 CP $33 '3'

F6D6 SET 0,(IX+$25)
F6DA RET
F6DB CALL $F399 find a free slot to place an object
F6DE RET NZ
F6DF LD A,C
F6E0 JR $F6C7
F6E2 CALL $ED24 Random number
F6E5 LD B,A
F6E6 AND $07
F6E8 CP E

F72B: Data block at F72B
deltaCrdX F72B DEFW $0000 distance along X / 4 7,=x sign
deltaCrdY F72D DEFW $0000 distance along Y / 4 7,=x sign
deltaCrdZ F72F DEFW $0000 distance along Z / 4 7,=z sign
LenVector F731 DEFW $0000 vector length
F733: ReactionObject
Used by the routine at DA1D.
Object's reaction to the player. Launching missiles, ships, escape capsule, etc.
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object data
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F733 LD A,(IY+$07) max energy of the object
F736 LD C,(IX+$22) current energy
F739 INC C
F73A CP $F0
F73C JR C,$F741 max energy >=$F0 current energy +4 max energy <$F0 current energy +1
F73E INC C
F73F INC C
F740 INC C
F741 CP C
F742 JR C,$F745 current energy > max energy
F744 LD A,C a=c(current energy) if current <= max energy
F745 LD B,A b - the object's changed energy
F746 LD A,($A80C) get CloakingDeviceOn
F749 AND A
F74A RET NZ
F74B LD HL,$DA0C =3 the Asp from mission 2 is destroyed / there is a canister with the cloak in space =2 the Asp's invisibility is off =1 mission 2 given =0 while moving on the J-drive
F74E LD A,B
F74F CP $50 'P'
F751 LD A,(HL)
F752 JR C,$F759 energy <$50
F754 CP $02
F756 JR NZ,$F759
F758 DEC (HL)
F759 CP $01
F75B JR NZ,$F76A
F75D LD A,B
F75E CP $34 '4'
F760 JR NC,$F767 energy >=$34
F762 LD A,$02

F765 JR $F76A
F767 SUB $02
F769 LD B,A
F76A LD A,B
F76B LD (IX+$22),A new energy
F76E LD B,(IY+$07)
F771 ADD A,A
F772 JR C,$F7DF energy is high
F774 CP B
F775 JR NC,$F7DF energy is more than half
F777 ADD A,A
F778 JR C,$F780
F77A ADD A,A
F77B JR C,$F780
F77D CP B
F77E JR C,$F7B6 critically low energy
F780 LD A,(IX+$20)
F783 AND $07
F785 JR Z,$F7DF no missiles and no ships
F787 CALL $ED24 determine the launch probability
F78A CP $80
F78C JR NC,$F7DF
F78E LD A,($DA16) get TimeWorkECM
F791 AND A
F792 JR NZ,$F7DF while ECM is running, the enemy cannot launch anything
F794 LD A,(IY+$0A)
F797 AND $F0
F799 JR NZ,$F79D
F79B OR $06
F79D XOR $04
F79F LD B,A
F7A0 PUSH BC
F7A1 CALL $F876 the enemy launches a missile or a ship
F7A4 POP BC
F7A5 JR NZ,$F7DF
F7A7 DEC (IX+$20)
F7AA LD A,B
F7AB CP $02 =2 launched a missile
F7AD JR NZ,$F7DF
F7AF LD HL,$A881 7,=1 the enemy fired a missile
F7B2 SET 7,(HL)
F7B4 JR $F7DF
F7B6 CALL $ED24 critically low energy
F7B9 CP (IX+$25) probability of dropping cargo/pod
F7BC JR C,$F815 not aggressive by default???
F7BE BIT 7,(IX+$20)
F7C2 JR Z,$F7D4 drop the cargo hold
F7C4 LD A,$07 set the object's escape capsule
F7C6 CALL $F876 Call a function
F7C9 JR NZ,$F7DF if the escape capsule isn't set
F7CB RES 7,(IX+$20)
F7CF RES 6,(IX+$20)
F7D3 RET
F7D4 LD A,(IY+$00) drop the cargo hold
F7D7 AND A
F7D8 JR Z,$F7DF
F7DA LD A,$06 set cargo
F7DC CALL $F876 Call a function
F7DF LD A,(IY+$12)
F7E2 CP $04
F7E4 JR NZ,$F815 not aggressive by default???

F7EF LD B,$06

F7F9 LD A,(IX+$21)

F7FE JR Z,$F803
F800 DJNZ $F7F1

F803 POP IX

F808 RES 6,(IX+$20)
F80C SET 1,(IX+$26)
F810 SRL (IX+$1B) decrease speed
F814 RET
F815 BIT 0,(IX+$25) not aggressive by default???

F81B LD A,($A871) get PilotMission3

F820 JR NZ,$F829
F822 LD A,($A872) +93 extra equipment present 7,=1 - ECM Jammer 6,=1 - Cloaking device 5,=1 - cannot buy goods (refugees in the hold) 4 3 =1 mission 3 was completed 2 =1 - mission 3 completed 1 =1 - mission 2 completed 0 =1 - mission 1 completed

F882: TestObjInRadius
Used by the routine at F8A2.
Check whether the object is within a given radius of the player

Out: cy =1 within radius
=0 outside
F882 LD H,(IX+$01)
F885 LD L,(IX+$00)
F888 AND A
F889 SBC HL,DE
F88B RET NC

F88F LD L,(IX+$03)
F892 AND A
F893 SBC HL,DE
F895 RET NC
F896 LD H,(IX+$07)
F899 LD L,(IX+$06)
F89C INC D
F89D INC D
F89E AND A
F89F SBC HL,DE
F8A1 RET

Used by the routine at F733.

F928: MoveMissile

In: ix - address of the missile slot

iy - address of the object data
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F928 LD A,($DA16) get TimeWorkECM
F92B AND A
F92C JR Z,$F937 destroy the target with the missile
F92E RES 6,(IX+$20) if ECM is running, destroy the missile
F932 SET 4,(IX+$20)
F936 RET
F937 LD A,(IX+$25) destroy the target with the missile
F93A AND A
F93B JR Z,$F90B handle the missile hitting the player
F93D PUSH IY
F93F LD IY,$6021 length 27h
F943 LD DE,$0027 '''
F946 SUB $0A
F948 CPL
F949 LD B,A convert the reverse index
F94A ADD IY,DE
F94C DJNZ $F94A
F94E PUSH IX
F950 PUSH IY ix address of the missile slot iy address of the missile's target slot

F966 POP IX

F973 JR NZ,$F9A5 far

F978 CALL $F9AA check distance hl < 003F
F97B JR NZ,$F9A5 far
F97D BIT 2,(IY+$26)
F981 JR Z,$F99A is destroyed by the missile
F983 LD HL,$A871 +92 >0 - mission 3 given =1 mission 3 will be given on docking ( =1 time to give the mission, will become =2 after the jump) =2 mission 3 will be given on docking =3 mission 3 given (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =4 mission 3 given, the ship is in the target system (the station can be destroyed with a missile) (Thargons launch from the station) =5 mission 3 completed, there will be congratulations on docking
F986 LD A,(HL)
F987 CP $04
F989 JR NZ,$F99E destroy the missile
F98B SET 3,(HL)
F98D DEC L
F98E XOR A
F98F LD (HL),A
F990 LD DE,$FFFF
F993 CALL $A8EE increase combat rating
F996 SET 7,(IY+$26)

F99E POP IY destroy the missile
F9A0 SET 4,(IX+$20)
F9A4 RET
F9A5 POP IY
F9A7 JP $F925

F9AB LD A,H
F9AC AND A
F9AD RET NZ
F9AE LD A,L
F9AF AND $C0

F9BB LD E,(IX+$00)

F9C1 LD B,(IX+$02)

F9C6 RR H
F9C8 RR L
F9CA SLA B
F9CC RR D
F9CE RR E

F9D3 LD DE,$0003
F9D6 ADD IX,DE
F9D8 ADD IY,DE
F9DA RET
F9DB: HL_CrdNdiv4_InvSign
Used by the routine at F8A2.
Convert a coordinate, flipping its sign
In: ix - address of the coordinate
Out: hl = -1 * CrdN / 4
ix=ix+3
F9DB LD A,(IX+$02)
F9DE XOR $80
F9E0 JR $F9E5
F9E2: HL_CrdNdiv4
Used by the routine at F8A2.
Convert a coordinate without changing its sign
In: ix - address of the coordinate
Out: hl = CrdN / 4
ix=ix+3
F9E2 LD A,(IX+$02)
This entry point is used by the routine at F9DB.
F9E5 LD L,(IX+$00)
F9E8 LD H,(IX+$01)
F9EB SRL H
F9ED RR L
F9EF RLA
F9F0 RR H
F9F2 RR L
F9F4 LD DE,$0003
F9F7 ADD IX,DE
F9F9 RET
F9FA: MoveObject
Used by the routine at F8A2.
Object movement. Set rotation angles, acceleration, speed, and fire the
laser
In: ix - address of the object slot
iy - address of the object data
(deltaCrdX) - the vector relative to which we compute
Out: hl,de,bc,a - undefined
F9FA PUSH IY
F9FC LD IY,$F72B distance along X / 4 7,=x sign
FA00 PUSH IX
FA02 LD IX,$F72B distance along X / 4 7,=x sign
FA06 CALL $DBD5 compute vector length and normalize the vector
FA09 POP IX
FA0B LD ($F731),DE set LenVector
FA0F PUSH IX
FA11 LD DE,$0009
FA14 ADD IX,DE ix+$09
FA16 CALL $FB1F dot product of two vectors with one vector's direction flipped
FA19 LD DE,$0006
FA1C ADD IX,DE ix+$0F
FA1E LD H,A
FA1F PUSH HL
FA20 CALL $FB1F dot product of two vectors with one vector's direction flipped
FA23 POP HL
FA24 LD L,A
FA25 PUSH HL
FA26 LD DE,$0006
FA29 ADD IX,DE ix+$15
FA2B CALL $FB1F dot product of two vectors with one vector's direction flipped
FA2E POP HL
FA2F POP IX
FA31 POP IY
FA33 LD D,A
FA34 AND $7F
FA36 JR Z,$FA5A
FA38 LD C,A
FA39 SRL C
FA3B SRL C
FA3D SRL C
FA3F ADC A,C
FA40 SRL A
FA42 SRL A
FA44 CP $0F
FA46 JR C,$FA4A

FA4A LD B,A

FADC LD HL,$707D which sound to play =$00 no sound (silent) 0,=1 energy bomb exploded 1,=1 missile launched 2,=1 colliding with an object/object destroyed 3,=1 laser fired 4,=1 laser fired 5,=1 ECM working 6,=1 hit by laser on the player/docking damage 7,=1

FB10: Mult_HL_A2_B2
Used by the routine at FB1F.
Multiplication hl = -1 * high((a*2) * (b*2))
In: b - first factor, 7,b - sign
a - second factor, 7,a - sign
Out: l,a = high((a*2) * (b*2))
l,a = high((a*2) * (b*2))
7,h - sign
FB10 LD C,A
FB11 XOR B
FB12 XOR $80
FB14 SLA B
FB16 SLA C
FB18 PUSH AF
FB19 CALL $E434 A=((b+c)/2)^2-((b-c)/2)^2
FB1C POP HL
FB1D LD L,A
FB1E RET
FB1F: ScalarMultVectors
Used by the routines at E791 and F9FA.
Dot product of two vectors with one vector's direction flipped
In: ix - start address of the OCS axis vector
iy - start address of the second vector's coordinates (normalized vector)
Out: a - result
7,a - new sign
FB1F LD B,(IX+$01)
FB22 LD A,(IY+$01)
FB25 CALL $FB10 (crdXv/2) * (cos(a)/2)
FB28 PUSH HL
FB29 LD B,(IX+$03)
FB2C LD A,(IY+$03)
FB2F CALL $FB10 multiplication hl = -1 * high((a*2) * (b*2))
FB32 PUSH HL
FB33 LD B,(IX+$05)
FB36 LD A,(IY+$05)
FB39 CALL $FB10 multiplication hl = -1 * high((a*2) * (b*2))
FB3C POP DE
FB3D CALL $E424 signed addition
FB40 JR NC,$FB44
FB42 LD A,$FF
FB44 POP DE
FB45 LD L,A
FB46 CALL $E424 signed addition
FB49 JR NC,$FB4D
FB4B LD A,$FF
FB4D RL H
FB4F RRA
FB50 RET

FC03 DEFB $B2,$42,$3A,$52,$52,$54,$92,$70 attribute +2 line feeds
FC0B DEFB $DA,$F1,$F0 attribute +2 line feeds
ControlKey1 FC0E DEFB $00 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device 6,=1 - 0 -- ??? 5,=1 - I -- Global Map 4,=1 - O -- Local Map 3,=1 - P -- Planet Info 2,=1 - K -- Planet Price 1,=1 - L -- Commander Info 0,=1 - Enter -- Inventory
ControlKeyMove FC0F DEFB $00 7,=1 - -- unused 6,=1 - -- unused 5,=1 - SymbolS -- Decrease Speed 4,=1 - Space -- Increase Speed 3,=1 - S -- Up 2,=1 - X -- Down 1,=1 - M -- Right 0,=1 - N -- Left
ControlKey2 FC10 DEFB $00 7,=1 - CapsS -- Pause 6,=1 - J -- Jump Drive 5,=1 - G -- Galactic Hyper Drive 4,=1 - H -- Hyper Drive 3,=1 - 4 -- Right View/Equip Ship 2,=1 - 3 -- Left View/Sell Cargo 1,=1 - 2 -- Rear View/Buy Cargo 0,=1 - 1 -- Front View/Launch
ControlKey3 FC11 DEFB $00 7,=1 - C -- Docking Computer 6,=1 - Q -- Escape Capsule 5,=1 - W -- Energy Bomb 4,=1 - E -- ECM System 3,=1 - U -- UnTarget 2,=1 - F -- Fire Missile 1,=1 - T -- Target Missile 0,=1 - A -- Fire Laser
ControlKeyInMap FC12 DEFB $00 7, -- unused 6,=1 - R -- Find Planet 5,=1 - D -- Cursor to Near Planet 4,=1 - B -- Cursor to Present System 3-0, -- unused
PressKeyPrnSymbol FC13 DEFB $FF printable character of the pressed key =$FE - BackSpace =$FF - nothing pressed
FlagsSetUp FC14 DEFB $8C 0-1,=00 SXNMA controls (LLFC0F)<>0 reset CS,F,c =01 Kempston controls =10 cursor controls 76850 =11 Sinclair right controls 2,=1 realistic control approximation (reaction) 3,=1 turn inertia on 4,=1 controls inverted 5,=1 up/down inverted 6,=1 Thargon interception on 7,=1 sound on
FC15 DEFB $00 something related to cursor and Sinclair joystick, unused in this version

FCC0: SetBitControlKey
Used by the routine at FB79.
Set the pressed key from the table
In: d - half-row number
e - reverse key index within the half-row
Out: (PressKeyPrnSymbol) - character of the pressed key
(ControlKeys) - the corresponding bit is set
FCC0 PUSH BC
FCC1 PUSH AF
FCC2 LD A,$05
FCC4 SUB E
FCC5 ADD A,A
FCC6 ADD A,A
FCC7 ADD A,A
FCC8 ADD A,D
FCC9 DEC A
FCCA LD B,A
FCCB SLA B
FCCD LD HL,$FB51 key character table
FCD0 ADD A,L
FCD1 JR NC,$FCD4
FCD3 INC H
FCD4 LD L,A
FCD5 LD A,(HL)
FCD6 LD ($FC13),A set PressKeyPrnSymbol
FCD9 LD HL,$FF58 table mapping key codes to bits +0 byte number in the control-key list +1 bit of the pressed key
FCDC LD A,L
FCDD ADD A,B
FCDE LD L,A
FCDF LD A,(HL)
FCE0 INC HL
FCE1 LD B,(HL)
FCE2 LD HL,$FC0E 7,=1 - Y -- Cloaking Device 6,=1 - 0 -- ??? 5,=1 - I -- Global Map 4,=1 - O -- Local Map 3,=1 - P -- Planet Info 2,=1 - K -- Planet Price 1,=1 - L -- Commander Info 0,=1 - Enter -- Inventory
FCE5 ADD A,L
FCE6 LD L,A
FCE7 LD A,(HL)
FCE8 OR B
FCE9 LD (HL),A
FCEA POP AF
FCEB POP BC
FCEC RET
